Strength.description: Strength determines whether you can lift a mortal off the ground, drop them with a single blow, and generally how powerful your dead muscles are. The approximate weight you can lift at a given rating without a roll is noted in parentheses.
Strength.dot1: You can crush a beer can without trouble (20 kg: a Christmas tree, a road sign).
Strength.dot2: You have average physical attributes (45 kg: a toilet bowl).
Strength.dot3: You could probably kick in a wooden door (115 kg: a large man, an empty coffin, a refrigerator).
Strength.dot4: Your physical conditioning is close to ideal. You probably have well-defined muscles (180 kg: a coffin with a body inside, an empty dumpster).
Strength.dot5: You're a real bulldozer. With enough effort you can kick in an iron door, tear through chain-link fencing with your bare hands, or knock down a chained gate (250 kg: a motorcycle, a piano).

Dexterity.description: Dexterity determines the speed of your reactions, your grace, and the precision of your movements, as well as how fast you can move. Can you dodge when someone aims a stake at your heart? Will you finish what you started before time runs out?
Dexterity.dot1: You can run well enough, but you have trouble with balance, and dodging doesn't come easily.
Dexterity.dot2: You're good at short-distance running; you sometimes even look graceful.
Dexterity.dot3: You can develop impressive speed, and your coordination is no worse than any other sports enthusiast's.
Dexterity.dot4: You're a true acrobat, capable of stunts few mortals could ever pull off.
Dexterity.dot5: You possess a striking, almost supernatural grace, and move as smoothly as a snake mesmerizing its prey.

Stamina.description: Your body's ability to withstand strain and the effects of a dangerous environment. This is your tirelessness and toughness. Stamina lets you stay combat-capable longer when you take physical damage, and remain standing even after taking a bullet or a hunter's knife. Your Health track rating equals your Stamina + 3.
Stamina.dot1: You get winded even after light exertion.
Stamina.dot2: You'll survive a fight, sure, but you'd better avoid one.
Stamina.dot3: A few days of hiking cross-country with a pack on your back? No problem!
Stamina.dot4: You're quite capable of finishing first in a marathon; physical pain doesn't faze you.
Stamina.dot5: You would never break a sweat, even if your dead body were still capable of it.

Charisma.description: Charisma is natural charm, grace, and sexual attractiveness. People are drawn to those who possess such qualities, which makes hunting easier. Charisma is independent of beauty (beauty is covered by the corresponding Looks advantage).
Charisma.dot1: You know how to talk, but few people listen to you.
Charisma.dot2: You're pleasant company, even though you're undead. You might even have friends.
Charisma.dot3: People trust you unconditionally. You make friends without any effort.
Charisma.dot4: You're blessed with enormous charisma. You have many admirers.
Charisma.dot5: You can charm almost anyone who speaks to you.

Manipulation.description: Manipulation is the ability to win others over to your point of view, to lie and bluff convincingly, and in general to mess with people's heads so that no one suspects a thing.
Manipulation.dot1: If you genuinely believe you're in the right, you can convince others of it too.
Manipulation.dot2: You can easily fool a weak-willed simpleton.
Manipulation.dot3: You never have to pay full price for anything.
Manipulation.dot4: You could be the leader of a cult... or a politician.
Manipulation.dot5: You can convince a Prince to invest in a completely pointless venture, or even act against their own interests.

Composure.description: Composure is the inner calm that helps you control your own emotions and puts others at ease even in stressful situations. It's also the ability to stay cool no matter the circumstances — in a firefight or in bed. Your Willpower track rating equals Composure + Resolve.
Composure.dot1: You can fly into a frenzy over the smallest provocation.
Composure.dot2: Most of the time, as long as nothing threatens you, you can keep your predatory nature under control.
Composure.dot3: People often come to you for advice on what to do when the Blood goes to their head.
Composure.dot4: Bluffing at cards is no trouble for you at all. To some extent, you even keep your inner Beast on a leash.
Composure.dot5: The Beast is on a short leash.

Intelligence.description: Intelligence is the ability to reason, think logically, and learn. Someone with high Intelligence can effortlessly recall information learned from books or experience and analyze it thoroughly. They can solve any puzzle and unravel any mystery.
Intelligence.dot1: You can read and write, but there are complex concepts that leave you baffled.
Intelligence.dot2: You're smart enough to recognize the limits of your own intellect.
Intelligence.dot3: You're an intellectual who has no trouble putting the pieces of a puzzle together.
Intelligence.dot4: Your wisdom is so great that clan Tremere might consult you.
Intelligence.dot5: Even the word 'genius' fails to capture the full depth of your intellect and breadth of knowledge.

Wits.description: Wits is the ability to think fast, react flawlessly to a sudden change in the situation, and show resourcefulness. 'You heard a sound' is Wits; 'you hear two guards approaching' is Intelligence. Wits lets you sense an ambush a mile away and never lose your footing in unexpected situations.
Wits.dot1: Some things need to be explained to you at length.
Wits.dot2: You know how to bet at poker and hit the brakes in time. Usually.
Wits.dot3: You can instantly size up a situation and make the best possible decision.
Wits.dot4: You can't be caught off guard, and you're never at a loss for words.
Wits.dot5: Few can keep up with your thinking. You react to danger faster than others even notice it.

Resolve.description: Resolve is the ability to focus on what matters and not back down. It determines how well you can concentrate and how easily you're thrown off balance. Resolve is what keeps you alert and stops you from letting yourself get distracted. Your Willpower track rating equals Composure + Resolve.
Resolve.dot1: Your attention span only stretches to the most pressing matters.
Resolve.dot2: You're mentally prepared for travel, as long as it isn't too long.
Resolve.dot3: Distracting you from your task takes so much effort that most people consider it a waste of time.
Resolve.dot4: Nothing can knock you off your path when you're searching for answers.
Resolve.dot5: You can think about something else entirely during a firefight, or keep watch on the door during a blood orgy, then pick up every shell casing and wipe away every last drop of spilled blood afterward.

Athletics.description:
Athletics comes into play when you want to catch up with someone, or conversely flee, jump out of the way of a speeding car, climb somewhere, or swim like a strong, healthy living person. Athletics can be used to resolve a physical conflict in place of any combat skill, but in that case the character can't inflict damage on the opponent no matter how many successes they roll.

Specialties:
acrobatics, archery, climbing, conditioning, jumping, parkour, swimming, throwing.
Athletics.dot1: You never skipped gym class. You still have a bouncy, energetic stride.
Athletics.dot2: Your body is technically dead, but it looks no worse than that of any mortal who exercises regularly.
Athletics.dot3: You're in perfect shape. You could even go pro, if more competitions were held at night.
Athletics.dot4: With your parkour skills, you'll probably never need to turn into a bat.
Athletics.dot5: You'd break any Olympic record. Few mortals can compete with you. Vampires assume you must have physical Disciplines.

Drive.description:
Anyone can drive a car (except maybe a 500-year-old vampire). The Drive skill is needed when you have to drive at high speed in difficult conditions or under stress, such as fleeing the Second Inquisition. This covers off-road driving, chases, street racing, and the like.

Specialties:
chases, off-road vehicles, motorcycles, stunts, surveillance, trucks, street racing, vintage cars.
Drive.dot1: You're an attentive driver who does everything right.
Drive.dot2: If visibility is good, you can floor it without much risk of crashing.
Drive.dot3: You've out-raced rivals before. Anarchs think you're cool.
Drive.dot4: You could work as a stunt driver, or even as a Prince's or Baron's personal chauffeur.
Drive.dot5: You know literally everything about cars. Few can match your skill.

Larceny.description:
The Larceny skill means the character knows how to pick a lock, plant a wiretap, disable an alarm in a house or car, forge a document, hot-wire a car, and probably crack a safe too. Breaking in somewhere without permission is no trouble either. This skill can be used to protect your own property and territory, and also to figure out how an intruder got in after the fact. Modern security systems rely on electronics, DVRs, and computer control, so on top of Larceny you might also need Technology.

Specialties:
breaking and entering, lock picking, pickpocketing, safecracking, security system analysis, alarm systems, car theft, forgery.
Larceny.dot1: You can pick a simple lock or pick the pocket of a distracted person.
Larceny.dot2: You know how to steal cars and shoplift.
Larceny.dot3: You can anticipate where security cameras are placed and plan a route around them. You know how to disable an alarm.
Larceny.dot4: You can crack a keypad lock, reprogram an ID card, or break into a safe.
Larceny.dot5: You can break into an international bank vault — and get back out.

Survival.description:
Survival means the ability to make it through extreme circumstances, particularly out in the wilderness. It's also the ability to find your way back to civilization. Navigating by the stars, finding shelter from daylight, noticing signs of werewolves in time — all of that falls under Survival. In some cases it can also be used in parks, vacant lots, and abandoned parts of the urban jungle.

Specialties:
tracking, urban wastelands, jungle, forest, traps, hunting, desert, shelter-building.
Survival.dot1: You know your way around the wilderness surrounding your domain.
Survival.dot2: You spend most of your time outdoors. You can track down anyone who hasn't specifically trained to cover their tracks.
Survival.dot3: You can survive outside the city, building yourself a shelter and setting traps for people.
Survival.dot4: Predator is your true nature. Outside the city, you feel right at home.
Survival.dot5: The Gangrel would gladly welcome you into their pack, but they'd have a hard time keeping up with you.

Brawl.description:
Brawl is the ability to deal damage with your fists, claws, and improvised objects like your own boot. If you have no weapon at all in a fight, you're using Brawl. This covers everything from elegant aikijutsu to dirty bar-room brawling.

Specialties:
unarmed mortals, armed mortals, ring fighting, animals, grappling, bar brawls, werewolves, fighting under Protean's effects, Kindred.
Brawl.dot1: You had a rough childhood: you had to fight for your place in the sun. Some of those skills have stuck with you.
Brawl.dot2: You trained for a while to learn how to throw a punch.
Brawl.dot3: You can hold your own in any fight.
Brawl.dot4: Either you trained with special forces, or you've spent decades of your immortal existence fighting.
Brawl.dot5: You could become a champion in no-rules fighting, without even using the Blood's advantages.

Craft.description:
This skill covers creativity in general: making things, from masterpieces of art to purely utilitarian objects, providing services, and so on. Craft covers both shaping a clay pot and a deep understanding of modern architecture that lets you design the perfect haven. When you take this skill, you get one specialty for free. Unlike most other skills, the number of specialties in Craft isn't limited by the skill rating.

Specialties:
designer, weaponsmith, carpenter, tailor, carver, sculptor, painter.
Craft.dot1: You're an amateur, though a fairly skilled one.
Craft.dot3: Your creations can be beautiful or terrifying. Whatever you set out to make, you can pull it off.
Craft.dot4: Your skill is recognized by everyone — mortals and Kindred alike.
Craft.dot5: You're often invited to help organize parties at Elysium.

Stealth.description:
Stealth lets you tail a target unnoticed — an extremely useful skill for a predator. A vampire who knows how to spy, sneak, and blend into a crowd has a huge advantage.

Specialties:
urban environments, wilderness, ambushes, disguise, tailing, silent movement, crowds, hiding spots.
Stealth.dot1: You're hard to spot in the dark or under a disguise.
Stealth.dot2: You can sneak past an ordinary person and successfully tail a target who has no idea they're being followed.
Stealth.dot3: You move very quietly and always know where to hide. Patrolling guards are no problem for you.
Stealth.dot4: You're elusive, moving silently like a cat. You'd make an excellent ninja — or a worthy opponent for one.
Stealth.dot5: The Children of Haqim come to you for advice and guidance. If they can find you, that is.

Firearms.description:
Cainites use firearms not just because they're flashy and effective, but also because they don't draw unwanted suspicion. The Firearms skill covers handling a wide range of guns, from concealable pistols to assault rifles. It also includes other trigger-mechanism weapons, such as crossbows or hand-held grenade launchers. A character with Firearms also knows how to maintain, repair, and quickly reload a weapon.

Specialties:
crossbows, fast draw, handloading ammunition, sniping, weapons dealing, trick shooting, weapon maintenance.
Firearms.dot1: You've fired a gun a couple of times — at a range, or somewhere less formal.
Firearms.dot2: You know how to maintain a weapon, can do it in practice, and can field-strip and reassemble one.
Firearms.dot3: You've been to hot zones ("seen the world," as older Kindred put it) and come back alive.
Firearms.dot4: You can shoot a squirrel in the eye from a hundred paces, fire from a kneeling stance, from the hip, in rapid bursts — whatever. Anything even remotely related to shooting, you can do.
Firearms.dot5: You've been training with firearms since Winchester made his first rifle.

Melee.description:
Melee covers a wide range of close-combat weapons — among other things, it includes knives, chains, and baseball bats. A hunter trying to drive a stake through your heart is also using Melee.

Specialties:
clubs, improvised weapons, stakes, swords, knives, disarming techniques, rapiers, axes, garrotes, chains.
Melee.dot1: You know how to handle a knife or a bat. You usually even manage not to hit your own people.
Melee.dot2: Your skill with a weapon sometimes makes an opponent think twice about whether tangling with you is worth it.
Melee.dot3: You're known throughout the domain as a skilled fighter.
Melee.dot4: Only a fool comes at you with a gun.
Melee.dot5: You're a master of the blade in your domain. Kindred from every corner of the world come to take a lesson or two from you.

Intimidation.description:
Intimidation is the skill of using threats to get something out of a victim, browbeating an opponent in a social conflict, and keeping everyone around you in line. Vampires who rely on this skill have no qualms about breaking someone's will — and sometimes their fingers too.

Specialties:
extortion, interrogation, veiled threats, insults, the hard stare, physical coercion.
Intimidation.dot1: You can come up with a cutting insult that hits exactly where it hurts.
Intimidation.dot2: You can push people around without much trouble.
Intimidation.dot3: Your cruelty and self-satisfaction are well known.
Intimidation.dot4: You left mundane threats of physical violence behind long ago.
Intimidation.dot5: Even Kindred are sometimes afraid to cross you.

Performance.description:
Performance covers a wide range of arts: dance, poetry, comedy, storytelling, and much more. You might be a genuinely brilliant performer, or simply an enthusiastic student. When you take this skill, you get one specialty for free.

Specialties:
guitar, drama, wind instruments, keyboards, singing, poetry, public speaking, rap, violin, stand-up, dance, drums.
Performance.dot1: Your performances would liven up any party, but it's not yet time for you to take the stage.
Performance.dot2: Your performances get a mixed reaction — some people like them, others think you're terrible.
Performance.dot3: You're far from a novice in your art, but you still have a lot to learn.
Performance.dot4: Your performances are so magnificent that even Toreadors are thoroughly delighted.
Performance.dot5: Improvising is no problem for you: every night you perform for a new audience, with a new show every time.

Leadership.description:
Leadership is the ability to lead a crowd, command a unit, inspire allies, and put down rebellions. Even the strongest Prince or Baron risks losing their position if they don't know how to lead.

Specialties:
combat unit, inspiring others, orders, oratory, praxis, teamwork.
Leadership.dot1: You have experience running informal clubs.
Leadership.dot2: You can rally a group of like-minded Kindred under your banner.
Leadership.dot3: The Council is willing to hear you out. Sometimes they even listen to your opinion.
Leadership.dot4: You know how to command in battle; soldiers are willing to follow you to certain death.
Leadership.dot5: As long as you're nearby, even the wounded and dying are ready to act. Your speeches make Kindred hearts beat as if they were still alive.

Animal Ken.description:
Animal Ken lets you scare off or calm an animal, or even befriend one. This skill means you understand animal behavior — you can predict how they'll act in a given situation, you know how to train domestic animals, and in some cases you can even soothe a beast or, conversely, deliberately enrage it. Animals are aggressive toward, or conspicuously avoid, vampires who lack this skill.

Specialties:
combat training, wolves, snakes, cats, rats, horses, dogs, falconry, tricks, calming.
Animal Ken.dot1: Animals don't like you, but at least they don't snap at you or bolt in terror.
Animal Ken.dot2: Animals won't pay attention to you unless you try to make contact with them yourself.
Animal Ken.dot3: As long as you don't try to hurt them, animals treat you like a beloved owner.
Animal Ken.dot4: Animals adore you. When you're around, even the most aggressive ones usually stay calm.
Animal Ken.dot5: You intuitively sense the thoughts and feelings of animals, and they, in turn, easily tune in to your wavelength and do what you want of them.

Insight.description:
Insight is the ability to read body language, catch subtle inflections of voice and details of facial expression, and tell truth from lies. It also lets you see what drives others and understand their motives.

Specialties:
interrogation, desires, lies, motives, vices, empathy, phobias, goals, emotions.
Insight.dot2: A person has a hard time hiding their true feelings about someone from you, and even Kindred don't always manage it.
Insight.dot3: The urge to eat the patient doesn't stop you from being an excellent psychotherapist.
Insight.dot4: You're a walking lie detector. It takes a master of deception to pull one over on you.
Insight.dot5: A person may be a "book of blood," but that book is printed in very large type — easy to read.

Persuasion.description:
Use this skill when you want to convince someone you won't hurt them, and that one little bite doesn't even hurt. Masters of Persuasion are equally skilled at playing on a victim's emotions and using logical arguments. Persuasion comes in handy both for a courtroom defense and at the Prince's court. It's useful everywhere: in conference rooms, bars, and bedrooms.

Specialties:
interrogation, sweet-talking, negotiation, rhetoric, litigation, bargaining.
Persuasion.dot1: You can close a deal with an interested party without any trouble.
Persuasion.dot2: You're a decent haggler and can usually talk someone into sharing the details of the latest rumors.
Persuasion.dot3: You can find a compromise in any conflict.
Persuasion.dot4: When you show up in court (whether among mortals or vampires), your opponents book a flight out of town.
Persuasion.dot5: You're probably the silver-tongued devil himself.

Streetwise.description:
Streetwise lets you speak the same language as thugs and lowlifes. You understand code words and slang, know how to use the right gestures, and can read the meaning of graffiti.

Specialties:
bribes, urban survival, graffiti, drug trade, prostitution, fencing stolen goods, weapons dealing, street gangs, private fixer, black market.
Streetwise.dot1: You know where drugs are sold in your domain and how to find a prostitute.
Streetwise.dot2: You can name every gang in your region, their colors, symbols, and rivalries.
Streetwise.dot3: You can tell good merchandise from bad and know where to buy weapons. The underworld treats you as one of their own.
Streetwise.dot4: When a thug says "I know a guy," he means you.
Streetwise.dot5: You can hire the right people and plan and pull off virtually any crime anywhere in the city.

Subterfuge.description:
Subterfuge is the art of lying convincingly, spinning tall tales, and finding plausible excuses for bad behavior. This skill determines how good you are at weaving intrigue, playing both sides, and hatching secret conspiracies. Subterfuge can also be used for seduction and for passing as mortal.

Specialties:
flawless deception, biological functions, bluffing, fraud, innocence, seduction.
Subterfuge.dot1: You can fool a listener as long as the lie isn't too convoluted.
Subterfuge.dot2: You can pull the wool over the eyes of a naive person, an old man, or a child.
Subterfuge.dot3: Your lies have layers: one deception covers another, which is meant to be uncovered as a decoy.
Subterfuge.dot4: You can work undercover for as long as it takes: the perfect double-agent candidate. You might actually be a double agent.
Subterfuge.dot5: No one believes you're even capable of deception.

Etiquette.description:
Etiquette is the ability to observe social conventions no matter what society you find yourself in, as well as the ability to set new rules of behavior and conduct yourself so that everyone stays satisfied. This skill can be applied in mortal society as well as among Kindred.

Specialties:
Anarchs, celebrities, the Camarilla, corporate etiquette, millionaires, secret societies, feudal etiquette, Elysium.
Etiquette.dot1: You know how to properly address the local ruler without seeming tactless.
Etiquette.dot2: You know how to behave correctly at every popular night spot in the domain.
Etiquette.dot3: People are struck by your politeness, deference, and sense of tact.
Etiquette.dot4: You set the tone with your behavior, especially when you're the one breaking accepted norms of etiquette.
Etiquette.dot5: The Keeper of Elysium and the Harpies trust you to draw up the domain's rules of conduct.

Academics.description:
This skill represents higher education in the humanities, an understanding of the field's core issues, and the ability to carry out research in it. History, for instance, stops being purely theoretical once your enemies actually lived through it and left their mark on the distant past. When you take this skill, you get one specialty for free. If you want to know foreign languages, use the Linguist advantage instead.

Specialties:
English literature, architecture, journalism, research, art history, history (specific period and region), education, theology, philosophy.
Academics.dot1: Basic primary and secondary education; night school.
Academics.dot2: Basic university education, or private lessons with a middling tutor; a bachelor's degree.
Academics.dot3: Further university education, or private lessons with an excellent tutor; a master's degree with honors, a doctorate.
Academics.dot4: Advanced specialized training; the study of subjects few people understand.
Academics.dot5: Exceptional erudition and a refined understanding of your professional field. High-level consulting.

Science.description:
Science covers an enormous area: from the basic principles of how living organisms function to understanding the nature of the universe's entropy. The laws of nature govern the mortal world. Vampires who wish to rule that world study them. This skill's rating roughly corresponds to the equivalent rating in Academics: from a high school graduate to a world-renowned scholar. When you take this skill, you also get one specialty for free.

Specialties:
biology, medicine, physics, chemistry, ecology, genetics, pharmacology, forensics, astronomy, engineering.
Science.dot1: You understand a bit about science and the basic principles of how the world is built.
Science.dot2: You can clearly explain to a fellow Kindred the difference between competing scientific hypotheses about the Embrace.
Science.dot3: You have deep knowledge in your field and know how to conduct research and analyze results.
Science.dot4: You're capable of running a lab, correctly interpreting experimental results, and you stay current with most modern research. You also know how to work with complex equipment.
Science.dot5: You're a recognized expert in your field and in some adjacent disciplines. People come to you for consultations.

Medicine.description:
Medicine lets you treat people, diagnose illness, and determine cause of death. You can use medical equipment, you understand medications, and you know how to stop bleeding (or cause it). Medicine is necessary to heal a mortal from severe injury.

Specialties:
veterinary medicine, hematology, pathology, first aid, trauma medicine, pharmaceuticals, phlebotomy, surgery.
Medicine.dot1: You know basic anatomy, can tell a vein from an artery, and know how to perform CPR and first aid. You might have been a medical student.
Medicine.dot2: You know what to do for minor injuries and how to treat common illnesses. You can make a rough diagnosis. You might have been an orderly or a paramedic.
Medicine.dot3: You can perform surgery and know how to help someone with a serious injury. You might have been an intern, a pediatrician, or a general practitioner.
Medicine.dot4: You can diagnose almost any illness and find a way to treat it. You might have been a surgeon or a specialist physician.
Medicine.dot5: You're a renowned luminary of medicine. Both mortals and immortals seek your advice.

Awareness.description:
Awareness is sharpness and speed of perception. You're capable of noticing the Children of Haqim before they strike, finding a key in a trash can, or catching the faintest trace of perfume.

Specialties:
wilderness, ambushes, animal instincts, sight, traps, disguise, smell, hearing, hidden objects.
Awareness.dot1: If something is out of place, you notice it immediately.
Awareness.dot2: You can tell when someone is acting strangely, and you recognize behavioral patterns and deviations from them.
Awareness.dot3: You can sniff out where to find a clue and where danger lurks. A disguise won't easily fool you.
Awareness.dot4: You never lose your vigilance, even when distracted.
Awareness.dot5: You have the eyes of an eagle and the nose of a hound.

Occult.description:
Occult is knowledge of the supernatural world. You understand the rituals and practices of all sorts of traditions, from Freemasons and Rosicrucians to Noddists and true mages. You know occult symbolism and are familiar with folk mystical practices, regardless of how effective they actually are.

Specialties:
alchemy, voodoo, grimoires, Infernalism, Blood Sorcery, mages, Necromancy, Noddism, werewolves, parapsychology, ghosts, the fae.
Occult.dot1: You know the legend of Caine and the Antediluvians. You may have read the Book of Nod.
Occult.dot2: You can tell the difference between the truth about the supernatural and plain superstition.
Occult.dot3: You've encountered things even Kindred would consider inexplicable.
Occult.dot4: You can name most of the Antediluvians and may even understand the substance of some clan Tremere rituals.
Occult.dot5: Tremere and the Children of Haqim come to consult you when they run into something inexplicable.

Politics.description:
Politics encompasses diplomacy and bureaucracy, both in the mortal world and among Kindred. You know how to win City Hall's support, can pressure local authorities, or operate at an even higher level. As for Kindred politics, you understand which faction holds the majority, who's feuding with whom, and where the bodies are buried. Literally.

Specialties:
Anarchs, diplomacy, the Camarilla, clan (name), City Hall, national politics, state/regional politics, media.
Politics.dot1: You understand mortal politics. As for Kindred politics, you know at least whatever the elders aren't trying to hide.
Politics.dot2: You can personally influence local politics, or you know someone who can.
Politics.dot3: You could lead a party if you had to, and you could launch your own political campaign. Members of your own faction are starting to worry about your success.
Politics.dot4: You know who's really pulling the strings of the living and the undead around you.
Politics.dot5: You could name the members of the Camarilla's Inner Circle off the top of your head — and not be wrong.

Investigation.description:
Investigation lets you unravel mysteries — both mundane and supernatural — notice clues, interpret them, and track down missing people. This skill is extremely useful for a vampire whose vessel has run off.

Specialties:
network traffic analysis, deduction, criminology, paranormal phenomena, missing persons searches, forensics, murder.
Investigation.dot1: You love reading detective novels and consider yourself an amateur sleuth.
Investigation.dot2: You're well versed in criminology and know the M.O. of every local criminal.
Investigation.dot3: You're either already a professional detective or could become one. No detail at a crime scene escapes your notice.
Investigation.dot4: The Sheriff comes to you for help when someone threatens the domain.
Investigation.dot5: You're no longer someone who solves mysteries — you're someone who creates them: a mysterious stranger leading an inscrutable existence.

Technology.description:
This skill changes depending on the era, which sets it apart from the others. Broadly speaking, it lets you use and understand "technological advances that most vampires consider alien and incomprehensible." In 1870, for instance, it would have meant understanding how steam engines worked and grasping the potential of electricity. In the 21st century, it means knowing how to handle computers and other electronics. In the modern world, a computer can control virtually any device — including a steam turbine or a skyscraper's power grid.

Specialties:
ordnance, data extraction, computer systems, programming, networks, surveillance systems, telephony, hacking.
Technology.dot1: You know how to reinstall the operating system on a home computer and how to protect it from viruses.
Technology.dot2: You know how to hide your IP address, can pilot drones, and can fake digital photographs.
Technology.dot3: You can write your own virus and release it onto the network without risking exposure.
Technology.dot4: The Prince might call you in personally to handle the domain's information security.
Technology.dot5: On the Internet, nobody knows you're a vampire. Or that you're conducting any activity online at all.

Finance.description:
Finance lets you invest your money profitably, understand market trends, manipulate prices, and sense when a market crash is coming. With this skill you can also appraise someone else's fortune, keep tabs on it, and broker financial deals. You can assess the value of artwork, real estate, and any other expensive items. Ventrue value this skill above some Disciplines.

Specialties:
banking operations, currency markets, corporate finance, money laundering, appraisal, artwork, forensic accounting, the stock exchange, the black market.
Finance.dot1: You understand bookkeeping and could run a firm.
Finance.dot2: You can run a division of a major corporation or a bank branch. Your tax deduction filings are flawless.
Finance.dot3: The growth of international trade has let you become an excellent broker on foreign stock exchanges.
Finance.dot4: You get real-time financial information before it reaches investment banks. Hiding fraudulent transactions is nothing to you.
Finance.dot5: Money is an obedient tool in your hands. You know how to earn it and use it to bring down entire countries.

Brujah.description:
The Brujah are the clan of passion, rage, and ideals. Once philosophers and thinkers, now rebels, revolutionaries, and breakers of systems. They see injustice where others look away, and they cannot ignore it. Every Brujah is torn between the desire to change the world for the better and the desire to destroy it completely.

They don't just get angry — their fury always has a reason. The trouble is, the Beast doesn't distinguish between ideals and impulses. That's exactly why Brujah revolutions so often end in blood.

The clan includes both street fighters and anarchists, as well as intellectuals — philosophers (sometimes called "Hellenes") who believe the system must be understood before it can be torn down. Others simply want to strike first.

The Brujah are closer to humanity than many other clans, but that's exactly what makes their existence so painful: they watch the world break the very people they want to protect.
Brujah.types: Revolutionaries, activists, punks, philosophers, gangsters, students, idealists, agitators

discipline_description.Celerity: Supernatural speed. The Brujah use it to strike suddenly, escape danger, or overwhelm an enemy before they can react.
discipline_description.Potence: Pure destructive force. A Brujah's blows can break bones, smash through walls, and end fights in seconds.
discipline_description.Presence: Charisma and force of personality. Lets them inspire crowds, intimidate enemies, or bend people to their will through emotion.

Brujah.bane: Brujah blood is a boiling cauldron. Their Beast is always right at the surface. The difficulty to resist frenzy is lower by 2, and even a minor provocation can trigger an outburst of rage.
Brujah.playstyle: Impulsive, aggressive, emotional. The Brujah excel at direct conflict, pressure, and charismatic influence. They handle long games and intrigue with no clear payoff poorly.
Brujah.conflict: Control vs. rage. Ideals vs. destruction. The desire to protect people vs. the need to feed on them.
archetypes: Voice of the People — an activist and ideologue leading the masses
Cancer of the System — tears the system apart from within
The Philosopher — searches for meaning and the reasons behind power
The Troll — provokes conflict for its own sake
Wolf in Sheep's Clothing — tries to live like a human

Ventrue.description:
The Ventrue are the clan of power, control, and responsibility. For centuries they have ruled both mortals and Kindred, believing it to be their destiny. Empires, kingdoms, corporations — the Ventrue have always stood at the top.

They believe in order, hierarchy, and the strength of tradition. In their eyes, the world is a system that must be managed, or it collapses. That's exactly why the Ventrue take on the role of rulers — not out of desire, but out of the conviction that no one else can handle it.

In the modern world, they no longer sit openly on thrones. Now they are figures in the shadows: corporate owners, political strategists, the powers behind the throne. They don't rule directly — they steer.

But their power is cracking. The world is changing, and more and more Kindred are questioning their right to rule. That only makes the Ventrue harder, colder, and more willing to go all the way.

They aren't just leaders. They are the system.
Ventrue.types: Aristocrats, politicians, CEOs, bankers, strategists, generals, powers behind the throne

discipline_description.Dominate: Absolute control over the mind. The Ventrue use it to command, erase memories, and control mortals and Kindred alike.
discipline_description.Fortitude: Supernatural endurance. Lets them ignore damage and keep acting where others would fall.
discipline_description.Presence: An aura of power and charisma. Lets them command respect, fear, and loyalty, subduing others through sheer presence rather than force.

Ventrue.bane: Finicky blood. Each Ventrue can only feed on a specific type of person (for example, by status, origin, or wealth). Trying to drink "unsuitable" blood costs Willpower or causes revulsion.
Ventrue.playstyle: Control, strategy, social pressure. The Ventrue excel at politics, management, and manipulation. They win not through force, but through position.
Ventrue.conflict: Responsibility vs. tyranny. Control vs. flexibility. Belief in their right to rule vs. fear of losing power.
archetypes: Business Shark — runs a corporation and treats people as resources
Secret Society Member — wields influence through closed structures
Political Strategist — controls information and public opinion
The Godfather — controls the criminal underworld
Dynasty Heir — obsessed with the greatness of their clan

Gangrel.description:
The Gangrel are the clan of freedom, instinct, and survival. They're closer to the Beast than any other clan, and they don't try to suppress it — they learn to live with it.

While other Kindred hide behind city walls and chains of hierarchy, the Gangrel choose the wild. They cross the borders of domains and countries, recognize no authority but their own, and feel at home where others wouldn't survive.

They aren't romanticized savages. They are predators. In their world there's no room for weakness — only strength, endurance, and the ability to survive at any cost.

The Gangrel have turned their backs on the Camarilla, preferring the pack to the structure. Their society isn't politics — it's relationships built on strength, respect, and personal reputation.

They don't submit. And they don't forgive anyone who tries to tame them.
Gangrel.types: Survivalists, drifters, hunters, bikers, loners, alpha leaders, ex-convicts, explorers

discipline_description.Animalism: A connection to the animal world. Lets them control beasts, sense their emotions, and use them as allies.
discipline_description.Fortitude: Incredible endurance. The Gangrel ignore pain, damage, and extreme conditions, and keep moving forward.
discipline_description.Protean: Transformation of the body. Lets them turn into animals or take on bestial traits, becoming a true predator.

Gangrel.bane: When a Gangrel frenzies, their body changes: bestial traits appear (scent, appearance, behavior). These changes persist into the next night and temporarily lower the character's attributes.
Gangrel.playstyle: Survival, mobility, physical dominance. The Gangrel are strong outside civilization and in direct conflict. They fit poorly into social games, but excel at hunting and combat.
Gangrel.conflict: Humanity vs. the Beast. Freedom vs. belonging to the pack. Instinct vs. control.
archetypes: The Wanderer — an eternal drifter who knows the roads better than people do
Alpha — pack leader, dominant through strength and charisma
Ex-Convict — despises the system and lives by their own rules
Corporate Predator — uses force in the world of business
Born Beast — has almost completely abandoned their humanity

Malkavian.description:
The Malkavians are the clan of madness, insight, and distorted perception of reality. Their minds are broken — or perhaps they simply see more than a mind can bear.

From the outside, they look mentally ill: schizophrenia, depression, obsessions, trauma. But the Malkavians themselves don't see it as an illness. To them, it's a side effect of understanding the world on a deeper level.

They feel more intensely, think differently, and often arrive at the truth by a path that looks absurd to everyone else. They're called fools, but there was a time when people came to them for counsel and prophecy.

Malkavians can be frighteningly precise or dangerously unstable — and more often than not, it's both at once.

They're said to all be connected through some kind of shared consciousness — "the Cobweb." Sometimes it grants them insight. Sometimes it breaks them for good.
Malkavian.types: Prophets, psychotherapists, mediums, artists, fanatics, visionaries, manipulators

discipline_description.Dominate: Control over the mind. The Malkavians use it to shatter psyches, amplify fears, or seize a victim's attention.
discipline_description.Obfuscate: Invisibility and stealth. Lets them observe, follow, and exist beyond others' perception.
discipline_description.Auspex: Heightened senses and insight into the mind. Lets them see the emotions, fears, and secrets of others.

Malkavian.bane: Every Malkavian suffers from an incurable mental disorder. It can take the form of hallucinations, obsessions, or distorted perception. Under stress or on a failed roll, it intensifies and affects the character's actions.
Malkavian.playstyle: Information, manipulation, unconventional thinking. The Malkavians excel at reading people and breaking down others' minds. They're unpredictable — and that's their greatest weapon.
Malkavian.conflict: Genius vs. madness. Control vs. the disintegration of the self. Understanding the truth vs. the inability to bear it.
archetypes: The Oracle — sees more than others, but pays for it with stability
The Psychoanalyst — takes other people's psyches apart piece by piece
The Fanatic — obsessed with a single idea to the extreme
The Medium — communicates with what others cannot see
The Manipulator — uses madness as a tool

Nosferatu.description:
The Nosferatu are the clan of ugliness, survival, and knowledge. Their Embrace is an agonizing transformation that turns the body into a grotesque, broken shell. There's no mistaking them for human.

But behind that appearance lies a paradox: the Nosferatu often turn out to be the most humane of the Kindred. Their curse destroys the body, but not necessarily the soul.

They live in the shadows — in basements, sewers, catacombs, and abandoned places. Where others couldn't survive, they build their networks.

The Nosferatu are masters of information. They watch, listen, gather, and trade secrets. Their power doesn't come from status, but from knowledge.

They're used to being hated, feared, or despised. Which is why they've learned the most important skill of all — surviving when no one is on your side.
Nosferatu.types: Spies, informants, hackers, drifters, survivalists, detectives, hermits

discipline_description.Animalism: Control over animals. Lets them use rats, insects, and other creatures as scouts, messengers, and weapons.
discipline_description.Potence: Physical strength. Lets them quickly neutralize enemies and act effectively in close combat.
discipline_description.Obfuscate: Invisibility and disguise. Lets them hide, spy, and exist outside others' field of view.

Nosferatu.bane: A monstrous appearance. The Nosferatu can't pass for human without a disguise. Their appearance provokes disgust and fear, complicating any social interaction.
Nosferatu.playstyle: Information, stealth, survival. The Nosferatu avoid direct conflict, preferring to know more than the enemy and act from the shadows.
Nosferatu.conflict: Humanity vs. isolation. The desire to be accepted vs. the impossibility of being seen. Power through knowledge vs. rejection.
archetypes: The Connection — keeps and passes along information between Kindred
The Sleuth — investigates mysteries and finds what's hidden
Rat Catcher — controls animals and lives among them
The Hunter — relies on strength and surprise
The Traitor — survives at any cost, even at the cost of others

Toreador.description:
The Toreador are the clan of beauty, emotion, and obsession. They strive for aesthetic perfection in all its forms: in art, in people, in suffering, even in death.

Their curse is feeling too intensely. Where others see an ordinary scene, a Toreador can see perfection — and lose themselves in it.

They aren't just connoisseurs of art — they're its creators, curators, and destroyers. They shape culture, influence tastes, lift some up to fame and destroy others.

The Toreador are the heart of the Camarilla. They inspire, unite, and set the tone. But behind that beauty lies danger: love taken to its limit becomes obsession.

They live for feeling. And they'll do anything to experience it.
Toreador.types: Artists, actors, musicians, designers, influencers, curators, hedonists

discipline_description.Presence: Control of emotion through charisma. Lets them charm, inspire, and subdue others through feeling.
discipline_description.Celerity: Supernatural speed. Used both in combat and in art — for flawless execution.
discipline_description.Auspex: Heightened perception. Lets them sense emotions, find "perfect" vessels, and understand others' desires.

Toreador.bane: An obsession with beauty. A Toreador can lose control when confronted with something aesthetically perfect, falling into a stupor or forgetting everything else.
Toreador.playstyle: Social influence, emotion, aesthetics. The Toreador lead through inspiration, charm, and cultural influence rather than force.
Toreador.conflict: Beauty vs. reality. Inspiration vs. obsession. Humanity vs. dependence on sensation.
archetypes: The Creator — makes art that people admire
The Impresario — manages other people's talent and fame
Life of the Party — the center of attention and emotion
The Patron — shapes other people's fates like a work of art
The Spy — uses charm and theatrics to gather information

Tremere.description:
The Tremere are the clan of magic, control, and an obsession with knowledge. Unlike other Kindred, they weren't born vampires — they became vampires deliberately, paying a monstrous price for it.

They were once mages searching for immortality. Their experiments led to a curse they brought upon themselves. Now their power is blood.

The clan used to be rigidly structured as the Pyramid — a strict hierarchy where everyone knew their place. But after its fall, the Tremere became scattered, ambitious, and dangerous.

They're still obsessed with knowledge. Artifacts, rituals, ancient secrets — a Tremere will go to any length for them.

They don't rule openly. They understand that real power is knowledge and control over reality itself.
Tremere.types: Mages, scholars, occultists, researchers, alchemists, manipulators

discipline_description.Dominate: Control over the mind. Lets them command the will, erase memories, and use others as tools.
discipline_description.Blood Sorcery: Blood magic. Lets them alter reality, attack, defend, and manipulate life force.
discipline_description.Auspex: Extrasensory perception. Lets them see auras, detect magic, and read hidden intentions.

Tremere.bane: Tainted blood. It's harder for the Tremere to create blood bonds — their vitae is weakened and requires more effort to bind others.
Tremere.playstyle: Control, knowledge, preparation. The Tremere are strong when they have time to study a situation and prepare. They win through information and magic.
Tremere.conflict: Knowledge vs. humanity. Control vs. chaos. Power through magic vs. losing one's own nature.
archetypes: Apologist of the Pyramid — seeks to restore the old order
Immortal Scholar — obsessed with knowledge
The Nonconformist — searches for new paths of magic
Ambitious Upstart — breaks tradition for the sake of power
Warding Sorcerer — uses magic for control and security

Caitiff.description:
The Caitiff are vampires without a clan. They have no lineage, no traditions, and no inheritance from the Antediluvians. Either they were rejected, or they left on their own.

To other Kindred, they're an error, a glitch in the system. But the Caitiff themselves see this as an advantage.

They have no inborn bane, no clan restrictions, and no obligations. They're free.

But that freedom has a price. They have no protection, no status, and no place in Kindred society. They're the bottom rung, and they have to survive alone or build their own alliances.

Every year there are more Caitiff. And the more of them there are, the more the entire clan system starts to shake.
Caitiff.types: Loners, survivors, outcasts, drifters, avengers, independents

discipline_description.Special Trait: A Caitiff isn't tied to clan Disciplines. They can study any ability, as long as they gain access to the right blood and knowledge.

Caitiff.bane: No clan. The Caitiff have no shared bane, but are considered "lesser" among Kindred. Their status is limited, and many don't trust them.
Caitiff.playstyle: Flexibility, adaptation, survival. The Caitiff have no fixed role and can adjust to any situation.
Caitiff.conflict: Freedom vs. isolation. No restrictions vs. no support. Individuality vs. the need to belong.
archetypes: The Urchin — survived against all odds
The Victim — gained power through suffering
Hidden Caitiff — pretends to be a member of a clan
Unwanted Child — rejected by their own "family"
A Clan of One — builds their own power from nothing

Thin-blood.description:
The thin-blooded are vampires on the border between life and death. Their blood is too weak for them to be full Kindred, but too strong for them to remain human.

They're either a glitch in the system or its future, depending on who you ask.

Unlike other vampires, the thin-blooded retain part of their human nature: they can eat, feel warmth, and sometimes even go out in the sun. That makes them closer to mortals — and, at the same time, alien to everyone.

They belong to no clan, inherit no banes, and don't fit into the hierarchy. Because of this, they're feared, despised, and often destroyed.

But that very instability gives them a unique advantage: they adapt to the world better than the older generations.

They aren't a relic of the past. They're a possible future for vampires.
Thin-blood.types: Survivors, newcomers, outcasts, experimenters, seekers of meaning, those balancing between worlds

discipline_description.Thin-blood Alchemy: Lets them create effects that mimic other vampires' Disciplines through formulas and blood.
discipline_description.Temporary Disciplines: When the thin-blooded consume blood, they temporarily gain abilities depending on the blood's resonance.

Thin-blood.bane: Weak blood. Blood Potency is 0, abilities are unstable, and siring offspring is practically impossible.
Thin-blood.playstyle: Adaptation, flexibility, survival. The thin-blooded adjust to the situation, using temporary powers and unconventional solutions.
Thin-blood.conflict: Human vs. vampire. Hope vs. curse. Freedom vs. vulnerability.
archetypes: The Everyman — lives almost like a human
The Victim — Embraced by accident
The Soldier — dragged into someone else's war
The Obsessed — seeks power or a cure
The Penitent — tries to remain human

special_rules.Blood: Cannot reliably create ghouls or offspring.
special_rules.Damage: Takes damage almost like a mortal.
special_rules.Spark of Life: Can look and function like a living human.

Assamite.description: The Assamites are the clan of assassins, judges, and keepers of ancient traditions. To most Kindred, they're known as hired killers who silently eliminate targets for powerful patrons. But behind the assassins' reputation lies a complex culture built on wisdom, sorcery, and the drive to draw closer to the ideal embodied by their progenitor, Haqim. For centuries, the Assamites have preserved their independence from the major sects, preferring to serve duty, contract, and clan rather than ideology.
Assamite.types: Assassins, bounty hunters, judges, mercenaries, spies, sorcerers, trackers, bodyguards

discipline_description.Celerity: Supernatural speed, letting them catch up to a victim before they even realize what's happening.
discipline_description.Obfuscate: The art of becoming invisible, vanishing from sight, and acting from the shadows.
discipline_description.Quietus: The Assamites' mysterious Discipline, letting them use blood to poison, weaken, and destroy enemies.

Assamite.bane: The Tremere curse made the blood of other Kindred lethally dangerous to the Assamites. Every point of vampire blood they drink inflicts one unavoidable point of aggravated damage. Diablerie likewise causes aggravated damage and grants none of the usual benefits. In addition, the Assamites are obligated to regularly hand over a share of their earnings to the clan and its elders.
Assamite.playstyle: Stealth, target elimination, reconnaissance, and precision operations. The Assamites excel alone or in small groups, favoring preparation and surprise over open confrontation.
Assamite.conflict: Duty vs. freedom. Wisdom vs. bloodlust. Independence vs. loyalty to Alamut. The drive for perfection vs. the price that must be paid for power.
archetypes: The Assassin — a silent killer who carries out contracts
Judge of Haqim — punishes the guilty and metes out their own justice
Sorcerer of Alamut — keeper of ancient rituals and knowledge
The Tracker — can find a target anywhere in the world
The Bodyguard — protects a client as effectively as they kill enemies

Giovanni.description: The Giovanni are the clan of necromancers, bankers, and keepers of the family legacy. Their power grew out of trade, financial empires, and the study of death. For centuries the Giovanni family has accumulated wealth, influence, and occult knowledge, becoming one of the most powerful independent forces among the Kindred. Behind their impeccable manners and aristocratic luxury lie cruelty, a cult of ancestors, and a drive to tear down the Shroud between the world of the living and the realm of the dead.
Giovanni.types: Necromancers, bankers, aristocrats, financiers, mobsters, mediums, keepers of family secrets, debt collectors

discipline_description.Dominate: The will of the family is stronger than the will of any one person. The Giovanni subdue debtors, relatives, and enemies with a single word.
discipline_description.Necromancy: The art of communing with the dead, commanding ghosts, and probing the Shroud between life and death.
discipline_description.Potence: Supernatural strength hidden behind expensive suits, impeccable manners, and an aristocratic appearance.

Giovanni.bane: A Giovanni's kiss causes mortals agonizing pain. Every time a Giovanni drinks a human's blood, they inflict twice as much damage as an ordinary vampire would. Many vessels can't withstand the feeding and die of shock.
Giovanni.playstyle: Manipulation, deals, finance, family intrigue, blackmail, and dealings with the world of the dead. The Giovanni excel at long-term schemes, accumulating influence, and using secrets from the past against their enemies.
Giovanni.conflict: Family vs. the individual. Wealth vs. humanity. Control over death vs. fear of one's own demise. Loyalty to family vs. morality.
archetypes: Family Patriarch — runs the relatives, assets, and debts
The Necromancer — studies ghosts and the secrets of death
The Financier — controls money, banks, and investments
The Mobster — uses criminal connections and family resources
Keeper of the Shroud — seeks to tear down the boundary between worlds

Lasombra.description: The Lasombra are the aristocracy of the night, lords of shadow and the spiritual architects of the Sabbat. They consider fear, power, and superiority the natural order of things. To them, the Beast isn't a curse but a tool. The Lasombra seek to rule over themselves, over others, and over darkness itself, using ambition, discipline, and an iron will to achieve greatness.
Lasombra.types: Aristocrats, clergy, inquisitors, strategists, manipulators, lords of shadow, spiritual leaders, dark nobility

discipline_description.Dominate: Power over mind and will. The Lasombra are used to commanding, and they demand obedience.
discipline_description.Oblivion: Command of living darkness, shadows, and the abyss beyond the material world.
discipline_description.Potence: Supernatural physical strength, letting them crush enemies and assert superiority.

Lasombra.bane: The Lasombra cast no reflection in mirrors, still water, polished surfaces, or other reflective objects. Cameras and modern devices also often fail to capture them correctly, or at all.
Lasombra.playstyle: Leadership, political intrigue, control, manipulation, and displays of superiority. The Lasombra feel right at home at the top of hierarchies, and prefer to lead rather than follow.
Lasombra.conflict: Duty vs. ambition. Control over the Beast vs. accepting one's own monstrous nature. Power for a purpose vs. power for its own sake.
archetypes: The Dark Aristocrat — heir to ancient power and tradition
Sabbat Confessor — a preacher who leads others through darkness
Lord of Shadows — a master of Oblivion and supernatural dread
The Inquisitor — hunts for weakness and betrayal
The Manipulator — builds networks of dependency, debt, and power

Followers of Set.description:
The Followers of Set are the clan of temptation, darkness, and forbidden liberation. Where other Kindred see degradation, addiction, despair, and vice, the Setites see merchandise, a tool, and a door into someone else's soul.

They aren't merely merchants of sin. They are priests of a dark cult, for whom every deal, every weakness, and every willing fall becomes part of their service to Set-Typhon — an ancient serpent god that devours souls.

The Setites arrive when a person or vampire is already nearly broken. They offer help, pleasure, secrets, money, drugs, flesh, power, or comfort — but always on their own terms. Their gifts rarely look like a trap in the moment. That's exactly why people come back to them.

Kindred despise them, fear them, and call them parasites, but they still come to the Setites for things they don't dare seek openly. The Followers of Set know how to keep secrets, honor obligations, and turn other people's desires into chains.

They don't see themselves as corruptors. In their eyes, they free souls from hypocrisy, fear, and false taboos. The only problem is that freedom, from the Serpents, almost always smells of poison.
Followers of Set.types: Cultists, priests, pimps, drug dealers, club owners, occultists, seducers, smugglers, secret-traders, outcasts, exiles, spiritual manipulators

discipline_description.Obfuscate: Stealth, disappearance, and a life lived in shadow. The Setites use it to run secret cults, vanish after deals, and watch others' weaknesses unnoticed.
discipline_description.Presence: Supernatural charisma and force of personality. Lets them charm, seduce, inspire trust, and make a forbidden offer almost impossible to refuse.
discipline_description.Serpentis: The clan's serpentine, chthonic power. Lets them take on serpent traits, hypnotize, instill dread, and manifest their bond with the dark god Set.

Followers of Set.bane: Vulnerability to bright light. Sunlight deals two extra points of damage to Followers of Set. In addition, if a Setite acts under bright light — a spotlight, stage lights, a flare, or a similar source — their dice pool for the check is reduced by 1d10.
Followers of Set.playstyle: Manipulation, seduction, secret deals, and cult influence. The Setites excel wherever they can exploit others' desires, addictions, shame, and despair. They rarely push directly — they offer exactly what the victim already wants.
Followers of Set.conflict: Liberation vs. corruption. Faith vs. exploitation. A willing bargain vs. an invisible trap. Darkness as refuge vs. darkness as poison.
archetypes: Silver-Tongued Devil — offers exactly what the victim is afraid of wanting
Priest of the Dark Temple — leads Set's cult and converts the desperate
Vice Merchant — controls drugs, sex, secrets, and forbidden pleasures
Keeper of Others' Secrets — sells confidentiality for more than gold
Broken Neophyte — was broken themselves, and so understands perfectly those ready to accept the forbidden fruit
Serpent at Court — an outwardly polite go-between secretly owed favors by half the influential Kindred

Ravnos.description:
The Ravnos are the clan of the open road, deception, and dangerous freedom. They appear and vanish like rumors: seen on the edge of the domain today, and tomorrow all that's left of them is debt, missing belongings, and a strange feeling that none of it was quite real.

Other Kindred consider them con artists, thieves, liars, and unreliable drifters. Sometimes that's prejudice. Sometimes it's the plain truth. The Ravnos have long since gotten used to being distrusted, and many of them simply start playing the role expected of them.

They rarely settle in one place. Many live on the road, sticking to the outskirts, industrial zones, motels, cars, and temporary shelters. The important thing for them is always having an escape route, because trouble finds the Ravnos faster than it finds almost any other clan.

But behind the swindler's image lies a more complex philosophy. Older and more devout Ravnos speak of rebirth, karma, the illusory nature of the world, and a spiritual path. The young and unscrupulous often use those same ideas as an excuse for chaos: if everything is fleeting, why not take whatever's lying around?

Their unique power — Chimerstry — only makes this reputation worse. The Ravnos can make others see things that aren't there, turning a lie into something almost tangible. In a world where every Kindred already wears a mask, the Deceivers are simply more honest than the rest in admitting: it's all a game.
Ravnos.types: Drifters, thieves, con artists, swindlers, smugglers, street performers, fugitives, fortune-tellers, nomads, survivors, adventurers, storytellers, illusionists

discipline_description.Animalism: A bond with animals and the Beast. The Ravnos use it on the road: as protection, reconnaissance, a means of survival, and a way to stay in touch with the world beyond city courtyards.
discipline_description.Chimerstry: Illusions and deception of the senses. Lets them create visions, distract enemies, hide the truth, and turn lies into a weapon.
discipline_description.Fortitude: Supernatural endurance. Helps the Ravnos survive the road, chases, fights, ambushes, and the fallout of someone else's anger when the latest scam spins out of control.

Ravnos.bane: Bondage to a vice. Every Ravnos is bound to a particular vice: lying, theft, cruelty, gambling, betrayal, or some other compulsive weakness. When the chance arises to give in to that vice, the character must resist it with a Resolve or instinct check. On a failure, they act according to their vice, even if it's dangerous or disadvantageous.
Ravnos.playstyle: Mobility, deception, survival, and playing on others' perceptions. The Ravnos excel at escapes, cons, unconventional solutions, and situations where throwing the enemy off balance matters. They rarely win through position — they win through movement, nerve, and illusion.
Ravnos.conflict: Freedom vs. exile. Deception as protection vs. deception as dependency. The road vs. a home. A philosophy of rebirth vs. a banal excuse for one's own vices.
archetypes: Eternal Drifter — never stays in a domain longer than necessary
The Con Artist — turns other people's greed and gullibility into profit
The Illusionist — hides the truth under layers of visions and false trails
Nomadic Survivor — knows the motels, highways, industrial zones, and safe escape routes
Karmic Preacher — sees deception and suffering as part of a spiritual path
Unwanted Child — was Embraced by accident or for someone's convenience, and now survives however they can
The Slippery Ally — helps against a shared threat, but vanishes the moment it's defeated

Tzimisce.description:
The Tzimisce are the clan of flesh, dominion, and cold, transcendent horror. They're not merely monsters out of old Eastern European castles — they are masters, sculptors, and god-like tyrants for whom the body is raw material, territory is an extension of the self, and fear is the natural response of lesser beings to greatness.

Since time immemorial the Old Clan has held lands, people, and the roads of the night. Their image — an ancient vampire lord in a castle above a ravine — isn't just a romantic legend, but a cultural memory of a clan that for centuries treated mortals as cattle, servants, raw material, and hereditary property.

Their signature power is Vicissitude. The Tzimisce reshape skin, flesh, and bone with frightening ease, turning themselves and others into living works of horror. For some, it's a tool of war, torture, and power. For others, it's a philosophy — a path beyond human form, beyond vampirism, beyond nature itself.

The Tzimisce tend toward extremes. Among them are ancient landowners, mystics, sorcerers, Sabbat fanatics, reshaped war machines, and beings that barely resemble humans anymore. But they're all united by one thing: they have a poor grasp of the line between hospitality and captivity, care and ownership, art and dismemberment.

They can be refined, cultured, and impeccably polite. But that politeness doesn't make them any more human. If anything, the opposite: standing next to a Tzimisce makes it especially clear that you're facing a being that stopped considering the human body something sacred long ago.
Tzimisce.types: Aristocrats, landowners, sorcerers, surgeons, body-horror artists, Sabbat fanatics, recluses, war monsters, mystics, sadists, domain lords, transhumanists

discipline_description.Animalism: A bond with the Beast, animals, and lower nature. The Tzimisce use it to command creatures, guard their domain, and assert their dominion over every living thing on their territory.
discipline_description.Auspex: Supernatural perception. Lets them read fear, see what's hidden, study a victim as raw material, and understand the world more deeply than ordinary senses allow.
discipline_description.Vicissitude: Manipulation of flesh and bone. The Tzimisce use it to reshape themselves, maim enemies, create monstrous servants, and search for a form beyond human nature.

Tzimisce.bane: An attachment to their native soil. To rest properly during the day, a Tzimisce must keep at least two handfuls of earth from a place closely tied to their mortal life or their Embrace close by. If they rest without their native soil, their dice pool for every check is halved. This penalty accumulates every night until the pool drops to a single d10, and disappears only after a full rest with their native soil.
Tzimisce.playstyle: Territorial control, body horror, mystical knowledge, and physical transformation. The Tzimisce are strong in a prepared haven, in the long game, in intimidation, in creating servants, and in turning the body into a weapon. They're rarely socially flexible, but they excel wherever power can be established, space reshaped, and surroundings bent to their will.
Tzimisce.conflict: Humanity vs. transcendence. Ownership vs. closeness. Hospitality vs. captivity. The body as identity vs. the body as raw material. Ancient aristocracy vs. the need to exist in the modern world.
archetypes: Lord of the Old Castle — an ancient master of land, servants, and the roads of night
Sculptor of Flesh — turns bodies into art, weapons, or punishment
Mystic of Vicissitude — seeks a path beyond vampirism and the human form
Pack Confessor — leads a Sabbat pack through horror, ritual, and a philosophy of superiority
War Machine — created or raised as their sire's living weapon
Cold Surgeon — studies the body as a machine that can be improved
Domain Recluse — almost never leaves their haven, and treats outsiders as a threat to their property
Reshaped Monster — willingly abandoned human form for a new shape

Ahrimanes.description:
The Ahrimanes are a bloodline of feline freedom, shamanic transformation, and sisterly exile. Their history begins with Maricia, a Gangrel antitribu whose pack was wiped out in a war between Sabbat factions. Surviving alone, she rejected both her clan and her former loyalties, took the name Ahrimane, and changed the very nature of her blood.

No one knows exactly how she managed it. Maricia may have started out as a mystical anomaly. She may have been helped by mortal mages hoping to trigger a change that would sterilize all Kindred and put an end to Caine's curse. But the result turned out far stranger: Maricia lost the ability to sire offspring, create ghouls, or form blood bonds, but instead learned to turn other female vampires — Gangrel above all — into beings like herself.

The Ahrimanes are called Cats because of a shared atavism inherited from Maricia: feline eyes. But the nickname suits them more deeply than it seems. They're independent, cautious, armed, distrustful, and recognize no masters. Formally they might be considered tied to the Sabbat, but in practice they've always walked their own path.

This bloodline was never numerous. Maricia accepted only women and turned away male candidates. Her followers built a network of havens across the southern United States, stayed close to one another, helped each other, and severed nearly every contact with the wider vampire society.

In the late 1990s, the Ahrimanes vanished. Their havens were found empty. Some believe the Cats were destroyed. Others believe Maricia led her sisters on a pilgrimage to hidden, sacred places other Kindred can't reach. Either way, the Ahrimanes remain a legend of vampires who tried to evolve beyond clan, blood, and the authority of sires.
Ahrimanes.types: Former Gangrel, shamans, exiles, huntresses, armed wanderers, mystical defectors, sisterly protectors, survivors, feline predators, southern recluses

discipline_description.Animalism: A bond with animals, instinct, and the Beast. The Ahrimanes use it as a legacy of their Gangrel nature: for survival, hunting, communing with animals, and defending their havens.
discipline_description.Presence: Supernatural influence and force of presence. For the Cats, this isn't courtly charisma but the magnetism of wild authority: the calm gaze of a predator, with a sensed threat behind it.
discipline_description.Spiritus: The bloodline's shamanic mystical practice. Lets them call on spiritual forces, work with the unseen world, and continue the path of transformation Maricia began.

Ahrimanes.bane: Inert blood. The Ahrimanes can't grant the Embrace, create ghouls, or form blood bonds. A human or animal who drinks their vitae doesn't become a ghoul, and their blood can't be used as a tool of inheritance, dependency, or control.
Ahrimanes.playstyle: Independence, survival, mysticism, and sisterly solidarity. The Ahrimanes are strong at solitary hunting, defending their territory, shamanic practices, and resisting outside authority. They fit poorly into court politics, but work excellently as a hidden, tight-knit, and dangerous small group.
Ahrimanes.conflict: Freedom vs. isolation. The evolution of the blood vs. the loss of a legacy. Sisterhood vs. loneliness. Shamanic awakening vs. the fear of being destroyed for being different. Rejecting a sire's authority vs. dependence on Maricia as a shared source.
archetypes: Sister of Maricia — personally Embraced or mentored by the bloodline's founder
Former Gangrel — left the clan for a new nature and a new family
Armed Cat — practical, armed, and always ready to disappear
Spiritus Shaman — studies the spirit world and the mystery of the blood's transformation
Southern Recluse — lives in one of the old havens on the outskirts of cities in the American South
Sabbat Runaway — formally counted among them, but lets no one give her orders
Vanished Pilgrim — one of those who may have left with Maricia for unknown, hallowed places

Anda.description:
The Anda are a vanished Gangrel bloodline born on the Great Steppe. Their founder is held to be Dobrul the Bold, an ancient traveler and Methuselah respected by the Mongol nomads. He granted the Embrace to the steppe's greatest warriors, and they became spirits of the night — protectors of their tribes, shepherds of blood, and keepers of the nomadic order.

For the Anda, vampirism wasn't only a curse. It was a vow. A new Tartar underwent a long rite of acceptance: the other Anda debated his worthiness, and strength alone was not enough. The candidate had to be not just a warrior, but a protector of his yasun — the mortal kin-community whose blood sustained his existence.

The Anda stayed close to their tribes, but not among them. They lived at a distance: far enough to remain spirits of the night and not frighten mortals, but close enough to come to the rescue when enemies appeared. They rode with war parties, guarded encampments, fought monsters and mortal armies, and sometimes set off on solitary journeys across the steppes and the lands of the West.

Their moral code was strict, because without it a long existence on the road quickly curdled into madness. A Tartar had to remember: he was not merely a predator. He was a shepherd. His people were a source of blood, but also a responsibility. Whoever forgot this stopped being a protector and became a bloodthirsty steppe spirit, doomed one day to fall into torpor from hunger beneath the earth.

The Anda's golden age came during the era of the Mongol conquests, especially under Kublai Khan, when the Tartars were openly venerated as mighty patrons of the night. But clashes with Chinese were-creatures and vampires, the loss of their patronage, and the changing world gradually destroyed the bloodline. By the end of the Dark Medieval period, the Anda had, as far as anyone knows, disappeared. If any of them still exist, they are ancient predators slumbering underground somewhere in the steppes of Mongolia or China.
Anda.types: Steppe warriors, nomadic protectors, night spirits, riders, trackers, shamans, hunters, tribal guardians, exiled predators, ancient wanderers

discipline_description.Animalism: A bond with animals, the Beast, and the spirit of the steppe. The Anda use it to communicate with horses, predators, and guard animals, and to stay connected to nature and instinct.
discipline_description.Protean: Bestial transformation and merging with the earth. For the Anda, this is not just a weapon but a way to survive on the steppe: to hide, wait out the day, take a predator's form, and become part of the land.
discipline_description.Fortitude: Supernatural endurance. Lets them endure hunger, cold, night marches, battles, long journeys, and the harsh conditions of the Great Steppe.

Anda.bane: The call of the road, and Gangrel atavisms. Like the Gangrel, the Anda can develop bestial traits when they frenzy, but it happens less often — roughly every other time. The Tartars' main weakness, however, has to do with staying still: starting on the fourth day of rest within a kilometer of the previous day's haven, the Anda's dice pool for every check is halved. This penalty accumulates every night until the pool drops to a single d10, and disappears only after a day's rest in a new location, sufficiently far away.
Anda.playstyle: Nomadic survival, defending territory on the move, hunting, combat, and spiritual duty. The Anda are strong on the road, on the steppes, on the fringes of civilization, and in campaigns where movement, reconnaissance, survival, and ties to the tribe matter. They fit poorly into static urban politics, because their very blood demands movement.
Anda.conflict: Protector vs. predator. Duty to the tribe vs. hunger. Nomadic freedom vs. the need to belong to a yasun. Movement vs. torpor. A spiritual code vs. bestial solitude.
archetypes: Night Shepherd — protects their yasun, because its blood belongs to them
Steppe Rider — lives on the road and thinks in routes, not domains
Warrior-Shaman — combines martial skill with the spiritual rites of the steppe
Khan of the Full Moon — a senior Anda whom the other Tartars defer to at a gathering
Lone Renegade — has lost their tribe and feeds on random victims
Spirit of the Earth — a master of Earth Meld, able to vanish into the steppe without a trace
Relic of the Dark Medieval — one of the last Anda, awakened after centuries of sleep underground

Baali.description:
The Baali are a bloodline of infernalism, secret cults, and apocalyptic knowledge. Even among Kindred used to blood, lies, and monstrosity, the Demons remain a bogeyman: a name sires use to scare overconfident neonates when they start thinking they've already seen real darkness.

According to legend, the Baali's founder was the offspring of one of the Antediluvians, who struck a pact with powers far beyond human understanding. Renouncing his former name, he became Baal the Destroyer and led his followers against the Second City. Their war ended in defeat, but the bloodline itself didn't disappear — it went underground, became a frightening fairy tale, and then began stirring again beneath the skin of the modern world.

The Baali survived not through strength, but through caution. The foolish and self-satisfied infernalists died long ago at the hands of the Inquisition, other Kindred, or their own patrons. Those who remained learned to hide in plain sight: behind the masks of other clans, in universities, libraries, old mansions, cults, closed societies, and places where history itself smells of rot.

The Baali's chief passion is secrets. They're ready to betray, spend a fortune, strike an impossible deal, or offer a sacrifice for a single new fragment of forbidden knowledge. To them, the occult isn't a hobby or a tool of power, but a way to hasten the inevitable end. They don't dream of ruling Hell, and they're not trying to sell their souls at a profit. They believe the world is already doomed, and that every drop of blood, every sacrifice, and every ritual only widens the cracks in the dam holding back Gehenna.

That's exactly what makes the Baali so dangerous. They aren't necessarily insane, theatrical, or impulsive. On the contrary, the best of them are patient, educated, charming, and almost unnoticeable. But behind that mask is a being who has already accepted the end of all things — and is ready to die with a smile if their death helps bring that end about.
Baali.types: Infernalists, occultists, cultists, defrocked priests, heretical scholars, cult leaders, demonologists, secret advisors, librarians of forbidden knowledge, apocalyptic fanatics, hidden agents

discipline_description.Presence: Supernatural charm and the power of temptation. The Baali use it to find a victim's weaknesses, inspire trust, tempt with forbidden knowledge, and lead people to a willing fall.
discipline_description.Daimoinon: The bloodline's infernal power. Lets the Baali call upon demonic influences, curse, corrupt, instill dread, and open cracks between the world and the abyss.
discipline_description.Obfuscate: Stealth, disguise, and disappearance. The Baali use it to hide their nature, run secret cults, survive purges, and exist among enemies wearing other people's faces.

Baali.bane: A revulsion toward sacred symbols. The Baali can't stand the sacred symbols of any religion: they find them painful to look at, and touching one burns their flesh. If a Baali is confronted with a manifestation of True Faith intended to repel, neutralize, or harm a vampire, the effect of that manifestation is doubled.
Baali.playstyle: Secret cults, forbidden knowledge, temptation, and slow destruction from within.
