Wall of Water.limits: The wall can't be bent. It can be jumped or flown over, if that's possible.

Dehydrate.description: The thaumaturge evaporates the water from the victim's body.
Dehydrate.roll: an opposed activation check against the victim's Stamina + Fortitude, difficulty 9
Dehydrate.mortal_effect: Each remaining success deals the mortal 1 unavoidable aggravated wound.
Dehydrate.vampire_effect: Each remaining success strips the vampire of 1 Blood point. Damage begins once there's no blood left.
Dehydrate.pain: The victim immediately makes a Courage check at a difficulty of 3 + the thaumaturge's remaining successes. On a failure, they lose the ability to act for 1 turn from the pain.

Path of Conjuring.description: A path for creating objects out of nothing. Conjured objects are always generic, repeating the same form every time they're created, and appear new, whole, with no signs of wear.

system.size_limit: An object larger than the thaumaturge themselves cannot be conjured.
system.familiarity: The sorcerer must know the object well. An image or imagination raises the difficulty; a very familiar object can lower it.
system.quality: 1 success creates a crude approximation, 5 successes create an almost flawless copy.

Simple Conjuration.description: Creates a simple object made of one material with no moving parts: a pipe, a stake, a club, a stone.
Simple Conjuration.maintenance: After creation, 1 Willpower point must be spent each turn or the object disappears.

Permanency.description: Lets an object created by Simple Conjuration be made permanent.
Permanency.cost: 3 Blood points instead of the usual 1.

Skilled Conjuration.description: Creates complex objects from multiple materials, with detail and moving parts: a gun, a bicycle, a chainsaw, a phone.
Skilled Conjuration.effect: Objects created this way are permanent by default.
Skilled Conjuration.extra_roll: For especially complex objects, the Storyteller may require a Craft, Science, Technology, or other fitting skill check.

Unmaking.description: Erases an object created by the powers of the Path of Conjuring.
Unmaking.effect: Requires scoring at least as many successes as the object's creator scored when conjuring it.

Simulacrum.description: Creates an imitation of a living being — a simulacrum.
Simulacrum.effect: The simulacrum has no free will, and can only carry out simple orders from its creator.
Simulacrum.duration: About a week, after which it unmakes itself.
Simulacrum.use: A human simulacrum's body can be used as a vessel for the power of Possession.

Path of Mars.description: A combat path used by Sabbat thaumaturges, directing blood sorcery toward war. It boosts courage, accuracy, evasion, and physical traits, and lets the caster share battle-magic with allies.

War Cry.description: The thaumaturge lets out a war cry, paints their face, or anoints themselves with blood, fortifying their spirit.
War Cry.effect: Courage increases by 1. When resisting hostile supernatural effects, Willpower is treated as 1 higher.
War Cry.limits: No more than once per scene.

True Strike.description: The thaumaturge makes a melee attack that lands without a check.
True Strike.effect: The attack is treated as successful with 1 success, with no Brawl or Melee roll needed.
True Strike.defense: The opponent may dodge, block, or parry as usual; they too only need 1 success.
True Strike.limits: Doesn't work if the pool is split across multiple actions.

Wind Dance.description: The thaumaturge moves with the ease of the night wind.
Wind Dance.effect: The character may dodge any number of attacks per turn.
Wind Dance.limits: If the character also wants to attack, they split their check pool as usual.

Brave Heart.description: The thaumaturge temporarily becomes a powerful fighter.
Brave Heart.effect: Strength, Dexterity, and Stamina increase by 1.
Brave Heart.limits: Cannot exceed the Generational cap. No more than once per scene.
Brave Heart.aftereffect: After the power ends, the character must rest for 2 hours. Until they do, they lose 1 Blood point every 15 minutes.

Battle Companion.description: The thaumaturge extends the Path of Mars's lesser powers to allies.
Battle Companion.activation: First a lesser power of the Path of Mars is activated, then the thaumaturge touches an ally and activates Battle Companion.
Battle Companion.effect: On a success, the chosen ally gains that power's effect.
Battle Companion.limits: The number of allies is limited by successes scored, the blood supply available, and the ability to touch the targets.

Path of the Father's Vengeance.description: A path based on interpreting the Book of Nod and the idea of Caine's punishment. Its powers only work on vampires, and require the victim to hear the relevant quotation, or read a passage written out in advance.
Path of the Father's Vengeance.limitations: Has no effect on mortals, ghouls, or other supernatural beings.

Litany of Zillah.description: The thaumaturge learns who the victim is tied to by sibling bonds or blood bonds.
Litany of Zillah.effect: If such ties exist, the power reveals the names of the bound vampires and gives a general idea of their appearance.

The Crone's Vanity.duration: until the end of the night
The Crone's Vanity.description: A curse of ugliness that strips the victim of social appeal.
The Crone's Vanity.effect: The victim's Looks drops to 0. Most social checks automatically fail, except attempts at intimidation.

Feast of Ashes.description: The victim loses the ability to digest blood, and can only feed on ash and cinders.
Feast of Ashes.effect: Any food or blood other than ash or cinders causes vomiting.
Feast of Ashes.ash_blood: Blood points gained from ash can only be used to wake from day-sleep.
Feast of Ashes.ash_amount: One Blood point equals roughly half a liter of ash or cinders.
Feast of Ashes.existing_blood: Ordinary blood already in reserve before the power was activated can be used normally.

Uriel's Disfavor.description: The victim becomes painfully vulnerable to light.
Uriel's Disfavor.effect: Even weak light causes discomfort. Bright artificial light aimed at the victim deals 1 aggravated wound per turn.

Final Word.description: Punishment for diablerie, temporarily returning the victim to their original Generation.
Final Word.effect: On a success, the victim returns to their original Generation, and their traits are reduced to that Generation's limits.
Final Word.aftereffect: After a week, the Generation and reduced traits return to their previous values.

Path of Corruption.description: A path of slowly distorting a victim's behavior, morality, and thinking. It doesn't control directly like Dominate, or press through emotion like Presence, but drags dark desires, addictions, discord, and spiritual submission to the surface.
Path of Corruption.special_limit: The Path of Corruption's rating cannot exceed the sorcerer's Subterfuge.

Temptation.description: The thaumaturge interferes with the victim's thoughts, pushing them toward the worse course of action.
Temptation.range: target within direct line of sight
Temptation.resistance: The victim rolls Awareness + Subterfuge at a difficulty of 2 + the thaumaturge's successes.

resistance_table.4 successes: The victim is thrown into confusion and does neither the original action nor the imposed one.
resistance_table.5 successes: The victim ignores the magic and does what they intended to.

Temptation.limits: Doesn't work in combat, and doesn't affect purely physical actions or reflexes.

The Fall.description: The victim gives in to their suppressed desires and secret passions.
The Fall.roll: an opposed activation check against the victim's Awareness + Subterfuge
The Fall.effect: The victim isn't required to act solely on the awakened desires, but whenever the opportunity arises, they must either give in to them or spend a Willpower point.

Discord.description: The thaumaturge breaks social bonds, making the victim suspicious, withdrawn, and estranged.
Discord.resistance: The victim makes a Willpower check at a difficulty equal to the thaumaturge's Manipulation + Empathy.
Discord.effect: If the sorcerer wins, the victim's pool for all social checks is reduced by 3d10.
Discord.brotherhood: If the victim is party to a brotherhood ritual or similar rite, the rating of the sibling bond is temporarily reduced by 3.

Craving.description: The thaumaturge instills in the victim a powerful psychological craving for a particular sensation, substance, emotion, or physical state.
Craving.requirement: The victim must be in the state they're meant to be bound to.
Craving.activation: The thaumaturge touches the victim. The victim resists with Awareness + Subterfuge at a difficulty of 3 + the sorcerer's successes.
Craving.effect: The victim must experience the craved sensation at least once a night. Each night of abstinence reduces their check pools by 1d10, cumulatively, down to a minimum of 1d10.
Craving.resist_temptation: To turn down a chance to get the desired sensation, the victim makes a Self-Control / Instinct check at difficulty 8.
Craving.extra_roll: Self-Control / Instinct (resisting temptation, difficulty 8); Self-Control / Instinct (extended recovery, difficulty equal to the thaumaturge's Manipulation + Subterfuge)
Craving.recovery: Each night, the victim may attempt an extended Self-Control / Instinct check at a difficulty equal to the thaumaturge's Manipulation + Subterfuge. They need three times as many successes as the thaumaturge scored on activation. If the victim relapses, the process starts over.

Dependency.description: The victim becomes spiritually dependent on the thaumaturge's presence and instructions.
Dependency.activation: Requires a conversation with the victim.
Dependency.resistance: The victim rolls Self-Control / Instinct at a difficulty of 3 + the sorcerer's successes.
Dependency.effect: If the victim isn't near the thaumaturge and isn't carrying out their order, the pool for all of their checks is reduced by 1d10.
Dependency.obedience: Orders given by the thaumaturge through Dominate, Presence, or other mental and emotional Disciplines are harder to resist: the victim's resistance pool is halved. The same applies to the thaumaturge's ordinary social checks.
Dependency.willpower: While away from the thaumaturge, a dependent victim cannot regain Willpower points.
Dependency.note: This power doesn't create love or loyalty, and doesn't stop the victim from attacking the thaumaturge.

Path of Technomancy.description: The youngest official Tremere path, created to control modern electronic devices, software, and networks. Conservative Tremere consider it almost sacrilegious, but its practical value is undeniable.

Analyze.description: The thaumaturge touches a device and gains a temporary understanding of its construction, purpose, and functions.

success_table.1 success: basic functions: turn on, turn off, the simplest use
success_table.3 successes: use at the level of a competent specialist
success_table.5 successes: the device's full range of functional capabilities

Analyze.software: Software can be analyzed too, but the activation difficulty increases by 2, and the program must be installed on the device the thaumaturge is touching.

Short Circuit.description: The thaumaturge causes a short circuit in an electronic device.
Short Circuit.difficulty_modifier: +1 to difficulty if the character isn't touching the device.

success_table.2 successes: noticeable problems; +1 to the difficulty of using it until the end of the scene
success_table.3 successes: the device breaks down until repaired
success_table.4 successes: the device breaks down, and even after repair always gives +1 to the difficulty of using it

Short Circuit.large_systems: Large, complex systems may raise the difficulty by 2–4.
Short Circuit.protected_systems: Protected systems may resist with a pool of 1–5d10 at difficulty 6.
Short Circuit.data: 3 successes erase the data on the storage medium; 5 successes make recovery possible only through magic.

Encryption/Decryption.description: The thaumaturge mystically protects a device or storage medium from unauthorized access, or breaks someone else's protection.
Encryption/Decryption.effect: Each success raises the difficulty of unauthorized access checks by 1.
Encryption/Decryption.dismiss: The thaumaturge may spend 1 Willpower point to end their own encryption.
Encryption/Decryption.decrypt: To break another thaumaturge's protection, the check is made at +1 difficulty. Each success removes one of the defender's successes.

Remote Access.description: The thaumaturge mentally connects to an electronic device within sight and uses it without touching it.
Remote Access.effect: Each success also lets the character apply 1 dot of a fitting ability to the check for interacting with the device.
Remote Access.shock: If the device is destroyed while connected, the thaumaturge suffers traumatic shock: 5 superficial wounds.

Direct Uplink.description: The thaumaturge projects their consciousness into a network and moves through it at the speed of data transfer.
Direct Uplink.requirement: Requires touching a device connected to the network.
Direct Uplink.duration: 5 minutes per success, or 10 minutes per success with an additional Willpower point spent
Direct Uplink.effect: While in the network, the character can apply Technomancy powers to connected devices.
Direct Uplink.shock: Losing the connection causes traumatic shock: 8 superficial wounds.
Direct Uplink.penalty: While the mind is in the network, the difficulty of Technomancy doesn't change, but the difficulty of all other Disciplines and abilities increases by 2.

Green Path.description: A path for controlling plant matter. It lets a thaumaturge speak with the spirits of plants, accelerate growth and decay, animate vines, create green shelters, and awaken giant trees.

Wisdom of the Grass.extra_roll: Intelligence + Occult (interpretation, at the Storyteller's discretion)
Wisdom of the Grass.description: The thaumaturge touches a plant and communicates with its spirit.

success_table.3 successes: a precise answer to a simple question
success_table.4 successes: a detailed answer to several complex questions
success_table.5 successes: the spirit's entire body of knowledge on the question asked

Wisdom of the Grass.interpretation: The Storyteller may require an Intelligence + Occult check for a correct interpretation.

Transience.description: The thaumaturge speeds up a plant's growth or decay.
Transience.effect: 1 success slightly speeds up or slows down growth. 3 successes noticeably grow or wither the plant. 5 successes turn a seed into a full-grown tree, or a full-grown tree into rotten mulch.
Transience.combat: In combat, 3 successes instantly ruin a wooden weapon, 2 successes noticeably weaken it, 5 successes destroy it.

Dance of the Vine.description: The thaumaturge animates a mass of plants roughly their own size and controls them.
Dance of the Vine.effect: The animated plants can grab, entangle, choke, write, move objects, or carry out simple actions.
Dance of the Vine.stats: The plants' Strength and Dexterity equal half the character's Willpower, rounded down. Their Brawl is 1 lower than the character's.
Dance of the Vine.limits: This power doesn't make a plant uproot itself from the ground.

Green Sanctuary.difficulty: the character's Willpower; more successes than the thaumaturge scored on activation are needed
Green Sanctuary.description: The thaumaturge creates a domed shelter of branches, leaves, grass, and climbing plants, about 2 meters in radius.
Green Sanctuary.requirement: Requires terrain with sufficiently dense vegetation.
Green Sanctuary.barrier: An unwanted guest must roll Wits + Survival at a difficulty equal to the character's Willpower, and score more successes than the thaumaturge did on activation.
Green Sanctuary.sun_protection: If activation scored more than 3 successes, the shelter protects against sunlight until it's destroyed.

Awaken the Tree Giant.description: The thaumaturge animates a tree, turning it into an enormous plant servant.
Awaken the Tree Giant.activation: Requires touching the tree and spending an additional 1 Blood point per success.
Awaken the Tree Giant.effect: The animated tree carries out the thaumaturge's verbal orders to the best of its ability.
Awaken the Tree Giant.stats: The tree's Dexterity is 2. Its Strength and Stamina equal the character's Thaumaturgy. Its Brawl equals the character's.
Awaken the Tree Giant.durability: The tree is immune to superficial damage, and the damage pool from aggravated wounds against it is halved.
Awaken the Tree Giant.aftereffect: When the power ends, the tree roots itself into the ground wherever it's standing, even if there's asphalt or concrete underneath.

Weather Control.description: An ancient path for controlling the weather. The early levels grant limited atmospheric changes, while the higher ones allow the caster to summon storms and lightning.

system.area: Usually a radius of up to 3 kilometers.
system.speed: 1 success — the weather change happens no sooner than a day later. 5 successes — an almost instant change.
system.weather_context: The current weather may lower or raise the difficulty by 1–2.
system.indoors: Indoors, difficulty increases by 2, and many effects become impossible.
system.extra_roll: Awareness + Occult (a lightning strike); Willpower (narrowing the area of effect)
system.difficulty: lightning: 6 in the open, 8 under shelter, 10 indoors near a window; area: 6
system.lightning_attack: To strike a specific target with lightning, an additional Awareness + Occult check is needed. Difficulty 6 in the open, 8 under shelter, 10 indoors near a window.
system.control_area: If the character wants to narrow the area of effect, they must declare it in advance. The Storyteller may require a Willpower check at difficulty 6.

Fog / Light Breeze / Minor Temperature C.effect: Fog gives +1 to the difficulty of hearing- and sight-based perception checks, and halves ranged attack distance. A light breeze gives +1 to the difficulty of smell-based checks. Temperature can be changed by 5°C.

Rain or Snow.effect: Like fog, but stronger: +2 to the difficulty of hearing- and sight-based perception checks, as well as Drive checks.

Strong Wind / Major Temperature Change.effect: Wind around 50 km/h, with gusts up to 100 km/h. +1 to the difficulty of firearms attacks, +2 for thrown weapons, bows, and crossbows. Temperature can be changed by 10°C.
Strong Wind / Major Temperature Change.stability: The Storyteller may require a Dexterity check at difficulty 6 for the character to keep their footing during gusts.

Storm.effect: The effects of rain and strong wind, simultaneously.

Lightning Strike.effect: Lightning can be used as a weapon. Armor offers no protection.

thaumaturgical_countermagic.description: Thaumaturgic Countermagic is a separate Discipline for resisting blood sorcery. It cannot be chosen as a primary path, grants no access to rituals, and is always considered an outside Discipline, even for the Tremere.

system.activation: In combat, activation counts as a free action.
system.requirement: The Thaumaturgic Countermagic rating must be at least as high as the level of the power or ritual the character is resisting.
system.roll: a pool equal to twice the Thaumaturgic Countermagic rating
system.effect: Each success cancels 1 of the opponent's successes.
system.scope: Works best against Thaumaturgy. Against Necromancy and other mystical vampiric Disciplines, the rating counts as half as much. Has no effect against the magic of non-vampire supernatural beings.
system.non_tremere: A non-Tremere Kindred who learns this Discipline gains clan Tremere as an enemy, with no compensating free dots.

1.effect: Can resist powers and rituals affecting the character directly, as well as their clothing and gear.

3.effect: Can resist powers and rituals affecting any object or being the character is touching.

5.effect: Can resist powers and rituals affecting any object or being within a radius (in meters) equal to the character's Willpower. If a sorcerer is within that radius, the character can resist their sorcery regardless of the actual target.

rituals.description: Thaumaturgic rituals are formalized magical rites, distinct from the paths' fast-acting powers. They require time, components, symbols, blood, grimoires, mentors, and access gained through the story.
rituals.levels: Rituals are divided into levels from 1 to 5. A ritual's level indicates both the minimum Thaumaturgy level needed to learn it, and roughly how powerful it is.
rituals.casting_time: Unless stated otherwise, casting a ritual takes 5 × the ritual's level, in minutes.
rituals.success: Usually 1 success is enough, but some rituals require more, or are strengthened by additional successes.
rituals.failure: On a failure, the ritual may simply not work, produce a strange side effect, or create a false sense of success.
rituals.botch: A botch often leads to catastrophic consequences, up to and including summoning hostile entities.
rituals.components: Special components are often required: herbs, ceremonial paraphernalia, animal parts, rare reagents, blood, symbols, and other materials.
rituals.learning: Upon gaining their first point of Thaumaturgy, the character receives one level-1 ritual. Other rituals are obtained through play, and are not purchased directly with experience.

Witchcraft.description: Witchcraft is an ancient sorcerous Discipline, requiring not just vitae and will but mastery of body, mind, and occult knowledge. It's divided into Ways, each tied to a single element and using its own base attribute.

system.theme: wind, water, earth, fire, ancient magic, the elements

activation.note: Uses the attribute's base value, without bonuses from blood or other Disciplines.

system.primary_way: Upon gaining their first point of Witchcraft, the character chooses a primary Way.

Way of Wind.description: Air, whispers, cold, sleep, flight, and storms.

Whisper of the Wind.effect: Each success carries an order or message lasting 1 turn. The recipient's reply returns on the wind within the next few turns.
Whisper of the Wind.limits: Doesn't work in sealed rooms; requires concentration.

Blizzard.effect: Victims in the area take 1 superficial wound per turn, -2d10 to Dexterity, and move at half speed. Lasts as long as the sorcerer concentrates.

Lethargic Breeze.effect: Every 2 turns, victims make a Stamina + Survival check at difficulty 8. A failure halves physical pools and speed until the end of the scene; a botch causes sleep or shallow torpor.

Wings of the Storm.effect: The sorcerer turns into a translucent gust of wind and flies toward a target under the open sky.
Wings of the Storm.limits: Doesn't work in caves, buildings, or enclosed spaces.

Thunderstorm.difficulty: 6 in the open; 8 under shelter; 10 indoors near a window
Thunderstorm.effect: A storm gathers after 6 hours, minus 1 hour per additional success. Rain, wind, thunder, possible flooding; mortals take 1 unavoidable superficial wound for every 5 minutes in the rain.
Thunderstorm.lightning: For 1 Willpower point and an Awareness + Occult check at difficulty 6, lightning strikes a target: 10 aggravated wounds, no more than once per turn.

Way of Water.description: Water, illusions over the surface, shelter, walking on water, elementals, and whirlpools.

Well of Lies.effect: Creates a three-dimensional, speaking, moving illusion above the surface of water within sight.
Well of Lies.duration: 1 turn per success; can be extended with a new activation at no blood cost, but at +1 difficulty.

Watery Shelter.effect: With 2+ successes, the character merges with the water as with Earth Meld.
Watery Shelter.requirement: A body of water at least 0.5 m deep and large enough for the body.

Sea Mist.duration: 1 hour, or a scene per success
Sea Mist.effect: The sorcerer walks on water as on dry land. Resurfacing after submerging requires a new activation.

Servants of the Deep.cost: 1 Blood point + 1 Willpower point + blood for the elementals
Servants of the Deep.effect: The character pours blood into a large body of water and summons a number of elementals no greater than the number of successes and Blood points spent.
Servants of the Deep.stats: All traits equal the sorcerer's Wits; Mental and Social traits are usually 1. Their skill is Stealth.
Servants of the Deep.rules: They take damage like vampires; the sun is lethal, fire deals superficial damage and is extinguished. Away from a body of water they take 1 aggravated wound per hour; in water they heal 1 wound per turn. At dawn they turn to water.

Fatal Depths.effect: Creates a whirlpool with a radius of 1.5 m, +1.5 m per additional success; strength 15, +5 per additional success.
Fatal Depths.escape: Strength + Survival against the whirlpool's strength, difficulty 8. On a loss, a mortal drowns; a supernatural victim is trapped until the end of the scene.

Way of Earth.description: Earth as womb, grave, healer, and destroyer.

Shifting Ground.effect: The earth binds the target for 2 turns, +2 per additional success. Breaking free requires an extended Strength + Survival check at difficulty 6, needing 5 successes.
Shifting Ground.attack: As an attack, deals 1 aggravated wound to the legs per success and immediately releases the target.

Strength of Stone.effect: +2 to Stamina; counts as natural Stamina and works for soak.

The Earth's Hungry Maw.effect: Each success immobilizes the target for 1 turn. Each turn underground, the target takes 1 aggravated wound, soak difficulty 7.
The Earth's Hungry Maw.escape: Strength + Survival at difficulty 8, requiring 5 successes; can be retried every turn.

Cradle of Life.effect: The earth absorbs the target and heals them: each success = 2 superficial wounds or 1 aggravated wound; 2 successes = 1 wound that risks death.
Cradle of Life.limit: Total healing is capped by the lesser of the two: blood spent, or successes scored.
Cradle of Life.time: superficial — 1 turn, aggravated — 1 minute, risk of death — 1 hour.

Kupala's Wrath.effect: An earthquake within sight, dealing 10 aggravated wounds to everyone in the area.

area_table.2 successes: half a dozen buildings or a small block
area_table.4 successes: several blocks or a massive building
area_table.5 successes: a city district or an industrial complex

Way of Fire.description: The heat of the depths, magma, fire, and resistance to the Red Terror.

Fiery Courage.effect: The difficulty of Courage checks against fire-induced Rötschreck is reduced by the Way of Fire's level. At difficulty 2 or lower, the character ignores the Red Terror entirely. Doesn't work against the sun.

Combustion.effect: Each success deals 1 aggravated wound. The attack can be dodged, but not blocked.
Combustion.difficulty_modifier: +2 to difficulty against living and non-living beings
Combustion.limit: No more than once per turn.

Wall of Magma.effect: Creates a three-meter wall of magma, usually forming a ring around the sorcerer.
Wall of Magma.fear: Anyone approaching makes a Courage check at difficulty 8.
Wall of Magma.damage: Approaching deals 1 aggravated wound from the heat; touching it deals 3 aggravated wounds, soak difficulty 9.
Wall of Magma.shape: Changing the wall's shape adds +1 to difficulty.

Heat Wave.effect: The victim takes 5 aggravated wounds. The vampire also loses 5 Blood points regardless of the damage absorbed.

Volcanic Eruption.duration: 1 turn, +1 per additional success; the lava spreads for the same duration
Volcanic Eruption.effect: A fountain of lava erupts within sight. Contact with the lava before it cools deals 3 aggravated wounds.
Volcanic Eruption.botch: On a botch, the eruption happens in the wrong place, possibly right under the sorcerer's feet.

Flight.description: Flight is the Gargoyles' natural Discipline, letting them use their wings with increasing power, maneuverability, and carrying capacity.

system.theme: wings, gliding, flight, maneuverability
system.note: Usually tied to physically having wings.

Glide.effect: The character can't fly properly, but can glide and slow a fall.

Running Start.effect: The character needs a running start to get off the ground and fly.

Free Ascent.effect: Unburdened, the character can climb vertically and fly with more confidence.

Burdened Flight.effect: The character can lift and carry up to 25 kg of load.

Free Aerial Combat.effect: While flying, the character maneuvers easily, carries a greater load, and can act effectively in the air.

Quietus.description: Quietus is the Assamite clan Discipline, the art of silent death, poisonous blood, and precise elimination of a target. Its powers involve silence, poisons, disease, control over vitae, internal destruction of the body, and turning blood into a weapon. Quietus doesn't always kill quickly: it often works in a way that conceals the very fact that an assassin was involved.

system.theme: killing, silence, poison, blood, ichor, disease, covert elimination
system.note: Many Quietus powers require spending blood or Willpower, touch, a successful hit, or prior contact with the victim. The Discipline is especially dangerous because its effects often look like illness, sudden weakness, internal bleeding, or natural death.

Silence of Death.description: The vampire creates a sphere of absolute silence around themselves, suppressing any sound made within it.
Silence of Death.effect: All sounds produced inside the sphere are completely muffled. Screams, footsteps, gunshots, blows, and speech cannot be heard outside it.
Silence of Death.limits: The sphere doesn't block sounds coming from outside. Those inside the Silence of Death hear external sounds as normal.

Scorpion's Touch.description: The vampire turns part of their own vitae into a powerful poison that weakens the victim's body.
Scorpion's Touch.poison_limit: The maximum number of Blood points that can be turned into poison equals the character's Stamina.
Scorpion's Touch.delivery: The poison can be delivered by touching bare skin, through a poisoned melee blade, through the lips, or by spitting.
Scorpion's Touch.effect: On a successful activation and hit, the victim's Stamina is reduced by a number of points equal to the Blood points turned into poison.
Scorpion's Touch.resistance: The victim may attempt a Stamina + Fortitude check at difficulty 6. Each success neutralizes one of the character's successes on activation.

duration_table.5+ successes: forever; lost Stamina points can be bought back with experience

Scorpion's Touch.mortal_effect: If a mortal's Stamina drops to 0, they fall gravely ill and lose their immunity to disease. If Stamina doesn't recover within a year, they die.
Scorpion's Touch.vampire_effect: If a vampire's Stamina drops to 0, they immediately fall into torpor until Stamina recovers. Permanent loss requires supernatural intervention.
Scorpion's Touch.poisoned_blood: While the power is active, a vampire trying to drink the character's blood drinks all of the stored poison first.

spit_attack.range: 3 meters per point of the character's Strength + Potence
spit_attack.limit: a single spit attack can deliver no more than 2 points of poison

Scorpion's Touch.immunity: An adept of Quietus is immune to their own poison, but other Kindred's poison affects them normally.

Dagon's Call.cost: 1 Willpower point per activation after the initial touch
Dagon's Call.difficulty: the opponent's Willpower, for each side
Dagon's Call.description: The vampire ruptures the victim's blood vessels from within, making them choke on their own blood and suffer from internal pressure.
Dagon's Call.activation: The character must first touch the victim. Within an hour of that, they may activate the power at a distance, without seeing or being near the victim.
Dagon's Call.effect: Each remaining success the character has deals the victim 1 aggravated wound.
Dagon's Call.repeat: On the next turn, the character may spend another Willpower point and repeat the opposed check. This can continue until the player stops, or the character runs out of Willpower.
Dagon's Call.stealth: From the outside, the power looks like sudden, agonizing pain, internal bleeding, or a death throe, which helps hide the assassin's involvement.

Baal's Caress.cost: 1 Blood point per 1 point of ichor
Baal's Caress.description: The vampire turns part of their vitae into a lethal ichor, capable of destroying living and non-living flesh alike.
Baal's Caress.application: The ichor can be applied to fingernails or to the blade of a melee weapon.
Baal's Caress.effect: Baal's Caress doesn't increase a weapon's damage, but turns the damage it deals into aggravated.
Baal's Caress.charges: Each successful hit that lands spends 1 point of ichor, even if the damage doesn't get through. If the attack misses, no ichor is spent.
Baal's Caress.tactics: Many assassins coat their weapon with several points of ichor at once, to preserve the effect across multiple hits.

Taste of Death.cost: a variable number of Blood points
Taste of Death.range: 3 meters per point of the character's Strength + Potence
Taste of Death.description: The vampire spews caustic ichor at a distance, burning flesh and eating away at the victim's bones.
Taste of Death.effect: Each Blood point spent on activation adds 2d10 to the damage pool on a hit. The damage is aggravated.
Taste of Death.limit: The number of Blood points is limited by the character's blood supply and the per-turn blood-spending limit.
Taste of Death.visual: Victims may be drenched in boiling streams of altered vitae, turning the body into decaying organic matter.

Blood Sweat.activation: 3 turns of concentration; the victim must be in direct line of sight
Blood Sweat.description: The vampire intensifies the victim's guilt, fear, or pride, making them bleed through their sweat glands.
Blood Sweat.effect: The victim loses 1 Blood point per success. In mortals, this causes damage as with the Kiss.
Blood Sweat.dead_blood: Blood that escapes through the skin is considered dead. A vampire who drinks it gains half as many Blood points as the victim lost. For the victim themselves, this blood is useless.
Blood Sweat.confession: The victim is overcome with remorse and guilt, if they have a conscience, or a desire to brag about their misdeeds, if they don't.
Blood Sweat.confession_scale: 1 success may make the victim confess to one misdeed; 5 successes makes them confess to many sins. The exact content is up to the Storyteller.
Blood Sweat.fear: The sight of bloody sweat can frighten witnesses, including vampires.

Selective Silence.description: The vampire mutes only chosen sounds, objects, or beings, bypassing the limitations of Silence of Death.
Selective Silence.effect: Each success fully mutes one individual or one object within range.
Selective Silence.limits: An object larger than a private jet, or a being larger than an adult elephant, cannot be muted.
Selective Silence.sound_scope: Only sounds produced directly by the target are muted. For example, a gunshot would be silent, but a supersonic bullet would still produce a crack.
Selective Silence.visual: Many vampires exhale a bloody mist that settles onto the muted targets.

Wave of Emotion.difficulty: usually 7; 5 if the mortal is already feeling the needed emotion; 9 if feeling the opposite
Wave of Emotion.description: The vampire poisons a mortal's blood with a chosen emotion. Any Kindred who drinks that blood feels the mixed-in emotion as their own.
Wave of Emotion.activation: The character must drink at least 1 Blood point from the mortal, then remain in physical contact with them for a minute while concentrating on the chosen emotion.
Wave of Emotion.limits: The victim's blood can only carry one emotion at a time. While the effect lasts, using Wave of Emotion again on the same victim automatically fails.
Wave of Emotion.drinker_resistance: A vampire who drinks the first point of poisoned blood makes a Self-Control / Instinct check at a difficulty equal to the mortal vessel's Willpower.

drinker_effect.1 Blood point: a brief surge of the emotion
drinker_effect.3+ Blood points: a complete change in emotional state

Wave of Emotion.duration_after_drinking: The emotion fades after a number of hours equal to the mortal victim's Willpower.
Wave of Emotion.mortal_effect: The mortal may not know they're affected, but becomes slightly inclined toward the mixed-in emotion.
Wave of Emotion.immunity: The poisoner is immune to their own emotional poison.

Cleansing.cost: Blood points equal to the victim's Willpower
Cleansing.roll: the victim's Willpower against each external supernatural effect
Cleansing.difficulty: 4 + the level of the power being removed; 7 if the level is unknown
Cleansing.description: The vampire cleanses another Kindred's spirit and mind of outside supernatural interference, anointing them with their own blood.
Cleansing.activation: The character places a hand on the victim's forehead, spends at least 5 minutes with them in deep concentration, and spends blood.
Cleansing.effect: The victim makes a separate Willpower check against each active outside effect. On a success, the corresponding effect ends.
Cleansing.works_against: Works against Dominate, Presence, mental disorders from Dementation, and some mental effects of Thaumaturgy.
Cleansing.thaumaturgy_limit: To remove a mental effect of Thaumaturgy, either the character or the victim must have Thaumaturgy at least equal to the level of the power being removed.
Cleansing.does_not_work_against: Doesn't remove blood bonds, the effects of drinking vitae, hereditary banes, ordinary persuasion, hypnosis, brainwashing, or natural emotions.
Cleansing.limits: The character cannot use Cleansing on themselves.

Bloody Claws of Baal.cost: a variable number of Blood points
Bloody Claws of Baal.description: The vampire creates an enhanced ichor that makes a blade weapon deal aggravated damage and gradually eats away at the blade itself.
Bloody Claws of Baal.activation: The character coats the blade of a melee weapon with their own blood.
Bloody Claws of Baal.effect: The weapon deals aggravated damage. The damage pool increases by a number of d10s equal to the sum of Blood points spent and successes on the check.
Bloody Claws of Baal.decay: Each turn, the additional damage pool decreases by 1d10, as the ichor evaporates and corrodes the blade. Once the bonus runs out, the weapon's base damage starts to decrease as well.
Bloody Claws of Baal.weapon_break: Once the damage rating drops to the character's Strength, the blade breaks after the very next hit.
Bloody Claws of Baal.resistance: Only blades created by supernatural powers at a level no lower than the character's Quietus can resist the destruction, at the Storyteller's discretion.
Bloody Claws of Baal.limits: The ichor cannot be saved for later. A few minutes after leaving the body, it becomes inert.

Poisoned Wellspring.description: The vampire turns a mortal's blood into poison that's lethal to any other Kindred.
Poisoned Wellspring.activation: The character touches the mortal victim, leaves a drop of their own vitae on their skin, then spends blood and makes the check.
Poisoned Wellspring.effect: Any vampire other than the character who drinks this victim's blood takes 2 aggravated wounds for every Blood point consumed.
Poisoned Wellspring.damage_flavor: The damage looks like a mix of acid burn and toxic shock.
Poisoned Wellspring.mortal_bonus: The mortal gains +2d10 to Stamina checks against poisons and acids.
Poisoned Wellspring.mortal_penalty: The mortal's bodily secretions begin to smell stronger. Their social check pool is reduced by 1d10 if the other party is close by or has a keen sense of smell.
Poisoned Wellspring.use: This power is well suited to protecting a herd, or turning a vessel into a trap for another vampire.

Song of Another's Vitae.roll: Wits + Intimidation against the victim's Willpower
Song of Another's Vitae.extra_roll: consequences per table: Self-Control / Instinct (difficulty 8 or 9); Courage (difficulty 8 or 9); Willpower (difficulty 9)
Song of Another's Vitae.description: The vampire revives the imprints of fear, pain, and personality in every drop of blood the victim has ever drunk, forcing them to relive the nightmares of their own vessels.
Song of Another's Vitae.activation: The character touches the victim, spends 1 turn focusing on the currents of their vitae, then spends blood and makes an opposed check.
Song of Another's Vitae.diablerie_bonus: If the victim committed diablerie no more than a number of days ago equal to the character's Awareness, the character gains 1 additional success.
Song of Another's Vitae.blood_loss: The victim loses Blood points equal to the character's successes. Blood seeps from their pores and natural openings, but no additional damage occurs.
Song of Another's Vitae.emotion_scope: All of the visions are tied to fear, pain, and negative emotion. Even memories of the Kiss feel like terror rather than pleasure.

result_table.failure: The character themselves experiences the sensations of the last vessel the victim gave the Kiss to. If they roll more than three 1s, they gain a permanent derangement related to drinking blood.
result_table.no_effect: The victim feels nothing unusual, and gains immunity to this power for a number of days equal to their own Willpower.
result_table.1 success: The victim experiences 10 seconds, or 3 turns, of the last vessel's sensations. While the effect lasts, they take +2 to the difficulty of all checks.
result_table.2 successes: The victim experiences 15 seconds, or 5 turns, of the sensations of several recent vessels. During the effect they take +3 to the difficulty of all checks; afterward, +1 until they pass a Self-Control / Instinct check at difficulty 8, no more than once per scene.
result_table.3 successes: The victim experiences a false memory drawn from their own fears and the sensations of recent vessels. They make a Courage check at difficulty 8 or fall into Rötschreck. Even on a success, they take +3 to the difficulty of all checks until the end of the scene.
result_table.4 successes: The victim cannot act for 8 minus Self-Control / Instinct turns. Afterward they make a Courage check at difficulty 9 or fall into Rötschreck. If they do, they then make a Self-Control / Instinct check at difficulty 8; on a failure, they gain the 'blood animism' derangement.
result_table.5 successes: The victim drowns in the nightmarish fears of everyone they've ever given the Kiss to. They make a Self-Control / Instinct check at difficulty 9. On a failure, they fall into torpor for 10 minus Stamina nights, after which their Willpower is halved and they gain 'blood animism.' On a success, they fall into Rötschreck until the end of the night and fear their own reflection. At the end of the night they make a Willpower check at difficulty 9; on a failure, Willpower drops by 1 and 'blood animism' appears.

Sins of the Father.cost: 10 Blood points + a 1-point reduction to Willpower
Sins of the Father.difficulty: 4 + the number of descendant Generations, maximum 10
Sins of the Father.description: The vampire extends the effect of any successfully applied Quietus power onto the descendants of a chosen victim.
Sins of the Father.activation: The character must first successfully apply any Quietus power to the chosen victim. They then pay the cost and make the check.
Sins of the Father.effect: On a success, all of the victim's descendants, across the chosen number of Generations, experience the effect of the same power.
Sins of the Father.resistance: If the original power involves a resistance check, each descendant makes it separately, using their own traits.
Sins of the Father.exclusions: The character may exclude certain descendants: no more than two individuals per point of Wits. To exclude someone, the character must either have tasted their vitae or know them by sight.
Sins of the Father.scope: This power turns a single, targeted killing or curse into punishment for an entire vampiric bloodline.

Animalism.description: Animalism is power over animals and over the inner Beast. With it, a vampire senses predatory intent, talks with animals, makes a beast their famulus, sends packs to scout or attack, and interferes with the frenzy of other Kindred. This Discipline is especially useful outside human society: it helps with hunting, hiding, watching enemies, and using urban or wild fauna as a network of eyes, ears, and teeth.

system.limitations: usually only works on vertebrate animals; herbivores usually raise the difficulty by +1; animals don't become smarter and won't carry out suicidal or unnatural orders without serious pressure

Sense the Beast.description: The vampire senses another's Beast: aggression, hunger, supernatural nature, and immediate threat.
Sense the Beast.effect: Winning the contest tells the character whether the target is hostile, and whether it's a mortal, a vampire, or some other supernatural being. A triumph reveals the exact type of creature, its Hunger or equivalent trait, and its resonance. The power can also passively warn of nearby danger.

Bond Famulus.description: The vampire feeds an animal its vitae for three nights, making it a permanent companion bound by the Blood.
Bond Famulus.cost: feeding vitae for 3 nights (with Blood trials)
Bond Famulus.effect: The famulus understands simple commands, defends itself and its master, responds better to other Animalism powers, and doesn't age as long as it feeds regularly. Only one famulus can be bound at a time; the bond is usually broken only by death.

Feral Whispers.description: Lets the character speak with animals and summon beasts of a single species, if any are nearby.
Feral Whispers.effect: Ordinary conversation requires no check. To convince an animal to do something risky or difficult, use Manipulation + Animalism; to summon beasts, use Charisma + Animalism, with difficulty based on the species' rarity. Animals remain animals: they don't grasp abstractions and dislike suicidal tasks.

Animal Succulence.description: Animal blood nourishes the vampire significantly better, and a famulus can be sacrificed for a powerful surge of strength.
Animal Succulence.effect: Animal blood removes 1 more point of Hunger than usual, and counts as suitable even for more potent Blood. Consuming a famulus removes 4 Hunger, but at least 1 Hunger always remains, and temporarily raises a fitting trait by 2 until the next feeding or until Hunger 5.

Quell the Beast.description: The vampire locks eyes with a target and lulls their Beast to sleep.
Quell the Beast.effect: The mortal becomes nearly listless and acts only in self-defense. A vampire under the effect gets no blood triumphs and cannot use a Blood Surge; a triumph on the roll can even halt an ongoing frenzy.

Carrion Hive.description: An amalgam with Obfuscate 2: the vampire controls a swarm of insects as a single creature, sometimes hiding it within their own body.
Carrion Hive.effect: The swarm can be made a famulus, and used for surveillance, distraction, and intimidation. It's weak in combat, but hampers a target: anyone surrounded by the swarm suffers a 2d10 penalty to actions; for intimidating mortals, the swarm can add 1–3d10.

Subsume the Spirit.description: The vampire's consciousness migrates into an animal's body, letting them see, hear, and act through it.
Subsume the Spirit.effect: The vampire's own body remains motionless and defenseless. Within the animal's body, the character can move and perceive the world; attempting to act during the day requires resisting sleep. Injury to the vampire's body, the animal's death, or sun-induced terror can interrupt the effect.
Subsume the Spirit.duration: 1 scene, or longer on a triumph

Drawing Out the Beast.description: During a powerless or feral frenzy, the vampire transfers the fit onto another creature nearby.
Drawing Out the Beast.effect: Winning the contest sends the target into the corresponding frenzy instead of the vampire. This doesn't protect against future fits in the same scene, and doesn't work against hunger frenzy.
Drawing Out the Beast.duration: as long as the frenzy lasts

Animal Dominion.description: The vampire commands animals already present as a pack, making them act in concert.
Animal Dominion.effect: Choose a species of animal nearby and give an order. Difficulty depends on the beasts' nature and the danger of the task: scouting is easier, attacking a vampire is harder. The power doesn't summon new animals — it commands those already close by.
Animal Dominion.duration: until the order is carried out (max. 1 scene)

Chimerstry.description: Chimerstry is the clan Discipline of the Ravnos, the art of supernatural illusion, phantoms, and deceived perception. It lets a vampire's thoughts take shape as illusory images capable of fooling not just the senses, but modern electronics too. Chimerstry even works on vampires, and the only ones usually able to effectively resist it are those who possess Auspex.

system.theme: illusion, deceived senses, phantoms, nightmares, distorted reality, maya
system.core_rule: Chimerstry illusions can be dispelled if at least one victim proves their illusory nature — for example, by walking through an illusory wall and openly showing that it's immaterial.
system.implausible_illusions: A blatantly implausible illusion can be dispelled by simple doubt, unless it's reinforced by a senior power.
system.familiarity: The vampire must be familiar with whatever they're creating an illusion of. Simple things can be imagined freely, but precisely imitating someone's voice, face, a photograph, or a unique object requires knowing the original.
system.mental_risk: Victims subjected to Chimerstry too often may begin doubting the reality of everything around them, which can lead to mental disorders.

Phantasm.description: The vampire creates a small, motionless illusion affecting only a single sense: smell, sound, sight, taste, or touch.
Phantasm.effect: The character could create, for example, a cloud of sulfurous odor, a bleeding stigma, the sound of breaking glass, a cold touch, or a small visual image.
Phantasm.volume: The volume of an illusory smell, light, smoke, or similar effect is limited to half a cubic meter per point of Chimerstry.
Phantasm.duration: The illusion persists as long as the character keeps it in view, until they dispel it themselves, or until someone proves its illusory nature.
Phantasm.limits: A tactile illusion remains immaterial. An invisible wall won't stop anyone, and an illusory rope can't strangle anyone.

Fata Morgana.description: The vampire creates a motionless illusion that affects every sense.
Fata Morgana.effect: A filthy basement can be turned into a luxurious ballroom, an ordinary room into a crypt, and a bucket of water into a bucket of champagne.
Fata Morgana.duration: Maintained and dispelled the same way as Phantasm.
Fata Morgana.limits: The illusion remains immaterial and motionless. Fata Morgana can change the appearance of a space, but doesn't create moving creatures or active events without Animate.

Animate.extra_roll: Willpower (concentration during an action that requires a check)
Animate.description: This power brings illusions created by Phantasm or Fata Morgana to life, letting them move.
Animate.effect: The vampire can create one complex animation, or many small movements: a sneaking burglar, running water, swaying curtains, roaring flame, flickering lanterns, or trash blown by the wind.
Animate.concentration: Controlling the animation requires focus. If the character performs an action requiring a check, they must first pass a Willpower check. On a failure, the illusion dissipates.
Animate.programming: If the character voluntarily stops concentrating, they can program the illusion with one simple, repetitive action described in a single sentence.
Animate.limits: Once concentration ends, the character can no longer take that same illusion back under active control. They may leave it as is, dispel it, or create it anew.
Animate.duration: Animated Phantasms and Fata Morganas are maintained and dispelled like ordinary illusions.

Permanency.description: The vampire makes a created illusion permanent, letting it exist outside their field of view.
Permanency.effect: Permanency can be applied to a Phantasm, a Fata Morgana, or an animated illusion.
Permanency.duration: The illusion persists even after the character leaves the scene.
Permanency.dismiss: The character can dispel a permanent illusion they created at any time.
Permanency.use: The Ravnos often use this power to turn temporary havens into luxurious dwellings, or to scare off uninvited guests with illusory guard dogs.

Horrid Reality.duration: until the end of the scene
Horrid Reality.description: The vampire implants a hallucination directly into a single victim's mind, making them genuinely believe in the reality of what's happening.
Horrid Reality.effect: Imaginary fire burns, a rope chokes, a wall blocks passage. To other observers, it's just an ordinary illusion, but their dispelling it doesn't help the victim themselves.
Horrid Reality.damage: If the character wants to cause harm, each success on the check inflicts 1 unavoidable aggravated wound on the victim. At the character's discretion, the damage can be capped in advance or made superficial.
Horrid Reality.side_effects: Some hallucinations may trigger additional consequences: for example, imaginary fire could provoke Rötschreck.
Horrid Reality.disbelief: To convince the victim the nightmare isn't real requires scoring 2 successes on a Charisma + Empathy check, at a difficulty equal to the illusion creator's Manipulation + Subterfuge.
Horrid Reality.healing: If the victim is convinced within a day of taking the damage, all damage from the power vanishes. Afterward, it must be healed like ordinary damage.
Horrid Reality.limit: Horrid Reality cannot kill the victim by itself. A mortal at the point of death loses consciousness instead; a vampire falls into torpor.

False Echo.description: The vampire layers a false aura, simple thoughts, emotions, Nature, and a psychometric trace onto an illusion.
False Echo.activation: At the character's discretion, it triggers automatically whenever a Chimerstry illusion is created.
False Echo.effect: If an opponent uses Auspex, Eye of Chaos, or another psychic power and scores fewer than 6 successes, they perceive the illusion as having thoughts, feelings, a Nature, and other signs of a real being.
False Echo.aura: The false aura reflects basic emotions: anger, sorrow, hatred, love, happiness, and similar simple states.
False Echo.thoughts: The illusion's thoughts are simple but plausible, and fit what's happening around it.
False Echo.psychometry: The illusion's psychometric trace persists until dawn.

Mastery.description: The Ravnos sheds the usual restrictions on Phantasm, Fata Morgana, and Animate.
Mastery.effect: The character no longer spends blood or Willpower to create and animate Phantasms and Fata Morganas.
Mastery.concentration: The character no longer needs to maintain full concentration to control animated illusions, as long as their number doesn't exceed their Intelligence.
Mastery.range: The character can sustain their illusions outside their field of view, up to a distance of 1.5 × their Willpower in kilometers.
Mastery.limits: If the character can't see what's happening around their illusions, they can't make them react appropriately to the situation.

Shared Nightmare.cost: 2 Willpower points + 1 Blood point per chosen victim
Shared Nightmare.description: The vampire extends Horrid Reality, letting a single terrifying hallucination harm several victims at once.
Shared Nightmare.effect: The character makes one check, and its result is applied to each chosen victim separately.
Shared Nightmare.damage: Damage and other effects work as with Horrid Reality.

Remote Illusion.description: The vampire projects illusions into an area they can see, or can picture accurately enough.
Remote Illusion.effect: The character can create illusions in a place where they aren't physically present, as long as they can attune to that area.

familiarity_difficulty.6: Familiar with the place as their own haven, or seeing it through Clairvoyance / Psychic Projection.
familiarity_difficulty.7: Has visited the place several times.
familiarity_difficulty.8: Has visited the place once or twice, or is viewing it through video surveillance.
familiarity_difficulty.9: Knows the place only from descriptions, video, or unprocessed photographs.

Remote Illusion.limits: In most cases the character needs to have visited the target location at least once, though a description, photo, or video may also suffice.

Veil of Disbelief.description: The vampire makes victims subconsciously accept an incredible illusion as truth, or, conversely, makes them dismiss a real event as a clever trick and quickly forget it.
Veil of Disbelief.effect: The illusion is perceived as believable even when it's completely unrealistic. If the power is used in reverse, victims regard reality itself as an illusion or a trick.
Veil of Disbelief.memory: Once the image vanishes from sight, memories of it fade quickly, and the victim may sincerely deny having seen anything unusual.
Veil of Disbelief.auspex: Only a victim with sufficiently high Auspex can see through the power's effect and tell truth from falsehood.

Synesthesia.cost: 1 Willpower point against a single victim
Synesthesia.difficulty: the victim's Willpower for a single target; 7 against a crowd
Synesthesia.description: The vampire scrambles the senses of a victim, or of an entire crowd, swapping sight, hearing, smell, pain, pleasure, touch, and other channels of perception.
Synesthesia.single_target: Against a single victim, the character decides exactly how to distort their perception: swapping senses, replacing pain with pleasure, making them hear color or see sound.
Synesthesia.crowd_effect: If the power is used against a crowd, it affects everyone within the vampire's field of view. The victims' senses are scrambled at random.
Synesthesia.crowd_penalty: Every victim's Awareness is reduced by a number of points equal to the character's successes.
Synesthesia.zero_perception: Victims whose Awareness drops to 0 can only remain helplessly in place, until their senses return to normal at sunrise.

Mayaparisatya.cost: 10 Blood points + a 1-point reduction to Willpower
Mayaparisatya.difficulty: 6 for an inanimate object; the victim's Willpower for a person
Mayaparisatya.range: several kilometers, as long as the character knows the target exists
Mayaparisatya.description: The vampire alters, creates, or erases material objects and living beings, turning illusion into a brief rewrite of reality.
Mayaparisatya.scope: It can affect any object or victim known to the character within a radius of several kilometers. The number of living victims that can be affected at once equals the character's Willpower.
Mayaparisatya.remote_use: It can be combined with Remote Illusion, but only on a mass or area basis; a precise, single-target effect can't be achieved this way.
Mayaparisatya.duration: Unless stated otherwise, changes persist until the end of the scene.

object_effects_table.1 success: Disable an object: dull swords, jam a weapon, create a large cloud of thick smoke.
object_effects_table.2 successes: Turn an object into another object: for example, candles into spiders.
object_effects_table.3 successes: Make a solid object immaterial, or the reverse: a door into mist, fog into marble.
object_effects_table.4 successes: Radically alter an object's properties: for example, make bricks flammable.
object_effects_table.5 successes: Radically alter the properties of the environment: reverse gravity, make water still and the shore fluid.
object_effects_table.6+ successes: Erase an object from reality. This is the only permanent effect.

Mayaparisatya.living_targets: If the power is used on a living victim, the described changes affect their entire body.
Mayaparisatya.erasure_damage: If the character tries to erase a victim from reality, each success deals 2 unavoidable aggravated wounds and reduces the victim's Strength and Stamina by 1.
Mayaparisatya.recovery: The damage heals normally. Strength and Stamina return to normal at the end of the scene.
Mayaparisatya.visual: Until the damage heals, victims of Mayaparisatya look like faint, blurred reflections of themselves.

Serpentis.description: Serpentis is Set's own legacy and a sacred gift to his children. This Discipline embodies serpentine dread, hypnosis, venom, bodily transformation, and mystical dominion over the image of an ancient god. The Followers of Set jealously guard its secrets and teach Serpentis only to the most deserving.

system.theme: serpents, hypnosis, venom, the heart, divine form, the darkness of Set
system.note: Many Serpentis powers are tied to serpentine traits: the gaze, the tongue, scales, venom, and transformation into a cobra. The senior powers involve the heart, a divine body, and ancient dark forms.

Eyes of the Serpent.roll: not required against mortals; Willpower against supernatural beings
Eyes of the Serpent.description: The vampire's gaze turns serpentine: the iris turns amber-yellow, the pupils widen, and a victim who meets their eyes freezes in place.
Eyes of the Serpent.activation: The victim must literally look directly into the character's eyes.
Eyes of the Serpent.effect: Mortals freeze with no check. Vampires and other supernatural beings are only affected if the character succeeds on a Willpower check at difficulty 9.
Eyes of the Serpent.escape: Vampires and other supernatural beings may spend a Willpower point to break free, but only if they're being attacked or someone is trying to harm them.
Eyes of the Serpent.note: This power differs from the normal eye-contact rules: the victim must be looking directly into the character's eyes.

Tongue of the Asp.description: The vampire's tongue lengthens and splits, coming to resemble the tongue of a giant snake. It reaches about half a meter and can serve as a weapon.
Tongue of the Asp.feeding: If a strike with the tongue deals damage, on the next turn the vampire may begin drinking blood as though they had sunk their fangs into the victim's neck.
Tongue of the Asp.kiss: A touch of the tongue counts as the Kiss, and paralyzes a mortal victim with a mix of fear and pleasure.
Tongue of the Asp.senses: The tongue is sensitive to vibration. By waving it through the air, the vampire halves penalties related to darkness.

Skin of the Adder.description: The vampire's skin becomes covered in tough, mottled scales, the body becomes supple and flexible, the mouth widens, and the fangs lengthen.
Skin of the Adder.effect: The difficulty of soak checks is reduced to 5.
Skin of the Adder.aggravated_resistance: The character may use Stamina on soak checks against aggravated damage from claws and fangs, but not from fire, sunlight, or supernatural energies.
Skin of the Adder.bite: The bite damage pool increases by 1d10.
Skin of the Adder.movement: The vampire can squeeze through any opening at least as large as their own head.
Skin of the Adder.appearance: Looks drops to 1. Any attentive observer will realize they aren't looking at a human, unless the character is hiding in shadow or under heavy clothing.

Form of the Cobra.duration: until dawn or until the character ends the power
Form of the Cobra.description: The vampire transforms into a giant black cobra about three meters long, weighing the same as their normal body.
Form of the Cobra.equipment: Clothing and personal items transform along with the character.
Form of the Cobra.senses: The Storyteller may increase the pool for perception checks related to smell. The pool for perception checks related to hearing is reduced by 2d10.
Form of the Cobra.bite: The cobra's bite deals the same damage as the vampire's ordinary bite, but the character doesn't need to grapple first.
Form of the Cobra.poison: The cobra's venom is lethal to mortals.
Form of the Cobra.disciplines: In cobra form, the vampire can use any Discipline except those that require hands.
Form of the Cobra.movement: The cobra's form lets the vampire reach hard-to-access places.

Heart of Darkness.roll: no check required for the character's own heart
Heart of Darkness.description: The vampire removes their own heart from their chest without harm, and places it in a vessel to hide or bury.
Heart of Darkness.other_vampires: The power can be used on another vampire, but this requires an hours-long surgical ritual performed on the night of the new moon.
Heart of Darkness.fear: Witnesses to the heart's removal make a Courage check. On a failure, they may suffer horror, revulsion, or Rötschreck.
Heart of Darkness.benefits: A heartless vampire cannot be paralyzed by a wooden stake. The difficulty of frenzy resistance checks is reduced by 2.
Heart of Darkness.heart_vulnerability: If someone bites the removed heart, the former owner takes aggravated damage. If the heart is pierced with a wooden stake, the owner immediately falls into torpor.
Heart of Darkness.destruction: The heart can be destroyed by fire or sunlight. In that case, the former owner crumbles into ash and charred bone within moments.
Heart of Darkness.risk: If an enemy gets hold of the heart, the vampire is completely at their mercy. Because of this, many carry their heart with them, hide it, or create decoy hearts.

Fangs of the Cobra.description: The vampire grows hollow, venomous fangs without transforming into a snake.
Fangs of the Cobra.activation: The fangs sharpen, become hollow, and fill with venom.
Fangs of the Cobra.attack: To inject the venom, the victim must be grappled and bitten. The bite itself deals normal damage.
Fangs of the Cobra.mortal_effect: A mortal who receives the venom can die within a minute.
Fangs of the Cobra.supernatural_effect: Vampires and other supernatural beings take 10 minus the victim's Stamina + Fortitude in aggravated damage over five minutes.
Fangs of the Cobra.risk: Death by snake venom may draw the attention of the police or other investigators.

Form of Divinity.duration: until the end of the scene
Form of Divinity.description: A Setite elder takes on the physical form of one of Set's divine offspring: Set, Sobek, Wepwawet, Renenutet, or Taweret.
Form of Divinity.effect: Strength and Stamina increase by 2. Charisma and Manipulation increase by 1. Willpower increases by 2, up to a maximum of 10.
Form of Divinity.generation_limit: This power may allow the character to exceed the trait limit set by Generation.
Form of Divinity.form_choice: Unless the character acquires this power again as a separate senior power, they're limited to a single divine form.
Form of Divinity.lore: The character doesn't know whose patronage they'll receive until they first take on the Form of Divinity; the player may choose the deity.

Theft of the Heart.roll: Dexterity + Brawl against the victim's Fortitude
Theft of the Heart.description: The vampire tears another vampire's heart out in a single swift strike, gaining power over them without the lengthy ritual of Heart of Darkness.
Theft of the Heart.effect: If the character ends up with at least 3 successes, they extract the heart from the victim's chest.
Theft of the Heart.damage: The victim takes 1 unavoidable point of aggravated damage.
Theft of the Heart.consequences: The victim is subject to the effects of Heart of Darkness: immunity to stake paralysis without the heart, vulnerability through the removed heart, torpor if the heart is staked, and final death if the heart is destroyed.
Theft of the Heart.note: The victim doesn't die immediately, as long as the stolen heart isn't destroyed.

Shadow of Apep.duration: until the end of the scene, or until the character ends the power
Shadow of Apep.description: The vampire takes on the form of Apep: a giant serpent of flickering darkness, anti-light, and living void.
Shadow of Apep.immunity: While the power is active, the character takes no damage from physical attacks. Bullets, blades, claws, fangs, explosions, and debris pass through them like through a shadow.
Shadow of Apep.weakness: Fire, sunlight, and mystical powers work as normal.
Shadow of Apep.attributes: Every Physical attribute increases by 3. This power may allow the character to exceed the trait limit set by Generation.
Shadow of Apep.movement: The body of darkness can seep through the smallest cracks, fissures, and openings.
Shadow of Apep.combat: The character retains the ability to physically attack with Potence-like force. A bite deals Strength + 2 aggravated damage.
Shadow of Apep.disciplines: The vampire can use any Discipline except those that require hands.
Shadow of Apep.lore: It's said that only Set and his direct descendants could ever take on the form of Apep, Set's defeated enemy.

Presence.description: Presence controls emotions, not thoughts. The vampire becomes irresistible, frightening, sacred, or nightmarish in the eyes of others: people want to listen to them, want to be liked by them, want to flee from them, or want to kneel before them. The power doesn't turn the victim into a puppet, but it changes their feelings enough that they often find their own justification for what they do. Presence works well for hunting, negotiation, seduction, intimidation, and crowd control.

system.limitations: the target must be able to perceive the vampire nearby, usually see or hear them; ordinary recordings, phones, and cameras don't carry the effect without special powers; multiple Presence effects on one target don't stack

Awe.description: The vampire becomes magnetic and persuasive: people are more willing to listen to them and accept their position.
Awe.effect: Adds Presence to Persuasion, Performance, and sometimes Charisma-based checks. Anyone who realizes they're being influenced may resist; a success lets them ignore Awe for the scene, a triumph until the end of the night.

Daunt.description: Instead of attractiveness, the vampire radiates an aura of danger that makes approaching them frightening.
Daunt.effect: Adds Presence to Intimidation. Anyone who wants to attack the vampire must first pass a Resolve + Composure check at difficulty 2. Daunt and Awe cannot be active at the same time.

Lingering Kiss.description: The vampire's kiss becomes addictively desirable, and the victim starts craving another feeding.
Lingering Kiss.effect: After a successful feeding, the victim gains a temporary bonus to one Social attribute, but then suffers a hangover and penalties to any action not aimed at getting another Kiss. The power can't be used on someone bound to the vampire as a vassal, or on someone immune to bonds.
Lingering Kiss.duration: effect and penalty: 1 night × Presence rating

Entrancement.description: The vampire focuses Presence on a single target, causing infatuation, devotion, or cult-like reverence.
Entrancement.effect: While the effect holds, the target tries to please the vampire, who adds Presence to social checks against them. Requests for clear harm to the target, their loved ones, or their principles break the effect, unless the vampire immediately renews it.

Dread Gaze.description: The vampire briefly reveals their predatory nature and unleashes pure terror on the victim.
Dread Gaze.effect: Mortals flee, freeze, or lose a turn depending on the result. Vampires don't flee like mortals, but may lose actions, spend Willpower, or have to check against powerless frenzy if the vampire scores a triumph.

Summon.description: The vampire calls to someone they've previously affected with Presence, or who has tasted their Blood.
Summon.effect: The target understands who is calling and where they are, and feels compelled to come. A normal win creates a summons for the night; a triumph makes the target set out immediately, unless it would mean death or total ruin.

Irresistible Voice.description: An amalgam with Dominate 1: the vampire's voice alone becomes a sufficient conduit for Dominate.
Irresistible Voice.effect: Dominate powers can be used without eye contact, as long as the target hears the voice directly. The power doesn't carry over phone, television, an intercom, or a recording.

Star Magnetism.description: Presence starts working through live broadcast and live sound, but not through recordings.
Star Magnetism.effect: Lets the vampire use Awe, Daunt, and Entrancement over a live video or audio feed. Entrancement requires naming a specific target; other viewers simply find the vampire impressive.

Majesty.description: The vampire becomes an icon of beauty, terror, or power, one that's hard even to argue with.
Majesty.effect: Onlookers can only watch, avert their eyes, or submit to the mood of the scene. To act against the vampire's will, other than in self-defense, a target must win a contest; a triumph frees them until the end of the scene.

Dominate.description: Dominate is direct violence against the will. The vampire catches the victim's gaze, gives an order, erases memories, or implants a command into their mind. It's one of the chief tools of the Masquerade: a witness can forget a feeding, a guard can open a door, a servant can carry out an errand and sincerely believe it was their own idea. But the Discipline is literal-minded: it works poorly with vague orders, doesn't extract information on its own, and usually can't force a target to do something physically impossible.

system.resonance: phlegmatic (the dominant and the submissive, leaders and followers)
system.limitations: usually requires eye contact, a voice, and a language the target understands; lethally dangerous orders are impossible without a level 5 power; vampires and resisting targets require a contest, and stronger Blood may protect against the effect

Compel.description: A short command like "stop," "run," "be quiet," or "give it here" is carried out literally and immediately.
Compel.effect: An unsuspecting mortal usually doesn't resist at all. Vampires, alert people, a repeat use in the same scene, or an order against the target's nature require a contest. Ambiguous commands are interpreted by the Storyteller, often unpleasantly for the vampire.

Cloud Memory.description: The command "forget" erases the present moment and the last few minutes.
Cloud Memory.effect: Good for covering up a feeding, a chance encounter, or a brief Masquerade breach. What's left is a gap in memory, not a new version of events; under pressure, the victim may realize they're missing something.

Mesmerize.description: A longer order with an explanation of the task, which the target begins carrying out immediately.
Mesmerize.effect: The instruction can't require independent judgment calls or complex reasoning. An unsuspecting mortal usually obeys with no roll; resistance, vampiric nature, or acting against character requires a contest.

Dementation.description: An amalgam with Obfuscate 2: an ordinary conversation turns into a quiet attack on the psyche.
Dementation.effect: For the length of the scene, the conversation counts as a social conflict in which the vampire deals superficial Stress each turn. An exhausted mortal suffers a breakdown or derangement; a Kindred gets an imposed compulsion.

Submerged Directive.description: Mesmerize gains a hidden trigger: a phrase, a date, meeting a particular person, or an event.
Submerged Directive.effect: The directive can lie dormant for years until the condition is met. Only one such command can exist on a given target at a time; it's convenient for the Storyteller to roll the check secretly, so the vampire doesn't know whether the planted trigger fired.

The Forgetful Mind.description: The vampire rewrites the target's memories, narrating a new version of events out loud.
The Forgetful Mind.effect: Each margin of success replaces one memory or fragment. A normal success creates plausible but imperfect memories; careful interrogation may reveal the seams. A triumph makes the substitution nearly indistinguishable from a real memory.

Rationalize.description: The victim rationalizes actions taken under Dominate and comes to see them as their own decision.
Rationalize.effect: Even absurd acts get an internal justification. If others pressure the victim and point out the contradictions, they may have to roll a check to start doubting their own logic — and possibly their sanity.

Terminal Decree.description: The instinct for self-preservation no longer blocks orders that lead to injury or death.
Terminal Decree.cost: none (but risks a loss of Humanity)
Terminal Decree.effect: Mortals and vampires still resist according to the rules of the power being used, but suicidal content no longer automatically cancels the command. Using this is almost always morally dangerous to Humanity.

Mass Manipulation.description: Dominate extends to a crowd, a group of servants, or several targets at once.
Mass Manipulation.effect: All targets must see the vampire's eyes. If resistance is required, the strongest member of the group rolls on behalf of all of them. Duration and the core effect match whichever Dominate power is being extended.

Celerity.description: Celerity accelerates the body, reflexes, and thought. The vampire dodges gunfire, acts without pause, crosses a room in an instant, runs along a wall or across water, and strikes faster than the target can defend. This Discipline is useful in chases, duels, sudden attacks, and escaping traps, and at high levels lets the vampire seemingly get ahead of the event itself.

system.limitations: speed doesn't cancel out obstacles, bad footing, or lack of space; many effects look inhuman, even if witnesses don't have time to register exactly what happened

Rapid Reflexes.description: The vampire reacts faster than an ordinary body should allow.
Rapid Reflexes.effect: In a firefight, they don't lose their defense pool for lacking cover, and each turn they gain one free minor action that would normally carry a 2d10 penalty.

Cat's Grace.effect: No Dexterity or Athletics checks are needed to keep balance on a ledge, a wire, or unstable footing, as long as the surface can physically bear the character's weight.

Fleetness.description: The Blood speeds up every movement for the scene.
Fleetness.effect: Outside combat, adds Celerity to Dexterity checks. In combat, once per turn, adds Celerity to a Dexterity + Athletics defense roll.

Blink.description: The vampire crosses a distance in a single burst, as if vanishing and reappearing at the destination.
Blink.effect: In one turn, the character can move about 50 meters in a straight line and still act without penalty. On difficult terrain, a Dexterity + Athletics check (or a contest, if outrunning an enemy matters) is needed.

Traversal.description: Through sheer speed, the vampire runs along walls, sloped surfaces, or the surface of water.
Traversal.effect: A Dexterity + Athletics check at difficulty 3 to 6. A success lets the character reach the nearest destination along the route; a margin extends the distance, though the limit is usually around 60 meters over water, or 30 stories upward.

Unerring Aim.description: An amalgam with Auspex 2: the world slows down for a ranged attack.
Unerring Aim.effect: Before firing or throwing, lowers the attack's difficulty to 1, and the target cannot dodge or defend. An opponent with Celerity 5 can cancel the effect by spending their own Blood trial.

Draught of Elegance.description: The vampire's blood temporarily passes part of their Celerity to whoever drinks straight from the vein.
