Draught of Elegance.effect: The drinker gains Celerity equal to half the vampire's rating (round down), along with any unmixed powers of that level the vampire knows. For vampires, the effect lasts until the next feeding or until Hunger 5.

Lightning Strike.description: A melee attack lands faster than the target can defend.
Lightning Strike.effect: Applies to a Brawl or Melee attack: difficulty 1, and the target neither defends nor dodges. An opponent with Celerity 5 can cancel the effect by spending their own Blood trial.

Split Second.description: The vampire reacts the instant an event is announced, as though they had already started acting in advance.
Split Second.effect: When the Storyteller announces a danger or a fact, the player may avert it with a brief action: slipping through a door, getting out of the line of fire, leaping clear of an explosion. For complex actions, the Storyteller sets a check.

Sanguinus.description: Sanguinus is the Discipline of the Blood Brothers, created by the Sabbat. It joins the vampires of a single circle into shared flesh, blood, and consciousness, letting them heal one another, exchange organs, act as a single mind, and merge into a monstrous union of blood.

system.theme: shared blood, the circle, flesh, brotherhood, collective mind, merged bodies
system.horror: Visible manifestations of Sanguinus require mortals to make a Courage check at difficulty 4, spend Willpower, or flee.

Brother's Blood.description: Members of a single circle use blood to heal one another and feel their shared kinship of blood.

Octopod.description: Blood Brothers can pass body parts, limbs, or organs to one another, creating grotesque but functional combinations.

Gestalt.description: The circle merges perception and consciousness, easing coordinated action.
effects: Mental and Social Disciplines can affect the whole circle as a single network
The perceptions of all members become interwoven
By concentrating, one brother can suppress another's will

Caine's Stride.description: The circle acts as a single organism, synchronizing movement and advance with inhuman precision.

Blood Union.description: Several Blood Brothers merge into a single enormous Frankenstein of flesh, organs, and minds.
Blood Union.generation: The monster's Generation improves by 1 for each participant beyond the first.
Blood Union.stats: Strength, Stamina, and Awareness equal the controller's ratings +1 for each participant beyond the first. Physical pools gain +1d10 for each participant beyond the first.
Blood Union.limits: Generation limits don't apply to the monster's inflated traits.

Obeah.description: Obeah is the Salubri's healing Discipline, tied to Saulot's third eye, compassion, diagnosis, pain relief, healing the body and mind, protecting a flock, and ultimate release from the bondage of flesh.

system.theme: healing, compassion, the third eye, protection, purification
system.third_eye: The third eye manifests as Obeah develops, becoming a mystical center of power.

Heartbeat of Life.description: Lets the character sense the target's bodily state: health, wounds, illness, death, the creature's nature, and traces of life force.

Soothe the Pain.description: Relieves pain, suppresses wound penalties, and can put a mortal to sleep.

Corpore Sano.cost: blood based on the type of wound
Corpore Sano.description: The Salubri heals another being's wounds by laying their hands near the injury.
Corpore Sano.effect: Superficial or aggravated: 1 turn and 1 Blood point. Aggravated wounds that risk death: 1 turn and 2 Blood points.

Shepherd's Eye.description: Creates an invisible barrier with a 3-meter radius around the Salubri, protecting anyone who was inside when it was created.
Shepherd's Eye.effect: Allies can leave and return freely. Enemies wishing to enter and cause harm must overcome the barrier.
Shepherd's Eye.note: Ranged weapons can still attack from outside.

Mens Sana.description: Heals mental disorders, trauma, and damage to the mind, if the victim is willing to accept the help.

Purification.description: A higher form of healing, helping cleanse the body, mind, or soul of foreign influence, corruption, and harmful distortion.

Renewal.description: Lets the character restore severe damage to body and spirit that ordinary healing doesn't cover.

Safe Passage.description: The Salubri gains a mystical harmlessness and an easier time seeking help, shelter, advice, and safe passage.
Safe Passage.social_effect: Charisma + Eloquence at difficulty 7; each success lowers the difficulty of the next innocent social request by 1.

Unbinding the Flesh.description: A senior power that frees a being from the limitations of the flesh, or lets them reach ultimate spiritual healing. The exact consequences are determined by the Storyteller.

Melpominee.description: Melpominee is the Daughters of Cacophony's unique Discipline, the power of speech, song, and tragic music. A Siren's voice affects not just the ear but the soul, so it even works on the deaf, as long as they're personally present for the performance.

system.theme: singing, tragedy, emotion, madness, the pain of sound
system.limit: Melpominee's level cannot exceed the character's Performance.
system.live_only: Doesn't work through a recording, radio, or the internet.
system.ensemble: Some powers can be performed as an ensemble: each additional Siren lowers the difficulty by 1, down to a minimum of 3.

Wandering Voice.effect: The character projects their voice to any visible point. If doing something other than speaking or singing at the same time, those actions suffer +2 difficulty.

Unseen Companion.effect: The voice is sent to any individual the character knows, at any distance, as long as it's night where they are.
Unseen Companion.duration: 1 turn per success; 3+ successes — until the end of the scene
Unseen Companion.penalty: +1 to the difficulty of vocal actions, since the character can't hear themselves.

Madrigal.effect: Each success infects one-fifth of the audience with the desired emotion. 6+ successes grant no additional effect.
Madrigal.resist: Anyone affected may spend Willpower if they realize they're being influenced.

Call of the Sirens.roll: Manipulation + Performance opposed by the victim's Willpower
Call of the Sirens.difficulty: the victim's Willpower opposed by the character's Looks + Performance
Call of the Sirens.effect: An extended, opposed action. Upon accumulating 5 successes, the Siren imposes a derangement or flaw on the victim for 1 night, +1 night per success beyond 5.

Virtuosity.effect: Lets the character apply Unseen Companion and Call of the Sirens to a number of victims equal to Stamina + Performance.
Virtuosity.cost: 1 Blood point for every additional group of five listeners beyond the first.

Piercing Crescendo.range: the victim must be within earshot
Piercing Crescendo.effect: Each success deals 1 unavoidable point of aggravated damage.

Frozen Echo.description: The Siren suspends a phrase, song, or Melpominee power in the air like a trap or a message.
Frozen Echo.effect: Each success lets the character suspend a phrase lasting 1 turn. If another Melpominee power is being suspended, its difficulty is +1.
Frozen Echo.trigger: Can be general, one-time, or addressed to a specific person the character knows.
Frozen Echo.detection: Heightened Senses can hear a faint melody; Awareness + Occult with 3 successes reveals the message, a failure stuns the listener until the end of the night.

Oblivion.description: Oblivion is the signature Discipline of clan Lasombra, granting power over darkness. Some Kindred believe its adepts merely control ordinary shadow; others are certain that this darkness is matter torn from the vampiric soul itself. Either way, Oblivion produces a frightening effect: living, viscous, almost tangible blackness gathers around the vampire, capable of concealing, choking, grasping, terrifying, and destroying.

system.theme: darkness, shadows, suffocation, fear, the abyss
system.note: A vampire using Oblivion sees freely through darkness they've created or control. Other vampires, even those who know Oblivion, don't get this benefit.
system.social_risk: Oblivion is strongly associated with the Lasombra and the Sabbat. Using this Discipline openly in Camarilla domains can raise suspicion.

Theater of Shadows.description: The vampire gains limited control over shadows and natural darkness: reshaping them, layering one shadow over another, detaching a shadow from its object, and creating illusions of presence.
Theater of Shadows.effect: A character wrapped in shadow gains +1d10 to Stealth checks. Ranged attacks against them suffer +1 to difficulty. When using shadows to intimidate, the character gains +1d10 to Intimidation. If the shadows wrap around a single target, that target's Stamina check pool is reduced by 1d10; if the pool drops to zero, a mortal, ghoul, or other breathing creature starts to suffocate.
Theater of Shadows.limits: Only one victim or object can be actively affected at a time. A group can gain the Stealth bonus if it stays relatively still.
Theater of Shadows.fear: Mortals and animals seeing a supernatural display like this for the first time make a Courage check at difficulty 8. On a failure, all their check pools are reduced by 1d10 until the end of the scene.

Shroud of Night.cost: usually no blood cost; 1 Blood point if the point of origin is out of sight
Shroud of Night.duration: 1 scene, or as long as the vampire maintains control
Shroud of Night.description: The vampire creates a cloud of impenetrable, ink-black darkness that snuffs out light, muffles sound, and feels like a viscous supernatural substance.
Shroud of Night.effect: A success creates a cloud roughly 3 meters across. Each additional success doubles the diameter. Everyone inside acts nearly blind and suffers +2 to check difficulty. The cloud extinguishes light sources except fire, muffles sound, and reduces victims' Stamina check pools by 2d10. If a breathing creature's Stamina pool drops to zero, they begin to suffocate.
Shroud of Night.limits: The Stamina-reduction effect doesn't stack with Theater of Shadows. Moving the cloud requires full concentration.
Shroud of Night.fear: Mortals and animals inside the cloud make a Courage check to avoid falling into panic.

Arms of the Abyss.description: The vampire weaves darkness into sturdy, mobile tentacles capable of grasping, binding, and crushing enemies.
Arms of the Abyss.effect: Each success creates one shadow tentacle about 2 meters long. The tentacle's Strength and Dexterity equal the creator's Oblivion level. If the vampire has Potence and Celerity, those ratings are added to the tentacles' Strength and Dexterity respectively. The tentacles can grapple and crush enemies, dealing damage each turn: Strength +1, aggravated.
Arms of the Abyss.health: Each tentacle has 4 Health Levels. Fire and the sun affect them as they would a vampire. Soak checks for the tentacles use the creator's Stamina + Fortitude. Aggravated damage to the tentacles is considered unavoidable.
Arms of the Abyss.escape: To break free, the victim must win an opposed Strength check against the tentacle's Strength, both at difficulty 6.
Arms of the Abyss.upgrade: The vampire may spend a Blood point to raise one tentacle's Strength or Dexterity by 1, or lengthen it by another 2 meters.
Arms of the Abyss.limits: The tentacles can't perform precise manipulation of objects, such as typing or driving.

Black Metamorphosis.description: The vampire merges with their inner darkness and transforms into a monstrous being of flesh and shadow. Their body becomes covered in shreds of gloom, and shadowy tentacles sprout from their chest and abdomen.
Black Metamorphosis.effect: The vampire's body grows 4 tentacles similar to Arms of the Abyss, but their Strength and Dexterity equal the vampire's own ratings, including Potence and Celerity. On contact with victims, directly or through the tentacles, the vampire reduces their Stamina check pools by 2d10. If the pool drops to zero, a breathing victim begins to suffocate.
Black Metamorphosis.combat: Using all of the tentacles, the vampire can make one extra attack per turn, beyond the normal limits on multiple actions.
Black Metamorphosis.vision: The vampire sees everything around them, even in total darkness.
Black Metamorphosis.fear: Mortals, animals, and beings unused to this kind of supernatural horror make a Courage check at difficulty 8 or fall into panicked madness.
Black Metamorphosis.bonus: While the power is active, the vampire gains +3d10 to Intimidation checks.
Black Metamorphosis.failure: On a failure, the blood is spent for nothing. On a botch, the darkness spirals out of control, and the vampire takes 2 unavoidable aggravated wounds.

Tenebrous Form.description: The vampire literally becomes a shadow, turning into an amoeba-like, impenetrably black mass of darkness.
Tenebrous Form.effect: In shadow form, the vampire is immune to physical attacks, can seep through cracks, openings, and fissures, move along walls and ceilings, see in any darkness, and use Mental Disciplines.
Tenebrous Form.combat: The vampire cannot physically attack, but can 'drown' opponents inside their incorporeal body with the same effect as Shroud of Night.
Tenebrous Form.weakness: Fire and sunlight continue to deal aggravated damage. The difficulty of Rötschreck checks from fire and the sun increases by 1.
Tenebrous Form.fear: Mortals and beings unused to such displays make a Courage check at difficulty 8 or fall into panic.

Inner Darkness.description: The vampire expels their inner darkness through their mouth or torn flesh, directing it at a victim.
Inner Darkness.effect: The darkness envelops the chosen victim. Each turn inside the cloud, the victim loses 1 Blood point unless they pass a Stamina check at difficulty 6.
Inner Darkness.feeding: When the vampire calls the cloud back, they receive half of the Blood points lost by victims, rounded up. This counts as normal blood consumption; if the blood belonged to a vampire, it can form the basis of a blood bond.
Inner Darkness.concentration: Maintaining the cloud requires full concentration. If the vampire is attacked, the cloud immediately returns to their body.
Inner Darkness.limits: The cloud can be in contact with several beings at once, but can only draw blood from one of them per turn.
Inner Darkness.fear: The victim and witnesses may fall into panicked madness if they aren't used to Oblivion's powers.

Shadow Step.description: The vampire enters one shadow and instantly emerges from another, bypassing walls, floors, and even supernatural barriers.
Shadow Step.effect: On a success, the vampire is transported to another shadow within a 15-meter radius. If there's no shadow or darkness at the exit point, they materialize in front of the obstacle instead.
Shadow Step.failure: On a failure, the character stays in place. On a botch, they get stuck between shadows; the consequences are up to the Storyteller.
Shadow Step.other_target: To push another character through a shadow, the vampire rolls Strength + Occult.

Shadow Twin.duration: 1 hour per success, but no later than dawn
Shadow Twin.description: The vampire animates their own shadow or someone else's, detaching it from its owner and turning it into a servant.
Shadow Twin.effect: The chosen individual's shadow becomes a free-standing Shadow Twin and serves the vampire. The twin's traits and abilities equal half of the shadow's owner's ratings, rounded down. The twin gains Oblivion at a level equal to half the creator's Oblivion, rounded down.
Shadow Twin.movement: The Shadow Twin can move up to 15 meters away from its owner, and can seep through small openings, cracks, and fissures.
Shadow Twin.combat: It can fight by normal rules, but deals and takes half the usual damage, rounded down. Fire, fangs, mystical powers, and other supernatural attacks deal full damage.
Shadow Twin.death: If the Shadow Twin is destroyed, its owner loses half of their current Willpower points and must immediately make a Courage check at difficulty 9 to avoid Rötschreck.
Shadow Twin.fear: Mortals and vampires unfamiliar with Oblivion make a Courage check upon seeing the twin.

Shadow Prison.roll: Wits + Larceny against the victim's Dexterity + Occult
Shadow Prison.description: The vampire creates a mass of utterly impenetrable darkness and turns it into a trap for a single victim.
Shadow Prison.effect: The victim is sealed inside a cell of darkness. A mortal inside suffocates after a number of minutes equal to their Stamina, unless the vampire leaves the victim's head outside, or seals a small air supply within.
Shadow Prison.vampire_target: A vampire inside the Shadow Prison hangs suspended in silent darkness, unable to use Disciplines or take actions.
Shadow Prison.limits: The vampire can maintain only one Shadow Prison at a time, and it can hold only one victim.
Shadow Prison.end: The Shadow Prison disappears at its creator's will, or when touched by sunlight.

Ahriman's Domain.description: The vampire calls forth thick, choking darkness, capable of snuffing out the life and unlife of anyone caught in its black vortex.
Ahriman's Domain.effect: At the end of the third turn, the character makes a check. Each success deals 1 aggravated wound to every victim in range; if the victim is a vampire, it deals 1 wound that risks final death instead.
Ahriman's Domain.special: After dealing damage, Ahriman's Domain disappears, carrying off into darkness the bodies of those who died within the cloud.
Ahriman's Domain.danger: The power doesn't distinguish between friend and foe.

Temporis.description: Temporis is the True Brujah's rare Discipline, power over the perception of time, personal temporal speed, delaying damage, aging objects, and briefly rewinding consciousness into the past.

system.theme: time, memory, delay, acceleration, aging, rewind

Hourglass of the Mind.description: The character senses the flow of time, its distortions, and subjective shifts in time.

Retrospection.description: The character reads the past of a place or object, observing echoes of past events.

Slow Time.description: The character slows time for a victim or object, worsening their reaction speed and ability to act.

Patience of the Norns.description: The character delays the effect of damage, events, or other effects, letting them catch up to the target later.

Clotho's Gift.description: The character speeds up their own subjective time, gaining an advantage in their actions.

Lachesis's Kiss.description: The character ages or rejuvenates an object with a touch.

effect_table.6+: +100 years per success beyond the fifth

Lachesis's Kiss.note: Rejuvenation is 1 harder. Living creatures aged beyond their natural lifespan quickly die of old age.

Deceiving the Fates.description: The character deceives the moment fate arrives, delaying or altering the consequences of events.

Clio's Kiss.description: The character interacts with history, memory, and the probable traces of the past, altering the perception of causes and consequences.

Atropos's Thread.cost: 3 Blood points + 1 Willpower reduction; Willpower can be reduced further for automatic successes
Atropos's Thread.description: The character reflexively casts their consciousness back in time into their own past body.
Atropos's Thread.effect: Each success rewinds 1 turn and deals 1 unavoidable point of aggravated damage.
Atropos's Thread.note: Events repeat along a known script until the character changes them; after that, the future is uncertain again.

Auspex.description: Auspex reveals what's hidden from ordinary senses. The vampire sharpens hearing, sight, and smell, notices supernatural traces, reads auras, gets premonitions, taps into others' senses, learns the history of objects and places, reads minds, or even takes over a mortal's body. This is the Discipline of detectives, prophets, blackmailers, and paranoiacs: it provides answers, but often brings overload and unpleasant truths along with them.

system.limitations: much of the information arrives in fragments or images; strong stimuli can overload the senses; resisting targets and hidden entities require a contest

Heightened Senses.description: All of the vampire's senses become supernaturally sharp.
Heightened Senses.effect: Adds Auspex to Awareness checks. A loud noise, a flash, or a strong smell can overload the senses: on a failed Wits + Resolve check, the character takes -3d10 to Awareness until the end of the scene.

Sense the Unseen.description: The vampire notices the supernatural, the hidden, and the unnatural nearby.
Sense the Unseen.effect: Can detect Obfuscate, magical traps, traces of rituals, ghosts, and surveillance via Auspex. Passive searching uses Wits + Auspex, active searching uses Resolve + Auspex; hiding entities resist with a contest.

Premonition.description: A brief flash of insight warns of danger or points out an important detail.
Premonition.effect: The Storyteller may grant a vision with no roll, to point out a missed clue or threat. The character can also deliberately invoke a premonition by asking a question and rolling Resolve + Auspex with a Blood trial; more successes give a clearer answer.

Scry the Soul.description: The vampire reads a target's aura: emotions, resonance, supernatural nature, and traces of magic.
Scry the Soul.effect: Each margin of success answers one question about the aura. A triumph reveals an additional, unexpected fact. In a crowd, a specific type of aura can be searched for, with difficulty based on the size and chaos of the location.

Share the Senses.description: The vampire taps into another being's senses.
Share the Senses.effect: Lets the character see, hear, or feel what the target does, without losing their own senses. An unfamiliar target must be seen; a target carrying the vampire's vitae can be sought at a distance. Sense the Unseen helps notice the intrusion and try to expel the watcher.

Spirit's Touch.description: Touching an object or place reveals the emotional traces of the past.
Spirit's Touch.effect: Difficulty depends on how old the trace is and how specific the question is. A margin of success lets the character look further back, seeing more owners of the object or more events at the location. The information is usually impressionistic, but gives unique leads for investigation.

Telepathy.description: The vampire sends thoughts and reads another's mind.
Telepathy.cost: 1 Blood trial (+ Willpower against a vampire)
Telepathy.effect: Sending messages to, and reading, a willing target within sight requires no roll. A resisting mortal requires eye contact and a contest; a resisting vampire additionally requires spending a point of Willpower. A triumph gives a clear picture of current thoughts and intentions.

Clairvoyance.description: In a trance, the vampire explores a part of the city or a haven with their inner eye.
Clairvoyance.effect: After the check, the player can ask questions about what's happening, rumors, moods, recent upheavals, and hidden events in the area. Gathering the information takes minutes, and a faint connection to the place persists until dawn.

Possession.description: An amalgam with Dominate 3: the vampire suppresses a mortal and takes over their body.
Possession.effect: The vampire's own body falls into something like torpor. In the victim's body, the character can walk in sunlight, eat, and act socially, but can't read the victim's thoughts or use their skills. Serious harm to either body can interrupt the effect; the victim's death deals heavy Stress.

Visceratika.description: Visceratika is the Gargoyles' Discipline, reflecting their affinity with stone, earth, concrete, and guarding havens. It combines camouflage, stone-like toughness, sentinel perception, and the ability to merge with stone structures.

system.theme: stone, earth, concrete, sentinel, armored skin, haven
system.note: In theory any vampire could learn it from a Gargoyle mentor, but the Gargoyles almost never share their secrets.

Chameleon's Hide.description: The character's skin takes on the color and texture of its surroundings.
Chameleon's Hide.limits: Only works while moving no faster than a walking pace. In flight, the skin darkens to match the night sky, but a silhouette can still be spotted.

Sentinel of the Hearth.extra_roll: Awareness + Sense the Unseen (to find a specific being); against a hiding target, opposed by Wits + Stealth
Sentinel of the Hearth.duration: as long as the character touches or remains inside the building; no longer than dawn
Sentinel of the Hearth.description: The character senses living and undead beings inside the structure they're guarding.
Sentinel of the Hearth.effect: Gives a general sense of the location, size, and condition of beings inside the building.
Sentinel of the Hearth.targeting: To find a specific being: Awareness + Sense the Unseen, difficulty 6; if the target is hiding, this is opposed by Wits + Stealth.
Sentinel of the Hearth.obfuscate: The character senses supernaturally hidden beings only as a presence, without pinpointing them, unless they have a way to see through Obfuscate.

Merge with Stone.description: The character merges with a large stone, concrete mass, or similar structure.
Merge with Stone.effect: Works like Earth Meld, but leaves a barely visible outline of the body.
Merge with Stone.defense: The defense pool against attacks while inside the stone is tripled.
Merge with Stone.forced_exit: If an attack deals 3+ points of aggravated damage at once, the character is ejected from the stone and suffers -2 to Initiative on the first turn.

Terra's Armor.description: The skin hardens and takes on a stone-like quality.
Terra's Armor.effect: +1d10 to soak against aggravated damage, +2d10 against superficial damage.
Terra's Armor.fire: The damage pool from fire is halved, but the Rötschreck rules don't change.
Terra's Armor.penalty: +2 to the difficulty of touch-based perception checks.

Move Through Stone.cost: 2 Blood points after Merge with Stone; 1 Blood point to pass through a wall
Move Through Stone.extra_roll: Strength + Potence (to pass through a wall)
Move Through Stone.description: The character moves through stone and concrete like lava through rock.
Move Through Stone.effect: After using Merge with Stone, the character can emerge at any point within the same structure.
Move Through Stone.wall_pass: To pass through a wall without merging first: Strength + Potence, difficulty 8; each success = 30 cm of stone or concrete.
Move Through Stone.failure: If there aren't enough successes, the character gets stuck inside until freed, or until they activate the power again.

Mytherceria.description: Mytherceria is the Kiasyd's clan Discipline, a strange fae art of lies, riddles, memory, and self-deception. It lets them sense lies, see magic, erase psychic traces, confound space, steal memories, and even temporarily convince themselves of their own mortality.

system.theme: lies, memory, riddles, auras, fae marks, self-deception

Nonsense.effect: The character knows when the other party is consciously lying. Doesn't reveal the truth, and doesn't trigger if the speaker themselves believes the lie.

Enchanted Sight.effect: Sees the fae and enchanted mortals in their true form, as well as traces of magic from mages, werewolves, and other beings, except ghosts, vampires, and the undead.

Aura Drain.difficulty: based on the age and strength of the impression
Aura Drain.effect: Each success grants one image from the object's past, or one aspect of the personality that left the impression: Nature, Mask, aura, name, sex, age.
Aura Drain.aftereffect: Further psychometry attempts must score more successes than the Kiasyd did, or they see nothing.

Fae Mark.difficulty: 2 + the victim's Willpower, or 7 for objects
Fae Mark.effect: The victim's Intelligence pool near the mark is reduced by 2d10. Anyone who sees the mark loses their bearings until they pass a Wits + Investigation check at difficulty 8.
Fae Mark.immunity: Kiasyd are immune to their own marks.

Magic Riddle.effect: The victim freezes in place, searching for the answer. To solve the riddle: an extended Wits + Occult check, difficulty 8 ± derangements, 1 check per hour; three times as many successes as the Kiasyd scored are needed.
Magic Riddle.botch: A botch on the victim's part deals 1 aggravated wound and resets accumulated successes; the damage only heals once the riddle is solved.
Magic Riddle.dismiss: The Kiasyd can personally state the answer and lift the power.

Memory Theft.effect: The Kiasyd gains access to all of the victim's memories and thoughts. The victim loses that knowledge, but retains motor skills.
Memory Theft.death: If the victim dies, the stolen memory remains with the Kiasyd permanently.

Mind Theft.roll: Awareness + Empathy opposed by the victim's Willpower
Mind Theft.effect: The Kiasyd permanently steals dots of the victim's abilities and gains them, if able to raise their own rating.
Mind Theft.victim_win: The victim gains immunity for a year and a day; a botch by the Kiasyd grants the victim full immunity to them.

The Greatest Trick.effect: At dawn, the Kiasyd becomes mortal for a duration set by the table, forgetting everything tied to their vampiric nature.
The Greatest Trick.limits: Cannot use Disciplines or spend blood, and traits above 5 are reduced to 5. Upon returning, the character remembers the period of mortality.

Fortitude.description: Fortitude makes a vampire hard to kill, in body and mind alike. It expands the Health track, helps resist fear, temptation, Dominate, and Presence, softens injuries, protects against fire and the sun better than usual, and lets the vampire keep fighting where another Kindred would already have fallen. It isn't immortality stacked on top of immortality, but the Discipline buys time to survive, retreat, or finish the job.

system.limitations: doesn't cancel damage entirely and doesn't make the sun safe; some protections must be activated in advance; once temporary effects end, old wounds can become a deadly problem again

Resilience.description: The Blood strengthens the body and expands the amount of damage the vampire can withstand.
Resilience.effect: Each point of Fortitude adds one Health Level to the track. The effect is permanent.

Unswayable Mind.description: The vampire's mind becomes stubborn, cold, or resistant to pressure.
Unswayable Mind.effect: Adds Fortitude to pools resisting intimidation, temptation, coercion, and supernatural attempts to shake the will, including Dominate and Presence.

Enduring Beasts.description: An amalgam with Animalism 1: the vampire shares their Fortitude with animals under their influence.
Enduring Beasts.effect: A famulus gains the bonus automatically. Other animals require a Blood trial and a check; each fortified animal gains additional Health Levels equal to the vampire's Fortitude. When the effect ends, the extra empty boxes disappear first.

Toughness.description: The vampire's body dampens ordinary wounds before they become dangerous.
Toughness.effect: When taking superficial damage, first reduce it by the Fortitude rating, then halve the remainder. A successful attack still deals a minimum of 1 point of damage.

Defy the Bane.description: The vampire temporarily lessens damage from fire, the sun, and other lethal threats.
Defy the Bane.effect: Until the end of the scene, a number of aggravated wounds or Stress equal to Fortitude can be converted to superficial. This pool is shared for the whole scene. If caught off guard by the danger, a reflexive Wits + Survival check at difficulty 3 is needed.

Fortify the Inner Facade.description: The vampire's thoughts, emotions, and aura become dull and closed off to supernatural reading.
Fortify the Inner Facade.effect: The difficulty of powers like Telepathy and Spirit's Touch increases by half the vampire's Fortitude, rounded up; if there's a contest, the vampire adds Fortitude to their defense pool.

Draught of Resilience.description: The vampire's blood temporarily passes part of their Fortitude to whoever drinks straight from the vein.
Draught of Resilience.effect: The drinker gains Fortitude equal to half the vampire's rating (round down), along with any unmixed powers of that level the vampire knows. For vampires, the effect lasts until the next feeding or until Hunger 5.

Prowess from Pain.description: Pain stops being a hindrance and starts fueling the body's fury.
Prowess from Pain.effect: The vampire ignores penalties from physical wounds. For every marked wound, they raise one Physical attribute by 1, though derived traits like Health aren't recalculated; the maximum is capped by Blood Potency.

Flesh of Marble.description: Skin and flesh instantly shrug off the first serious hit each turn.
Flesh of Marble.effect: Each turn, the first successful physical attack against the vampire deals no damage, including fire, but not sunlight. A triumph by the attacker breaks through the defense.

Obfuscate.description: Obfuscate hides a vampire not through physical invisibility, but by deceiving perception. People look right past them, don't notice their footsteps, mistake a Kindred for an unremarkable passerby, or forget they saw them a second ago. At higher levels, it can hide objects, make the vampire vanish before someone's eyes, copy another's appearance, and shelter an entire group. This is the Discipline of spies, killers, fugitives, and anyone who prefers to be nearby while no one realizes they're there.

system.limitations: the effect breaks if the vampire's presence can't be ignored: they attack, make loud noise, block a passage, or draw direct attention; cameras and automated systems usually aren't fooled without special powers

Silence of Death.description: The vampire makes no sound of movement: no footsteps, rustling clothes, breathing, or incidental touches.
Silence of Death.effect: The power only affects the hearing of living observers, not microphones. It doesn't muffle sounds of objects the vampire drops or moves, but makes ordinary sneaking almost silent.

Cloak of Shadows.description: As long as the vampire stays still and has at least some cover, the minds of those around them slide right past.
Cloak of Shadows.effect: The effect requires no roll, but ends if the character moves, makes noise, draws attention, or is detected supernaturally or electronically.

Unseen Passage.description: The vampire can move while remaining unnoticed by ordinary senses.
Unseen Passage.effect: Works as long as they make no more noise than a whisper, carry no strong odors, and do nothing that obviously draws attention. The vampire can't vanish this way if someone is looking directly at them the moment it's activated.

Mask of a Thousand Faces.description: The vampire looks like a completely ordinary, unremarkable person who fits the location.
Mask of a Thousand Faces.effect: Onlookers see a guard, a worker, a passerby, or some other background person of similar sex and build. The power doesn't copy a specific identity, but lets the vampire interact without being immediately exposed.

Ghost in the Machine.description: Obfuscate begins to fool live cameras and degrade recordings.
Ghost in the Machine.effect: Live-feed observers are subject to the same rules as eyewitnesses. On recordings and photos, the figure is blurred: +3 to identification difficulty. Against automated surveillance, the vampire gains +3d10.

Vanish.description: The vampire disappears right before someone's eyes, and mortals start to doubt they ever saw them at all.
Vanish.effect: Requires a contest against the observers. A win lets the vampire activate Cloak of Shadows or Unseen Passage under direct observation; a triumph erases the moment of vanishing from a mortal's memory. Once per scene.

Conceal.description: An amalgam with Auspex 3: hides an inanimate object from anyone's attention.
Conceal.effect: The vampire touches the object and rolls Intelligence + Obfuscate, with difficulty based on its size and conspicuousness. The object's contents are hidden from outside observers too. The effect fails if the object starts moving on its own.

Impostor's Guise.description: The vampire copies the appearance of a specific person, and, with observation, their voice and mannerisms too.
Impostor's Guise.effect: Requires studying the face for at least 5 minutes, and another 10 minutes for behavior. A hidden roll determines the quality of the likeness; a normal success may require Manipulation + Performance, while a triumph makes the impersonation flawless.

Cloak the Gathering.description: The vampire extends a known Obfuscate power to willing companions.
Cloak the Gathering.effect: By default, a number of targets equal to the vampire's Wits can be covered; additional targets require more Blood trials. If one companion gives themselves away, only they are exposed; if the initiator gives themselves away, the whole group is exposed.

Ogham.description: Ogham is the Lhiannan's secret runic Discipline, tied to blood, trees, groves, curses, and ancient natural signs. Its powers require inscribing mystical symbols and are often bound to territory, the moon, and the earth.

system.theme: blood marks, groves, earth, moon, curses, druidic magic

Consecrate the Grove.description: The character consecrates a natural place with blood and runes, creating a mystical territory for further Ogham powers.

Scarlet Patterns.description: The character inscribes bloody marks that strengthen their connection to a place, a target, or a nature spirit.

Inscribe a Curse.description: The character inscribes a cursed mark on the victim's body, choosing the type of curse.

curse_options.Curse of the Body: a mark on a limb; weakens the body and actions taken with that limb
curse_options.Curse of the Mind: a mark on the forehead; hinders willpower-driven and magical actions
curse_options.Curse of the Voice: a mark on the throat; the victim loses articulate speech
curse_options.Curse of the Soul: a mark along the breastbone or over the heart; +2 to frenzy resistance difficulty; for non-vampires — animal terror and flight on a failed Courage check at difficulty 8

Moon and Sun.description: The character uses the phase of the moon to strengthen certain checks.

Drain the Land.description: The character draws life force out of the earth, plants, and territory, causing withering and spiritual desolation.

Greater Inscription of Blood.description: A senior form of inscription, letting the character create longer-lasting and deeper runic effects on a territory, target, or object.

Thin-blood Alchemy.description: Thin-blood Alchemy is the unstable science and magic of diluted Blood. An alchemist mixes their own vitae, human blood of the right resonance, and strange components to produce elixirs, vapors, injections, or internal reactions that mimic the Disciplines of true Kindred. It requires preparation and formulas, but in exchange gives the thin-blooded flexibility: telekinesis, fog, flight, restoring blood, reshaping the body, and even waking someone from torpor.

costs.activation: as for the power being replicated

notes: each formula is learned separately and tied to the chosen distillation method
the mandatory component of any formula is the alchemist's vitae; human blood of the right resonance is usually also needed
rare, plot-relevant, or especially fitting components can grant a bonus, while cheap substitutes give a penalty
alchemy can copy many Discipline powers, but not Blood Sorcery rituals

Athanor Corporis.description: Distillation inside the alchemist's own body
Athanor Corporis.result: the effect is stored in the body

Calcination.result: the effect is stored in the victim's blood

Fixation.limit: limited by how many potions can be carried

components: human blood with the needed resonance
notes: the quality of components can grant a bonus or penalty
powers of other Disciplines can be copied
there are no rituals (unlike Blood Sorcery)

Far Reach.extra_roll: target's resistance: Strength + Athletics; precise manipulation is a separate check
Far Reach.effect: Works on visible targets up to 10 meters away and weighing up to about 100 kg. A resisting target rolls Strength + Athletics; if the alchemist wins, the target can be pulled, pushed, dropped, or dealt superficial damage based on the margin. Precise manipulation requires a separate check.
Far Reach.description: A telekinetic effect: the alchemist grabs, pushes, and holds objects or people at a distance.

Haze.effect: Opponents take -2d10 to ranged attacks against the alchemist and to attempts to identify them. An additional Blood trial expands the cloud to cover up to five people.
Haze.description: Vapor or fog surrounds the alchemist, blurring their silhouette and hiding their face.

Smog.effect: The target takes -3d10 to being visually spotted and to ranged attacks. Against a mortal, the alchemist can win a contest to trigger a coughing fit, or unconsciousness on a triumph. Only one target can be affected at a time.
Smog.description: A caustic cloud thickens around the chosen target, making it hard to see and breathe.

Discipline Imitation (Level 1).effect: The formula must be learned separately, usually taking at least a week. When used, all checks substitute Alchemy for the original Discipline, and the Storyteller may forbid an effect that doesn't fit the chosen distillation method well.
Discipline Imitation (Level 1).description: The alchemist develops a formula that copies a level-1 power of another Discipline.

Blood Renewal.effect: Each success on the distillation restores roughly one unit of blood, removing 1 Hunger. This is especially useful to anyone who normally can't feed on medical blood without the Iron Gullet merit.
Blood Renewal.description: The elixir restores the vampire-nourishing quality to fractionated donor blood.

Unholy Hierogamy.effect: The target mixes the elixir with their own blood, drinks it, and falls into a feverish sleep until the next night. A success changes the body within the bounds of human anatomy; a triumph may buy a Looks merit or remove a Looks flaw with experience. The Nosferatu cannot use this to remove their clan bane.
Unholy Hierogamy.description: A rare formula reshapes the target's body to match their inner self-image.

Discipline Imitation (Level 2).effect: Learning it usually takes at least a month, and distillation takes at least one night. The formula, components, and resonance are all fixed separately.
Discipline Imitation (Level 2).description: The alchemist develops a formula that copies a level-2 power of another Discipline.

Impulse.effect: Movement at roughly running speed. A load about the weight of a person can be carried at walking speed. To lift a resisting target or break free of a grapple, a Strength + Alchemy contest against Strength + Athletics is needed.
Impulse.description: The alchemist overcomes gravity and can fly or hover.

Discipline Imitation (Level 3).effect: Usually requires a drop of vitae from a vampire of the needed Discipline or clan, at least three months of research, and at least three nights of distillation. The amount of vitae is too small to form a blood bond, but obtaining it socially is dangerous.
Discipline Imitation (Level 3).description: The alchemist develops a formula that copies a level-3 power of another Discipline.

Awaken the Sleeper.effect: The maximum Blood Potency of the one being awakened equals the distillation's successes minus 2. With Calcination or Athanor Corporis, the elixir is drawn from the alchemist's own body or from a vessel; with Calcination, the vessel takes aggravated damage.
Awaken the Sleeper.description: The elixir mixes with human blood and can raise a vampire out of torpor.

Discipline Imitation (Level 4).effect: This is almost legendary work: usually at least three years of research and about a month of distillation. The formula must be agreed upon with the Storyteller, since not every high-level power can be plausibly or safely copied.
Discipline Imitation (Level 4).description: The alchemist develops a formula that copies a level-4 power of another Discipline.

Valeren.description: Valeren is the war Discipline of the Salubri warriors, a legacy of the clan's knightly nights. Like Obeah, it awakens Saulot's third eye, but it's aimed not at mercy, but at pain, armor, battle-ecstasy, and the sacred fury of the Furies.

system.theme: the third eye, pain, war, the wrath of Caine, Samiel
system.third_eye: The third eye appears upon reaching the second level, as with Obeah.

Heartbeat of Life.description: Works like the identically named Obeah power: lets the character sense the target's life force, illnesses, injuries, and bodily state.

Soothe the Pain.description: Works like the identically named Obeah power: suppresses pain, can boost an ally's combat ability, or put a mortal to sleep.

Searing Touch.description: A Fury's touch causes the victim pain comparable to touching red-hot metal.
Searing Touch.effect: While the character maintains the touch, each Blood point spent reduces the victim's pool for all checks by 2d10.
Searing Touch.limits: The pain stops almost immediately once the character removes their hand.

Armor of Caine's Wrath.description: A scarlet, glowing halo surrounds the Fury, protecting against damage and combat Rötschreck.
Armor of Caine's Wrath.effect: Each success grants 1 point of armor against superficial and aggravated damage, up to a maximum of 5.
Armor of Caine's Wrath.rotschreck: Every 2 successes increase the pool to resist Rötschreck from combat events. Doesn't help against fire or the sun.

Samiel's Vengeance.extra_roll: damage pool: Dexterity + Melee or Brawl; every die counts as a success
Samiel's Vengeance.description: The third eye opens with an icy blue glow, and the next blow becomes unstoppable.
Samiel's Vengeance.effect: The next single melee attack automatically hits. It cannot be dodged, but can be blocked, parried, or absorbed.
Samiel's Vengeance.damage: The hit is treated as though every die in the Dexterity + Melee or Brawl pool came up a success.
Samiel's Vengeance.limits: Only once per turn, only immediately after activation; doesn't work with ranged weapons.

Blissful Agony.cost: 1 Blood point; additional Blood points for damage
Blissful Agony.description: The Fury makes the pain of Searing Touch continue even after contact ends.
Blissful Agony.extension: The effect can be extended with a new Willpower check, with no additional blood cost.
Blissful Agony.damage: By touching the target again, the character may spend blood: each point inflicts 1 aggravated wound. For mortals, these wounds are unavoidable.
Blissful Agony.aftereffect: For supernatural beings, the damage fades at dawn. For mortals, it heals normally.
Blissful Agony.frenzy: If the number of wounds exceeds the victim's Willpower, each subsequent wound requires a Willpower check at difficulty 6 to avoid frenzy.

Dementation.description: Dementation is the Malkavian clan Discipline, letting them command madness, drag hidden fears, passions, and psychoses to the surface, break perception, and shatter a victim's sanity. This power doesn't create madness out of nothing: it uncovers what's already hidden in the depths of the mind, amplifies inner chaos, and turns suppressed impulses into a weapon.

system.theme: madness, emotion, hallucination, psychosis, chaos, personality change
system.note: Dementation acts on the mind, emotions, and perception. Its effects often depend on the victim's current state, their hidden fears, their Nature, and the Storyteller's judgment.
system.style: Information gained through Dementation rarely arrives as a dry fact. More often it comes as allegory, symbol, distorted imagery, and painful revelation.

Passion.description: The vampire amplifies or dampens an emotion the victim is already feeling in the moment.
Passion.effect: The character doesn't choose a specific emotion, but acts on a feeling that already exists. Mild irritation can become rage, love can become faint fondness, anxiety can become panic or indifference.
Passion.mechanical_effect: At the Storyteller's discretion, the altered emotional state may lower or raise the difficulty of a fitting check by 1–2 points: frenzy resistance, Courage, resisting Presence, and so on.

Haunting.description: The vampire fills the victim's mind with brief, vague, but convincing false sensations: visions, sounds, smells, touches, or other sensory distortions.
Haunting.activation: The character must speak with the victim and choose which sense the haunting will afflict.
Haunting.effect: The vampire doesn't control the exact content of the hallucinations. They mainly appear at night, when the victim is alone, and usually trigger anxiety, fear, unpleasant memories, or a sense of approaching madness.
Haunting.mechanical_effect: An especially vivid haunting can reduce the pool of one or two checks, at the Storyteller's discretion.

Eye of Chaos.description: The vampire finds patterns in chaos: reading the hidden nature of a soul, recognizing psychoses, seeing secret connections between events, and drawing revelations from absurd imagery.

nature_reading.description: To determine a victim's Nature, the character concentrates for 1 turn and makes a check.

coded_message.effect: Lets the character attempt to read a coded message or hidden meaning.

pattern_reading.effect: Lets the character search for meaning in random phenomena: falling leaves, cracks, noise, patterns of blood, shadows, and other chaotic sources.

Eye of Chaos.story_use: This power can provide story hints, point to missed clues, foreshadow important events, or reveal hidden connections. Revelations should be symbolic, allegorical, and not always obvious.

Voice of Madness.description: The vampire addresses a group of victims and plunges them into bloodthirsty rage or panicked terror.
Voice of Madness.activation: The character must address the potential victims: through speech, mockery, a command, a scream, or some other vocal effect.
Voice of Madness.effect: Each success affects one listener. Victims immediately fall into frenzy or a state of terror resembling Rötschreck.
Voice of Madness.supernatural_resistance: Kindred, Lupines, and other beings capable of frenzy may attempt a frenzy or Rötschreck resistance check, but the difficulty increases by 2.
Voice of Madness.mortal_effect: Mortals fall into rage or terror with no check, and don't remember what they did in that state.
Voice of Madness.duration: Until the end of the scene. Vampires and Lupines may try to control the state by normal means.
Voice of Madness.self_risk: The character also makes a frenzy or Rötschreck check, but at a difficulty 1 lower than usual for them.
Voice of Madness.failure: If activation fails, the difficulty of the character's own frenzy / Rötschreck check increases by 1.
Voice of Madness.botch: On a botch, the character themselves falls into frenzy or Rötschreck with no check.

Mind Shatter.description: The vampire plunges the victim's mind into the depths of their own madness, inflicting a severe derangement.
Mind Shatter.activation: The character must hold the victim's full attention for at least 1 turn.
Mind Shatter.effect: On a success, the victim gains 5 derangements of the Storyteller's choosing.
Mind Shatter.early_recovery: The victim can shake off the effect early by spending Willpower points equal to the number of successes scored on activation. The Storyteller decides when and under what conditions this is possible.
Mind Shatter.botch: On a botch, the consequences are up to the Storyteller; usually the vampire confronts whatever they themselves awakened in another's mind.

Permanent Derangement.roll: Manipulation + Empathy against the victim's Willpower
Permanent Derangement.description: The vampire implants a permanent mental disorder in the victim.
Permanent Derangement.activation: The character must speak with the victim for at least 1 minute, describing the disorder they intend to impose.
Permanent Derangement.effect: If the character scores more successes than the victim, the victim gains a permanent derangement of the character's choosing.
Permanent Derangement.limits: Only one derangement can be implanted in a given victim per night. Attempts can be repeated until one is successfully implanted.

Shattered Mirror.roll: Charisma + Subterfuge against the victim's Wits + Self-Control / Instinct
Shattered Mirror.description: The vampire infects the victim with their own manias, psychoses, disorders, and mental flaws.
Shattered Mirror.activation: The character must make eye contact with the victim.
Shattered Mirror.effect: If the character wins the contest, the victim gains all of the character's derangements and mental flaws for a set period.

duration_table.6+ successes: 1 year for each success beyond the fifth

The Remaking.description: The vampire completely changes the victim's Nature, reshaping the basic structure of their personality.
The Remaking.activation: The character must make eye contact with the victim.
The Remaking.effect: If the number of successes exceeds the victim's Self-Control / Instinct rating, their Nature changes to one chosen by the player.
The Remaking.duration: Permanent. The effect can only be undone by another successful use of The Remaking.
The Remaking.side_effect: Such fundamental changes rarely come without consequence; minor deviations from the original personality are almost inevitable.
The Remaking.botch: On a botch, the character's own Nature changes instead, becoming the Nature they failed to impose on the victim.

Self-Destruction.description: The vampire turns the victim's mind against itself, making their own will into a weapon of self-annihilation.
Self-Destruction.activation: The character must touch the victim or make eye contact with them.
Self-Destruction.effect: The number of successes determines how many turns the victim suffers the effect. Each turn, the victim makes a Willpower check; each success on that check deals them 1 aggravated wound.
Self-Destruction.resistance: Instead of a soak check, the victim may instead make a Humanity / Path check at difficulty 6 each time. Fortitude and armor don't apply.
Self-Destruction.condition: While the power lasts, the victim cannot take actions, not even spending blood to heal. They can only scream and writhe in pain.
Self-Destruction.visual: Through Auspex, the victim's aura looks like it's boiling with madness, throwing off psychic flashes of rage.

Eruption of Madness.description: The vampire triggers a psychic catastrophe of enormous scale, plunging every sentient being in the area of effect into wild, bloodthirsty rage.

area_table.5 successes: the entire city, an area roughly 45 kilometers across
area_table.6+ successes: the entire city and surrounding area; +15 kilometers to the diameter per success beyond the fifth

Eruption of Madness.effect: Every sentient being in the area of effect falls into madness. Mortals riot, brawl, set fires, and loot. Vampires are gripped by hunting frenzy and a desire to drain as many vessels as possible.
Eruption of Madness.resistance: Victims may attempt a Self-Control / Instinct check at a difficulty equal to the character's Willpower. Each success grants 1 hour of immunity to the effect.
Eruption of Madness.escape: If a victim leaves the area of effect during their period of immunity, the threat of going mad stays behind them.
Eruption of Madness.detection: A character with Heightened Senses can locate the epicenter of the psychic shockwave with no check, but not the vampire who triggered the Eruption.
Eruption of Madness.aftermath: Even after dawn, the consequences can continue: the city remains in chaos, and the wave of violence may carry on under its own momentum.

Protean.description: Protean changes the vampire's dead body. They see in darkness, grow claws, become nearly weightless, sink into the earth, take on a bestial form, turn into mist, or make their heart mobile and nearly impossible to reach. It's a very practical Discipline for hunting, escape, and survival, but its manifestations are hard to explain to witnesses: animal eyes, claws, transforming into a wolf or into mist almost always threaten the Masquerade.

system.resonance: the blood of animals and hybrid creatures
system.limitations: clothing and small items usually change along with the body; bags, weapons, large gear, and heavy objects stay as they are; in bestial or misty form, some Disciplines and ordinary actions may be unavailable

Eyes of the Beast.description: The vampire's eyes glow red, and darkness stops being an obstacle.
Eyes of the Beast.effect: The character ignores penalties for darkness, including supernatural darkness, and gains +2d10 to Intimidation against mortals if the glowing eyes are visible.

Weight of the Feather.description: The body becomes nearly weightless, keeping its shape but losing its dangerous mass.
Weight of the Feather.effect: Protects against fall damage, collisions, and throws, and may avoid triggering pressure sensors. To activate the power instantly, a Wits + Survival check at difficulty 3 is needed.

Feral Weapons.description: The vampire grows claws, lengthens their fangs, or enhances some other natural weapon.
Feral Weapons.effect: The chosen natural weapon becomes a light piercing weapon with a +2 damage modifier. A bite deals the usual 2 aggravated wounds, but without the targeted-attack penalty; superficial damage from such a bite isn't halved.

Earth Meld.description: The vampire sinks into natural earth, stone, or grass and becomes nearly unreachable until the next night.
Earth Meld.effect: Requires a natural surface, not asphalt or concrete. Sinking in takes a turn; belongings stay on the surface. The vampire dimly perceives their surroundings if not asleep, and can wake from a disturbance just as during day-sleep.

Shapechange.description: The vampire transforms into a chosen animal roughly their own size, most often a wolf, a large cat, or a snake.
Shapechange.effect: The transformation takes a turn. The character gains the physical traits, senses, and limitations of the form: no human speech, no use of hands, and they can only carry items the way an animal could. Other Disciplines' availability is decided by the Storyteller.

Metamorphosis.description: Grants a second bestial form, no longer limited to the vampire's own body size.
Metamorphosis.effect: Works like Shapechange, but lets the character become, for example, a bat, a rat, a small snake, or an unusual insect. Requires already knowing Shapechange.

Mist Form.description: The vampire becomes a visible cloud of mist, almost untouchable by the physical world.
Mist Form.effect: The mist moves at a walking pace, seeps through cracks and pipes, and can neither speak nor make eye contact. Only sunlight, fire, and immaterial supernatural attacks can harm it. The transformation takes up to three turns, sped up by spending additional Blood trials.

The Unfettered Heart.description: The vampire's heart shifts within the chest, making it extremely difficult to stake.
The Unfettered Heart.pool: Strength + Resolve (to break free of paralysis)
The Unfettered Heart.effect: The difficulty to stake the heart outside combat increases by 3, and in combat a triumph is required. If a stake does land, the vampire can attempt a Strength + Resolve check at difficulty 5 plus a Blood trial, once per hour, to expel it, as long as Hunger is below 5.

Potence.description: Potence is the raw supernatural strength of the Blood. It lets a vampire jump impossible distances, break bones with bare hands, tear metal, climb walls with bare fingers, drink blood at a monstrous speed, and strike hard enough to shake the ground. In combat, Potence makes a vampire dangerous even unarmed, and outside combat it helps force open doors, carry heavy loads, and solve problems through sheer physical force.

system.limitations: most effects are obvious from their aftermath: broken walls, crushed bodies, and cracked concrete are hard to explain away; strength doesn't replace the right skill when precision or technique is what's needed

Soaring Leap.description: The vampire jumps, with no running start, distances impossible for a mortal.
Soaring Leap.effect: Jump distance equals Potence × 5 meters, height equals Potence × 3 meters. The power is constant and requires no roll under normal conditions.

Lethal Body.description: The vampire's bare hands become a weapon against mortals.
Lethal Body.effect: At will, the character inflicts aggravated wounds on mortals with unarmed attacks, and ignores one level of armor per point of Potence.

Prowess.description: The Blood fills the muscles with destructive strength for the scene.
Prowess.effect: Adds the Potence rating to unarmed attack damage and to feats of strength. When using a melee weapon, damage increases by half the Potence rating, rounded up.

Brutal Feed.description: The vampire drinks blood through torn vessels at a monstrous speed.
Brutal Feed.effect: A mortal can be drained in a single turn, but takes an aggravated wound for every point of Hunger removed this way. In combat, a successful bite attack is needed first; after that the vampire is guaranteed to drink blood, but the scene looks savage and may raise doubts.

Spark of Rage.description: An amalgam with Presence 3: the vampire ignites a desire for violence in a crowd or target.
Spark of Rage.effect: Adds Potence to checks made to incite violence. Against a vampire, this can trigger a feral frenzy check: Manipulation + Potence opposed by the target's Composure + Intelligence, after which the target resists frenzy at difficulty 3.

Vise Grip.description: The fingers of the hands and feet dig into a surface, letting the vampire climb walls and hang from a ceiling.
Vise Grip.effect: The vampire climbs most non-metallic surfaces without a check, and can hang motionless for a scene. This leaves claw marks and dented gouges; glass and soft metals are up to the Storyteller's discretion.

Draught of Might.description: The vampire's blood temporarily passes part of their Potence to whoever drinks straight from the vein.
Draught of Might.effect: The drinker gains Potence equal to half the vampire's rating (round down), along with any unmixed powers of that level the vampire knows. For vampires, the effect lasts until the next feeding or until Hunger 5.

Earthshock.description: A blow to the ground causes a local tremor that knocks enemies down and breaks the surroundings.
Earthshock.effect: Everyone within a 5-meter radius makes a Dexterity + Athletics check at difficulty 3. The successes determine whether the target stays upright, loses an action, or falls. Forewarned allies get a result one step better. Usable once per scene.

Fist of Caine.description: The vampire's unarmed blows become lethal even against supernatural beings.
Fist of Caine.effect: For the scene, bare-handed Brawl attacks deal aggravated damage to mortals, Kindred, and other supernatural targets alike.

Bardo.description: Bardo is the Children of Osiris's mystical Discipline, created by Osiris and passed down from mentor to student. It's a path of asceticism, meditation, denying the Beast, and holding on to Humanity. Bardo is not Golconda: Golconda accepts the vampire's nature, while Bardo tries, through discipline and enlightenment, to reject the fall into monstrosity.

system.theme: asceticism, Humanity, enlightenment, protection from the Beast, Egyptian mystery
system.humanity_limit: Bardo's level cannot exceed the character's current Humanity.
system.humanity_loss: If Bardo and Humanity are equal, losing Humanity may cause the loss of the corresponding Bardo level. To regain the level, Humanity must be restored and experience spent again.
system.path_limit: The Children of Osiris may only follow the Path of Humanity; other Paths of Enlightenment are closed to them.

Rebirth of Humanity.description: The character meditates for several hours and tries to regain the last lost point of Humanity without spending experience.
Rebirth of Humanity.limits: The power must be used no later than a week after the loss of Humanity. If another point is lost in the meantime, only the most recently lost point can be restored.
Rebirth of Humanity.extra: If the loss of Humanity was accompanied by a failed degeneration roll, a success also restores a point of Conscience, and the resulting derangement fades within a week.

Thoth's Averting Sign.description: The character makes the sign of Thoth over themselves, using the power as a dodge against a directed mystical attack.
Thoth's Averting Sign.effect: Each success neutralizes one success of the attacking supernatural effect, including Disciplines, without taking damage.
Thoth's Averting Sign.limits: Doesn't protect against mystically enhanced physical attacks, such as a blow empowered by Potence.

Gift of Apis.description: Animal blood becomes as nourishing to the character as human blood.
Gift of Apis.effect: Every animal is treated as having a blood supply equal to its number of Health Levels.

Obelisk of Osiris.difficulty: 5–9, based on the place's purity and seclusion
Obelisk of Osiris.description: The character creates a conceptual Obelisk of Osiris, thereby founding a temple.
Obelisk of Osiris.effect: Under the obelisk's shelter, the difficulty of mystical actions, activating Disciplines, and rituals is reduced by 3.
Obelisk of Osiris.maintenance: The creator must visit the obelisk at least once a month. Every week spent away from any obelisk raises the difficulty to resist frenzy by 1.

Paradox.difficulty: the victim's Willpower; for a group, the highest Willpower among the listeners
Paradox.description: The character speaks a phrase or riddle in which the victim glimpses a grand truth and freezes in awe.
Paradox.effect: Victims remain frozen until the end of the scene, or until attacked. After the scene the revelation fades, but the difficulty of any attempt to harm the character increases by 1.

Anubis's Protection.activation: a kiss given to a mortal, usually on the forehead
Anubis's Protection.description: The character asks Anubis to protect a mortal from the Embrace.
Anubis's Protection.duration: 1 month per success; voluntarily lowering Willpower by 1 makes the effect permanent
Anubis's Protection.effect: If the protected mortal is Embraced anyway, they sleep for a full day and wake up alive, weak, and sickeningly ill.
Anubis's Protection.limits: Doesn't protect against ordinary murder. The difficulty of degeneration checks for harming the protected victim increases by 3.

Call of the Dawn.roll: Manipulation + Occult opposed by each victim's Humanity / Path
Call of the Dawn.description: The character traces a sign, making the victims' Beasts react as though dawn had arrived.
Call of the Dawn.effect: If the character wins, all chosen vampires fall into a deep day-sleep for a number of hours equal to the remaining successes.
Call of the Dawn.special: The pool of Followers of Set and bloodlines sharing the same bane is reduced by 2d10. Protective day-sleep rituals function as normal.

Mummification.roll: Willpower opposed by the victim's Willpower
Mummification.description: The immobilized victim is anointed with herbs and incense, wrapped in bandages, and sent into a long torpor.
Mummification.effect: If the character wins, the victim falls into torpor. Once every 100 years, they may attempt to wake with a Willpower check at difficulty 9.
Mummification.limits: The attempt can be repeated every night. A ghoul can be mummified, but cannot wake on their own.

Blessing of Ra.activation: a month of fasting and meditation, followed by a ritual washing before dawn
Blessing of Ra.description: The character briefly experiences sunlight as a mortal would.
Blessing of Ra.effect: Each hour spent under the sun allows a Humanity or virtue check at difficulty 9; on a success, the chosen trait increases by 1.
Blessing of Ra.botch: On a botch, the character cannot attempt the Blessing of Ra again for 10 years.

Spiritus.description: Spiritus is the Ahrimanes' shamanic Discipline, tied to animal spirits, totems, and borrowing bestial traits. It lets them ask spirits for help, summon a totem beast, receive individual animal gifts, and transform into a wild predator.

system.theme: animal spirits, totem, feline freedom, bestial gifts

Aid of Spirits.description: The character calls on animal spirits for advice, signs, or minor help.

Summon Totem Beast.description: The character summons a totem animal or the spiritual embodiment of a beast for help, scouting, or protection.

Gift of the Beast.description: The character gains one bestial gift.

gift_examples.Beaver's Teeth: teeth that gnaw through tough materials

Spirit Hunger.description: The character feeds on spiritual power or uses bestial spirit-bonds to recover and grow stronger.

Wild Beast.description: The Ahrimane transforms into a bipedal feline beast.
Wild Beast.effect: +3 to Strength, +2 to Dexterity and Stamina. Looks drops to 0, Manipulation -3.
Wild Beast.combat: +1d10 to bite damage; claws deal aggravated damage.
Wild Beast.senses: Sees in the dark; animals fear this form.

Daimoinon.description: Daimoinon is the Baali's secret Discipline, a collection of black arts, sinful insight, hellfire, curses, and pacts with the Outer Dark. It isn't merely magic of horror, but a tool of corruption, exploiting weaknesses, and preparing the world for its fall.

system.theme: sin, fear, curses, demons, hellfire, moral decay

Scent of Sin.description: The Baali senses the chief vice, weakness, or secret of a living being or the undead.

success_table.1 success: a weak will, low virtue, or an exploitable weakness
success_table.2 successes: a secret vice or carefully guarded secret
success_table.3 successes: deep trauma or a serious aberration

Base Fear.description: The Baali plays on the victim's known secrets, plunging them into terror.
Base Fear.requirement: The sin must be learned in advance via Scent of Sin or some other means.
Base Fear.effect: 1 success — terror; 2 successes — blind panic as with Rötschreck; 3+ successes — unconsciousness.
Base Fear.vampire_resist: Kindred may resist with a Courage check at a difficulty equal to the Baali's Willpower; if they score more successes, the power fails.

Wreath of Flame.description: Black, cold hellfire appears in the Baali's hand.
Wreath of Flame.damage: 1 aggravated wound for the first Blood point, +1 aggravated wound for each additional Blood point.
Wreath of Flame.maintenance: If the Baali stops spending blood to sustain it, the flame goes out the next turn.
Wreath of Flame.attack: The flame is thrown as a physical attack; the victim may dodge.
Wreath of Flame.rotschreck: Vampires check for Rötschreck as from ordinary fire of the same size.

Psychomachia.description: The Baali forces the victim to confront an embodiment of their darkest fears.
Psychomachia.requirement: The victim's secret or weakness must be known.
Psychomachia.roll: the victim rolls their weakest virtue
Psychomachia.effect: On a failure, the victim fights a nightmarish antagonist invisible to everyone else: a father, a dead loved one, a childhood bogeyman, or their own Beast.
Psychomachia.botch: On a botch, the victim falls into frenzy, panic, or submits to their inner demons.
Psychomachia.damage: The damage is illusory; on 'death,' the victim falls into a stupor or torpor, at the Storyteller's discretion.

Curse.description: The Baali lays a curse, splitting successes between duration and severity.

severity_examples.2: +2 to the difficulty of Strength, or a ban on boosting Strength with blood
severity_examples.3: friends stop trusting the victim; social and frenzy complications
severity_examples.4: sterility; the vampire can't grant the Embrace or create ghouls
severity_examples.5: every failure counts as a botch

Curse.dismiss: Whoever cast the curse can lift it at any time.

The Bargain.description: The Baali strikes a deal with the Devil and gains a supernatural gift along with a disfiguring mark.
The Bargain.effect: Most often the first gift is protection from fire, but with the Storyteller's permission, any power of equal strength is possible.
The Bargain.cost: Each pact changes the body and soul: bronze skin, wings, claws, horns, and so on.
The Bargain.repeatable: Can be acquired multiple times as a senior power.
The Bargain.limits: Doesn't remove vulnerability to True Faith and sacred symbols, vulnerability to the sun, or the need to drink blood.

Summon the Herald of Tophet.description: The Baali summons a servant of the demonic Masters.
Summon the Herald of Tophet.stats: Typically attributes 10/7/3, abilities 15, Willpower 8, Disciplines 10, Fortitude at least 3, regenerates 1 wound at minimum every turn.
Summon the Herald of Tophet.note: The herald temporarily obeys its summoner, but the text provides no guaranteed method of banishment.

Moral Corruption.description: The Baali poisons an area with envy, lies, hatred, and fanaticism.
Moral Corruption.success_allocation: Successes are split between area and intensity; at least 1 success is required in each category.

intensity_table.4: hate crimes and bloodshed in the streets

Summon the Great Beast.description: A theoretical ritual to open a gate into the Outer Dark and summon an entity capable of changing the world of the chronicle.
Summon the Great Beast.system: Not intended as an ordinary character power. It's a story tool: a threat to be prevented, or the start of a demonic apocalypse.
Summon the Great Beast.cost: Long preparation, mass sacrifices, rituals lasting many hours, and the high priest's Willpower dropping to 0 at the climax.

Vicissitude.description: Vicissitude is the signature Discipline of clan Tzimisce, letting a vampire reshape living and non-living flesh. Unlike Protean, Vicissitude isn't limited to the user's own body: an adept can sculpt mortals, ghouls, and younger-Generation vampires, turning the body into material for art, torture, disguise, enhancement, or monstrous rebirth.

system.theme: flesh, bone, mutation, body art, monstrousness, transformation
system.core_rule: Using Vicissitude usually requires direct, unimpeded touch. The vampire literally sculpts flesh and bone with their hands, the way a sculptor works clay.
system.permanence: Changes made to the body of a mortal, ghoul, or younger-Generation vampire are permanent. Older-Generation vampires can shed the effects of Vicissitude the same way they heal aggravated wounds.
system.limitations: Vicissitude cannot overcome ancient clan banes. The Nosferatu always 'heal' from changes that increase attractiveness, and Gangrel atavisms can never be removed for good.
system.body_crafts: The Medicine specialty of 'body arts' is useful for effective use of Vicissitude. It covers anatomy, flaying, bone carving, embalming, taxidermy, tattooing, piercing, and the practice of artistic body modification.
system.rumor: Some Kindred consider Vicissitude not a normal Discipline but a disease, but the Tzimisce prefer not to explain the truth to outsiders.

Malleable Visage.cost: 1 Blood point per body part changed
Malleable Visage.description: The vampire alters details of their own appearance: height, build, voice, facial features, skin tone, and other cosmetic features.
Malleable Visage.effect: The changes are minor and cosmetic. Height can be increased or decreased by no more than 30 centimeters from the original.

impersonation.effect: Lets the character take on the appearance of a specific individual. A flawless impersonation requires 5 successes; fewer successes leave more noticeable flaws.

appearance_increase.extra_cost: 1 additional Blood point for each point of Looks above the current rating
appearance_increase.effect: Lets the character raise their Looks rating.
appearance_increase.botch: On a botch, the vampire's Looks permanently drops by 1.

Fleshcraft.description: The vampire reshapes soft flesh: skin, muscle, fat, and cartilage. This power lets them radically alter a victim's body, create deformities, improve appearance, or reinforce flesh.
Fleshcraft.activation: If the victim resists, the vampire must grapple them first.
Fleshcraft.difficulty_note: Difficulty 5 suits crude, simple changes. Difficulty 9 suits precise, elegant, and complex transformations.
Fleshcraft.appearance_change: Each success raises or lowers the victim's Looks by 1. Raising Looks is as difficult as with Malleable Visage; lowering it is usually easier, difficulty 5, though especially inspired deformities can be harder.

body_reinforcement.effect: The character redistributes skin, muscle, and fat, reinforcing the body. Each success increases the soak check pool by 1d10, drawn from Strength points or Health Levels, the character's choice.

Bonecraft.description: The vampire manipulates bone as easily as soft flesh. Combined with Fleshcraft, this power can change a body beyond recognition.

damage_use.effect: Without using Fleshcraft, the power can directly deal damage. Each success inflicts 1 aggravated wound, as bones deform, pierce, and tear through flesh.

bone_claws.effect: The vampire can grow bone claws on themselves or another being.
bone_claws.self_damage: The recipient of the claws takes 1 aggravated wound.

bone_spines.effect: The vampire can cover a body in protective bone spines.
bone_spines.self_damage: The recipient of the spines takes 5 aggravated wounds, minus 1 wound per success.
bone_spines.botch: A botch kills a mortal, and sends a vampire into torpor.
bone_spines.defense: An unarmed opponent attacking a spine-covered character in melee takes damage equal to their own Strength, aggravated. They avoid this damage if they score at least 3 successes on their attack.
bone_spines.grappling: The spines add 2d10 to the damage pool in a grapple, clinch, or throw.

rib_heart_pierce.requirement: a minimum of 5 successes on the Strength + Medicine check
rib_heart_pierce.effect: The vampire bends the victim's ribs inward, piercing the heart.
rib_heart_pierce.vampire: A vampire loses half their available Blood points: the pierced heart expels vitae outward.

Horrid Form.description: The vampire transforms into an enormous, lethal monster: their height grows to roughly two and a half meters, their skin becomes covered in dark chitin, their arms lengthen, their muscles swell with strength, their fingers become claws, their face becomes a nightmarish muzzle, and their spine becomes a sharp bony ridge.
Horrid Form.social_penalty: All Social attributes drop to 0, except when interacting with other vampires also in Horrid Form.
Horrid Form.intimidation: For Intimidation, the character may use Strength as their Social attribute.
Horrid Form.combat: The Brawl damage pool increases by 1d10, thanks to claws, spines, and bony growths.

Blood Form.cost: depends on the body part transformed
Blood Form.description: The vampire turns their entire body, or parts of it, into sentient vampiric blood.
Blood Form.effect: The blood retains the properties of ordinary vampire vitae: it can be used to feed, create ghouls, and bind vampires with a blood bond.
Blood Form.reform: To return the blood to a body part, the character only needs to touch it.
Blood Form.loss: If the blood is used or destroyed, the vampire can regrow the lost body part by spending the same number of Blood points that were used in the transformation.
Blood Form.full_form: If the vampire becomes entirely blood, they're immune to physical damage, but remain vulnerable to fire and sunlight.
