Blood Form.movement: In blood form, the vampire can flow across horizontal surfaces, climb walls, and seep through tiny openings.
Blood Form.disciplines: Mental Disciplines can be used, as long as they don't require eye contact or speech.
Blood Form.perception: The vampire perceives their surroundings; the center of perception is in the largest pool of blood.
Blood Form.fear: If the blood washes over a mortal or animal, the victim makes a Courage check at difficulty 8 or flees in panic.
Blood Form.final_death: If all of the blood the vampire turned into is drunk or destroyed, final death occurs.

Winged Hunter.description: The vampire transforms into a giant, monstrous bat with a mouth full of needle-like teeth and enormous leathery wings in place of arms.
Winged Hunter.base_effect: The character gains all the benefits and drawbacks of Horrid Form.
Winged Hunter.flight: The wings allow flight at speeds up to 40 km/h.
Winged Hunter.hands_limit: Wings replace the character's hands. They can carry, but not use, objects no larger or heavier than they could otherwise hold in their hands.

wing_claws.effect: The character can grow sharp claws at the tips of the wings.
wing_claws.damage: Wing claw damage equals Strength + 2, aggravated.

Winged Hunter.senses: The difficulty of hearing-related perception checks is reduced by 2. The difficulty of Wits and Awareness checks related to sight increases by 1.
Winged Hunter.fear: The form can send mortals and impressionable vampires into a panic.

Acidic Blood.description: The vampire's blood becomes a sticky, caustic acid. Any blood it absorbs likewise turns to acid.
Acidic Blood.effect: Every point of blood that touches anything other than the character's own body deals 5d10 aggravated damage.
Acidic Blood.combat_splash: If the vampire is wounded in melee, the acidic blood may splash onto the opponent. The opponent must pass a Dexterity + Athletics check to avoid being hit.
Acidic Blood.limits: Splashing is only possible if the opponent attacked with a multiple action and split their check pool.
Acidic Blood.side_effect: The vampire loses the ability to create ghouls or grant the Embrace, since their blood is destructive.
Acidic Blood.anti_diablerie: Diablerists risk death trying to drink such blood.

Cocoon.description: The vampire creates a translucent cocoon of blood and transformed bodily fluids around themselves. Within a few minutes, it hardens into a tough white shell.
Cocoon.effect: The cocoon fully protects against sunlight and grants a soak pool against any damage, including aggravated, equal to twice the character's Stamina.
Cocoon.duration: The vampire may remain inside for as long as they wish.
Cocoon.release: At the character's will, the cocoon softens and turns into a pliable, bloody mass.
Cocoon.disciplines: Mental Disciplines can be used inside the cocoon, as long as they don't require eye contact or active gestures.
Cocoon.limits: The vampire can only enclose themselves in the cocoon.

Dragon's Breath.description: The vampire exhales a stream of roaring flame, like the dragons and serpents of Old World legend.
Dragon's Breath.effect: Everyone in the area takes 2 aggravated wounds.
Dragon's Breath.fire: The flame can ignite flammable objects. Those who take damage may catch fire as from ordinary flame.
Dragon's Breath.self_risk: The breath itself is harmless to the vampire, but the resulting fire can become a deadly trap.

Shelter in the Earth's Womb.description: The vampire sinks into the earth and literally dissolves into it, becoming almost completely unreachable from the outside world.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.effect: While the power is active, the body cannot be found or dug up. Only a very powerful explosion can harm the vampire.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.duration: The vampire may remain in the earth for as long as they wish.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.senses: From sunset to dawn, the character mystically sees and hears everything happening around them.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.disciplines: Mental Disciplines can be used, as long as they don't require eye contact or physical touch.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.communication: The vampire can communicate telepathically with any being standing on the ground in which they've made their refuge.
Shelter in the Earth's Womb.lore_note: The existence of this power troubles the Tzimisce: some elders still aren't certain that their clan's diablerie of its Antediluvian truly succeeded.

Necromancy.description: Necromancy is not just a Discipline, but a school of blood sorcery devoted to controlling the souls of the dead, ghosts, corpses, the Shroud, and the powers of death. Unlike ordinary linear Disciplines, Necromancy is divided into paths and rituals. Most modern necromancers first learn the Sepulchre Path, and then master additional paths.

system.type: non-linear discipline / school of blood sorcery
system.theme: death, ghosts, corpses, the Underworld, the Shroud, rituals, decay, entropy
system.structure: Necromancy consists of paths and rituals. The primary path advances together with the character's Necromancy level. Other paths are purchased separately as secondary paths.
system.path_rules: To learn a second path, a necromancer must reach level 3 in their primary path. To learn a third path, they must reach level 5 in their primary path.
system.activation_note: Activation checks vary depending on the specific path and power.
system.rituals_note: Necromantic rituals work more slowly than path powers, but produce long-lasting mystical effects. They often require remains, corpses, objects belonging to the dead, and other grim components.

Sepulchre Path.description: The Sepulchre Path lets a necromancer see, summon, control, bind, and torment ghosts. It's the most common path of Necromancy, and the usual starting point for beginning necromancers.
Sepulchre Path.special_rule: If a power is used against a ghost near a place or object important to them, the activation check's difficulty is reduced by 2. This could be the place of death, remains, a beloved possession, a childhood room, or some other meaningful object.

Ghost Sight.description: The necromancer gains the ability to see and sense ghosts present in the world of the living.
Ghost Sight.effect: The character sees ghosts as translucent shadows, hears their whispers and moans, and feels the cold of their touch and a musty odor. Ghosts cannot be mistaken for living people.
Ghost Sight.visual: The necromancer's eyes glow with a pale blue light, visible only to the dead.
Ghost Sight.botch: On a botch, until the end of the scene the vampire sees only the dead, and perceives the world of the living as vague shadows. They take +3 to the difficulty of visual perception checks.
Ghost Sight.ghost_notice: To notice the glowing eyes, a ghost makes an Awareness + Alertness check at difficulty 7.

Summon Ghost.difficulty: the ghost's Willpower, or 7, whichever is higher
Summon Ghost.duration: until the end of the scene
Summon Ghost.description: The necromancer summons a ghost from the Underworld and can speak with it.
requirements: The ghost's name must be known, or a mental image of them must be held.
The mental image can be obtained through Ghost Sight, the Spirit's Eye, Auspex, or similar powers.
The summoning is performed near a place or object the ghost was in contact with during life.
The ghost's remains reduce the check's difficulty by 1.
Summon Ghost.effect: On a success, the ghost appears and remains within the necromancer's sight. A successful summoning also confirms that the ghost exists.
Summon Ghost.limits: Not every deceased soul can be summoned. Souls that have found peace, destroyed ghosts, spirits that are too weak, the ghosts of vampires who achieved Golconda, and victims of diablerie are usually unreachable.
Summon Ghost.control: A summoned ghost cannot voluntarily leave the necromancer's sight, but may act as it sees fit, including attacking.
Summon Ghost.banish: The necromancer may spend a Willpower point to banish the ghost, provided the summoning wasn't botched.
Summon Ghost.botch: On a botch, the wrong ghost arrives — often a hungry spirit hostile to the necromancer.

Compel the Ghost.roll: Manipulation + Occult against the ghost's Willpower
Compel the Ghost.description: The necromancer temporarily bends a ghost to their will.
requirements: The ghost must be found or summoned.
The ghost's name or image must be known.
An object the ghost was in contact with during life is needed.
Compel the Ghost.effect: If the necromancer wins the opposed check, the effect depends on the remaining successes.

success_table.1 success: The ghost carries out one simple, safe order, or answers one question honestly within the limits of its knowledge.
success_table.2 successes: The 1-success effect can be used twice, or the necromancer can give one simple order involving possible danger.
success_table.3 successes: The 1-success effect can be used three times, the necromancer can give one complex, dangerous order, or force the ghost to carry out one simple, safe order for a month.
success_table.4 successes: The 1-success effect can be used four times, the 2-success effect twice, the necromancer can give an extremely risky order, or enslave the ghost for a month for simple, safe orders. Voluntarily lowering Willpower by 1 extends the service to a year and a day.
success_table.5+ successes: Five successes can be split between effects, or the necromancer may give any order the ghost is capable of carrying out within a month, even if it leads to betrayal, murder, or destruction.

Compel the Ghost.ghost_resistance: The ghost may stall for time by spending Willpower, if the chosen effect allows it.
Compel the Ghost.if_ghost_wins: If the ghost wins, the necromancer loses 1 Willpower point for each of the ghost's remaining successes.
Compel the Ghost.tie: On a tie, the check is repeated each turn until one side wins.
Compel the Ghost.botch: A botch by the necromancer makes the ghost immune to their Necromancy until the end of the scene. A botch by the ghost counts as 5 successes for the necromancer.

Bind the Ghost.description: The necromancer ties a ghost to a place, or seals it inside an object.
requirements: The necromancer must be at the place of binding, or touching the object.
The ghost must be lured or summoned to the site of activation.
The ghost's remains reduce difficulty by 1, to a minimum of 3.
Bind the Ghost.effect: Each success holds the ghost for one night.
Bind the Ghost.extended_duration: Spending a Willpower point extends the duration to one week per success. Voluntarily lowering Willpower by 1 extends it to a year and a day per success.
Bind the Ghost.object_binding: If the ghost is sealed in an object rather than a place, the difficulty increases by 1.
Bind the Ghost.escape: To break free before the end of the scene, the ghost makes an extended Willpower check at difficulty 9 and must accumulate 4 successes. Each attempt deals it 1 aggravated wound.
Bind the Ghost.danger: If these wounds bring the ghost to the point of destruction, it's pulled back into the Underworld and meets final death.

Torment the Ghost.description: The necromancer harms a ghost's ectoplasmic body while remaining in the world of the living.
Torment the Ghost.effect: The character strikes the ghost and makes a check. Each success deals 1 aggravated wound.
Torment the Ghost.advantage: The necromancer remains in the world of the living, so the ghost cannot respond with a normal attack.
Torment the Ghost.result: If the ghost is brought to the point of destruction, it falls into the Underworld. It isn't permanently destroyed, but for a month it cannot approach the Shroud or return to the world of the living.

Cenotaph Path.description: The Cenotaph Path lets a necromancer find and create links between the world of the living and the Shadowlands: ghostly traces, anchors, thinning points in the Shroud, and new tethers for spirits.

Touch of Death.description: The necromancer touches an object or being and determines whether a ghost was recently nearby, or whether ghostly powers affected the target.

success_table.1 success: A trace from the last turn; activation of ghostly powers.
success_table.2 successes: A trace from the last three turns; activation of ghostly powers.
success_table.3 successes: A trace from the last hour; a ghost's touch and activation of ghostly powers.
success_table.4 successes: A trace from the last day; a ghost's touch and activation of ghostly powers.
success_table.5+ successes: A trace from the last week; presence, touch, and activation of ghostly powers.

Touch of Death.effect: If a ghost is hiding inside the object or individual, the necromancer senses it regardless of the number of successes. This sense can be used as a mental image for Sepulchre Path powers.
Touch of Death.failure: On a failure, there's no information.
Touch of Death.botch: On a botch, the necromancer gets false information.

Seek the Anchor.activation: 3 turns studying the object or individual
Seek the Anchor.description: The necromancer determines whether an object is a ghost's anchor.
Seek the Anchor.effect: On a success, the character learns whether the object is tied to a ghost. More than two successes reveal the ghost's identity. If the necromancer already knows that ghost, one success is enough to confirm the connection.
Seek the Anchor.botch: On a botch, the character can never again successfully study this object with Seek the Anchor.

Defile the Grave.description: The necromancer senses the state of the Shroud at a chosen location.
Defile the Grave.effect: On a success, the character learns whether the Shroud is thinner than usual, thicker than usual, or unremarkable.
Defile the Grave.extra_success: More than two successes reveal whether the Shroud's state is the result of deliberate interference.
Defile the Grave.failure: On a failure, there's no information, and the activation can't be repeated here until the end of the scene.
Defile the Grave.botch: On a botch, the character is gripped by feverish trembling and despair, and loses 1 Willpower point.

Death Knell.description: The necromancer senses when someone nearby dies and becomes a ghost.
Death Knell.passive: The character can sense the appearance of a new ghost within a kilometer's radius, though many necromancers suppress this sense.
Death Knell.effect: A check reveals the direction and distance to where the ghost appeared.

success_table.3 successes: An exact direction and approximate distance, accurate to within half a kilometer.
success_table.5 successes: The ghost's location, accurate to within half a meter.

Death Knell.botch: On a botch, the information is wrong.
Death Knell.note: Storms in the Underworld, magical disturbances, and other supernatural phenomena can drown out the Death Knell.

Ephemeral Tether.description: The necromancer creates a temporary anchor for a ghost out of an ordinary object or individual.
Ephemeral Tether.activation: The character sprinkles the object with their vitae. If the object is a living being, they must drink its blood.
Ephemeral Tether.effect: On a success, the object becomes a temporary anchor for one ghost. If the necromancer knows that ghost's name or image, the object can become an anchor specifically for them, regardless of where the spirit currently is. Otherwise, the necromancer must see or sense the ghost.
Ephemeral Tether.anchor_effect: The anchor can be detected with Necromancy, used to boost checks against the bound ghost, and makes it easier for the ghost to interact with it. A ghost can heal while inside its anchor.
Ephemeral Tether.danger: If the anchor is destroyed, the ghost is banished to the distant reaches of the Underworld.
Ephemeral Tether.duration: Each success maintains the anchor's properties for one night; with an additional Willpower point, for one week; with a voluntary 1-point reduction to Willpower, for a year and a day.

Path of Bones.description: The Path of Bones works with corpses and the return of departed souls to the world of the living — temporarily or permanently. It animates dead flesh, creates zombies, and prepares bodies for possession.

zombie_stats.rules: Zombies usually act last, don't dodge, have no mind or consciousness, and aren't affected by Dominate or Presence. Health penalties don't apply to them unless the damage comes from the claws or fangs of supernatural beings. Zombies don't heal damage.

Spasms.description: The necromancer makes a dead body move briefly: twitch, sit up, open its eyes, raise a hand.
Spasms.effect: The more successes, the more complex the movement. 1 success — a simple, brief movement. 5 successes — a prolonged, complex movement with an activation condition.
Spasms.limits: This power cannot be used to attack or deal damage.

Zombie Servants.description: The necromancer animates dead bodies as mindless servants for simple tasks.
Zombie Servants.effect: Each success animates one body. A zombie carries out a simple task until it fulfills the order, becomes an ordinary corpse again, or is destroyed.
Zombie Servants.orders: Orders must be simple and unambiguous: dig, carry, sweep, copy a text. Complex intellectual tasks are impossible.
Zombie Servants.limits: Zombie servants don't attack or defend themselves. If interfered with, they try to return to their task.

Rotting Horde.cost: 1 Willpower point + up to 1 Blood point per zombie
Rotting Horde.description: The necromancer creates combat zombies for guarding and attacking.
Rotting Horde.effect: Each success lets the character spend 1 Blood point to animate one corpse as a fighting zombie. Extra successes are wasted if the blood isn't spent.
Rotting Horde.orders: Zombies only understand simple combat and sentry orders: guard, attack anyone who enters, kill them.
Rotting Horde.duration: Once given an order, zombies can wait for years or centuries. Once the flesh decays, they continue to exist as skeletons with the same traits.

Soul Expulsion.description: The necromancer expels a living being's soul from its body, temporarily turning it into something like a ghost.
Soul Expulsion.effect: If the necromancer wins, each remaining success expels the soul for 1 hour. The body remains alive, but unconscious.
Soul Expulsion.use: This power can prepare a body as a vessel for Spectral Possession.
Soul Expulsion.limits: Doesn't work on supernatural beings other than ghouls, unless the target is dead. For a vampire, this means final death.

Spectral Possession.roll: usually not required if the soul consents; possessing a vampire's body requires an opposed Willpower check
Spectral Possession.description: The necromancer settles a soul into a recently deceased or specially prepared body.
requirements: The dead body must have died no more than half an hour ago.
Alternatively, a living body emptied by Soul Expulsion can be used.
The soul being moved must do so willingly.
Spectral Possession.vampire_body: To place a soul into a vampire's body, this must be done before the body decays, and requires scoring 5 successes in an opposed Willpower check against the deceased Kindred's Willpower.
Spectral Possession.effect: The soul gains a temporary or permanent fleshly vessel. The dead body continues to decay and usually becomes unusable within a week.
Spectral Possession.abilities: The soul uses the new body's physical abilities and its own mental abilities. The soul's old physical abilities and the new body's mental abilities aren't used.

Path of the Corpse.description: The Path of the Corpse uses the vampire's dual nature as a living corpse. It strengthens or weakens the properties of a dead body: numbness, a corpse-like appearance, resistance to pain and sunlight, and even a temporary semblance of life.
Path of the Corpse.mortis_note: The Cappadocians often chose the Path of the Corpse or the Path of Decay as their primary path. The Lamia usually used the Path of the Four Humors. The Harbingers of Skulls, according to rumor, fused the Path of the Corpse and the Path of Decay into a combined Mortis path, but usually still start with the Sepulchre Path regardless.

Mask of Death.roll: none for oneself; Stamina + Medicine against another vampire
Mask of Death.difficulty: 3 + the victim's Stamina, when used against another vampire
Mask of Death.duration: until sunset, or until the character turns the power off
Mask of Death.description: The necromancer makes themselves or another vampire look like a corpse.
Mask of Death.self_effect: The flesh pales and withers. Dexterity and Looks drop by 2, but Dexterity never below 1, Looks never below 0. Intimidation gains +2d10.
Mask of Death.corpse_disguise: If the character stays motionless, 5 successes on an Awareness + Medicine check at difficulty 7 are needed to tell them apart from an ordinary corpse.
Mask of Death.target_effect: To use this on another vampire, the necromancer must touch the target, spend blood, and make the check.

Chill of the Grave.description: The necromancer sinks into deathly numbness and detachment.
Chill of the Grave.effect: Until the end of the scene, Health penalties don't apply to the character.
Chill of the Grave.mental: The pool for checks resisting mental influence increases by 1d10, but the pool for the character's own checks to mentally influence others decreases by 1d10.
Chill of the Grave.limits: Doesn't protect against the Beast. Frenzy checks proceed as normal.

Curse of Life.description: The necromancer imposes the worst aspects of a living body onto a vampire: sweat, hunger, thirst, weakness, and bodily distractions.
Curse of Life.effect: The target takes +2 to the difficulty of all checks.
Curse of Life.willpower_resist: The target may spend a Willpower point to ignore the distracting effects until the end of the scene.
Curse of Life.blood_limit: The target cannot boost Physical attributes with blood, even by spending Willpower.
Curse of Life.limits: The target gains none of the benefits of a living body: the sun, true faith, and other threats still affect them as normal.

Gift of the Dead.description: The necromancer temporarily becomes closer to a true corpse than to a vampire.
Gift of the Dead.effect: While the power is active, sacred relics and hallowed ground have no effect on the character. They're immune to frenzy and Rötschreck.
Gift of the Dead.sun_fire_stake: Sunlight deals superficial damage instead of aggravated, and only with direct exposure on bare skin. Fire deals aggravated damage instead of damage that risks final death. A stake to the heart doesn't paralyze, and is treated as an ordinary wound.
Gift of the Dead.end_danger: If the power ends while the character is exposed to sunlight, fire, a stake, or a holy site, all of those effects trigger immediately.

Gift of Life.description: The necromancer temporarily gains some of the benefits of mortal life: food, drink, sex, staying awake during the day, and protection from the sun.
Gift of Life.activation: Blood must be spent continuously, as fast as Generation allows. The vampire may drink a victim's blood during activation, up to 3 points per turn.
Gift of Life.effect: The sun becomes less dangerous: the difficulty of soak checks against it is halved, and thick, covering clothing can provide full protection. Fire is as dangerous as it is to a mortal. Frenzy and Rötschreck are still possible, but the difficulty of those checks is halved.
Gift of Life.life_benefits: The character can enjoy human pleasures and stay awake after sunrise with no checks.
Gift of Life.vampire_traits: The character is still considered a vampire for superficial damage, holy sites, true faith, a stake to the heart, and the nature of blood. Some Disciplines may still function, at the Storyteller's discretion.
Gift of Life.botch: A botch can lead to catastrophic consequences: the involuntary Embrace of a victim, the character's death, or some other severe effect.
Gift of Life.aftereffect: For the 6 nights after the power ends, the difficulty of all frenzy checks increases by 3.

Path of Ash.description: The Path of Ash lets a necromancer see the Underworld, speak with its inhabitants, interact with objects beyond the Shroud, physically cross into the world of the dead, and alter the Shroud itself. It's one of the most dangerous paths of Necromancy.

The Spirit's Eye.description: The necromancer sees through the Shroud into the Shadowlands.
The Spirit's Eye.effect: The character sees the Underworld's ghostly structures, objects, landscapes, and ghosts.

Tongue of the Dead.description: The necromancer can freely speak with ghosts and hear their answers.
Tongue of the Dead.effect: Once activated, the conversation requires no additional effort from either the necromancer or the ghost.

Hand of the Beyond.description: The necromancer interacts with ghosts and objects beyond the Shroud as though they were material.
Hand of the Beyond.effect: The character can attack ghosts, use ghostly objects, walk through ghostly buildings, and exist in both worlds at once.
Hand of the Beyond.danger: The necromancer becomes material to the inhabitants of the Underworld too, including those who wish to harm them.
Hand of the Beyond.extension: Each additional scene beyond the first requires 1 Blood point.

Ex Nihilo.description: The necromancer is physically transported into the Underworld through a door drawn in chalk or blood.
Ex Nihilo.activation: The door can be drawn in advance on a suitable surface. On a success, it opens, and the character finds themselves in the world of the dead.
Ex Nihilo.effect: In the Underworld, the vampire exists as a material ghost. They retain their Health, but are vulnerable to powers and weapons capable of harming ghosts.
Ex Nihilo.living_world: They can pass through material objects of the living world, taking 1 unavoidable aggravated wound each time. This incorporeality lasts a number of turns equal to their Stamina.
Ex Nihilo.return: To return, the character makes a Stamina + Occult check at difficulty 6. If they've gone too deep, the Storyteller may require them to find a place where the Shroud is thin.
Ex Nihilo.danger: If the vampire is destroyed in the Underworld, they disincorporate and cease to exist.
Ex Nihilo.feeding: In the Underworld, a vampire cannot feed on ghosts without Devour the Spirit.

Sway the Shroud.description: The necromancer weakens or strengthens the Shroud, making it easier or harder for ghosts to cross between worlds.
Sway the Shroud.effect: Each success lowers or raises the difficulty of Shroud-crossing checks by 1.
Sway the Shroud.decay: After activation, the Shroud returns to its previous state at a rate of 1 point of difficulty per hour.

Path of Decay.description: The Path of Decay governs decomposition, rot, disease, and the breakdown of mortal matter. Its philosophy grew out of observing how time turns bodies to dust, stone to sand, and living flesh to rot.

Crumble the Shell.description: The necromancer turns a corpse into a loose pile of dust.
Crumble the Shell.activation: The character sprinkles the corpse with their vitae.
Crumble the Shell.effect: On a success, the corpse turns to ash within 5 minus the number of successes minutes.

Rigor Mortis.description: The necromancer induces the effect of rigor mortis in a living or undead body.
Rigor Mortis.effect: Each success paralyzes the victim for 1 turn. The effect is similar to a stake to the heart.
Rigor Mortis.resistance: The victim may attempt a Willpower check at difficulty 7. Two successes let them overcome the stiffness for 1 turn. On a failure, the victim takes 1 superficial wound, and the effect persists.
Rigor Mortis.failure: On a failure, the Willpower point is wasted.
Rigor Mortis.botch: On a botch, the victim becomes immune to the Path of Decay for 24 hours.

Wither.roll: Dexterity + Brawl, if the victim is avoiding the touch
Wither.description: The necromancer desiccates a chosen limb or body part of the victim.
Wither.effect: On contact, the victim takes 2 aggravated wounds. If they don't soak them off, the chosen body part is disabled until the damage heals.

targets.an arm: Strength in that arm becomes 0; nothing heavier than a couple hundred grams can be held; Potence cannot be applied with that arm.
targets.a leg: Normal weight-bearing is impossible; the victim suffers a limp.
targets.an eye or ear: +1 to the difficulty of the corresponding perception checks.
targets.the face: Looks decreases by 1 for each wound inflicted.

Wither.limits: This power doesn't affect internal organs and cannot instantly kill.
Wither.healing: Vampires heal the damage as aggravated wounds. Mortals are usually left maimed without supernatural healing.

Plague of the Dead.roll: Intelligence + Medicine against the victim's Stamina + Fortitude
Plague of the Dead.difficulty: 6 for the necromancer; the necromancer's Willpower for the victim
Plague of the Dead.description: The necromancer infects the victim with a mystical disease that afflicts both the living and the undead.
Plague of the Dead.infection: Mortals are infected after a few hours near the sick. A vampire is infected if they drink the sick person's blood.
effect: The victim's Strength and Wits are halved, rounded down.
A vampire spends 1 additional Blood point to wake from day-sleep.
A mortal loses 1 Health Level per day.
After feeding, a vampire makes a Self-Control / Instinct check at difficulty 8. On a failure, the blood isn't absorbed and is expelled. The same happens with food for mortals.
Plague of the Dead.recovery: Each sunset, the victim may attempt a Stamina check at a difficulty of 10 minus the number of sunsets since infection. On a success, the disease stops, feeding returns to normal, and the reduced traits return at a rate of 1 point per day.

Decay the Flesh.description: The necromancer turns the power of decay against a vampire, reducing their flesh to dust.
Decay the Flesh.activation: The character infuses their vitae with the energy of decay, and over the next few turns must sprinkle the victim with a large amount of this blood.
Decay the Flesh.effect: Each success deals 1 wound that risks final death. Each such wound destroys roughly one-eighth of the victim's flesh.
Decay the Flesh.aftermath: If the victim survives, the lost body parts must be slowly regrown along with healing the aggravated damage.
Decay the Flesh.material: The resulting ash has mystical properties and can be valuable to mages and sorcerers.

Path of the Four Humors.description: The Path of the Four Humors is based on ancient and medieval philosophy of the bodily fluids: blood, phlegm, yellow bile, and black bile. Ancient necromancers believed that in vampires the balance of humors is static, and therefore can be altered by will. The path is considered a secret of the Lamia.

Ghostly Whisper.description: The necromancer whispers the victim's name into their ear and poisons their mind with bile.
Ghostly Whisper.effect: On a failed check, the victim suffers from nightmares in their sleep and indistinct voices while awake.
Ghostly Whisper.penalty: The victim's pool for any checks is reduced by 1d10. The difficulty of Rötschreck checks may increase by 1, at the Storyteller's discretion.

Kiss of the Dark Mother.activation: a reflexive action before a bite attack
Kiss of the Dark Mother.description: The necromancer mixes vitae with black bile, turning their fangs into a source of poisoned wounds.
Kiss of the Dark Mother.effect: If the next bite attack hits, the damage check's result is doubled. The soak check against the aggravated damage proceeds as normal.
Kiss of the Dark Mother.duration: The power remains active until a successful bite, or until the character spends a turn spitting out the poison.
Kiss of the Dark Mother.limits: Doesn't interfere with drinking blood, and adds no damage to the Kiss itself.

Dark Humors.description: The necromancer secretes a chosen humor through their skin, expresses it into a drink, or dissolves it in vitae.
Dark Humors.effect: The victim must pass a Stamina check at difficulty 8 to avoid the effect of the chosen humor.

humors.Phlegm: The victim is overcome with apathy; the pool for all of their checks is reduced by 2d10 until the end of the scene.
humors.Blood: Blood clotting worsens. On the turn after taking an aggravated wound or one that risks final death, the victim takes 1 more wound of the same type. The altered vitae cannot create ghouls or form the basis of a blood bond.
humors.Black Bile: The victim takes aggravated wounds equal to the character's Stamina. The soak check works as normal; armor offers no protection.
humors.Yellow Bile: The victim is overcome with melancholy and visions of death. Until the end of the scene they cannot spend Willpower, and take +2 to the difficulty of Willpower checks.

Dark Humors.aftereffect: After secreting the humor, the character experiences the opposite emotional state: after blood, depression; after yellow bile, calm; after black bile, mirth; after phlegm, irritation.

Spectral Veil.description: The necromancer drinks blood from the body of a mortal who died 24–72 hours ago, and wraps themselves in an unseen shroud of the Veil.
Spectral Veil.activation: 5 points of dead blood must be drunk and spent. The power only activates once the entire amount has been spent.
effect: The soak check pool increases by 2d10; these dice work even against damage that risks final death, as if they were two points of Fortitude.
The character can tell at a glance who is healthy, sick, wounded, undead, a ghoul, or mortal.
The character may roll Manipulation + Occult to speak with a ghost they've noticed.
Spectral Veil.ghost_talk_difficulty: The difficulty of the conversation depends on how close the place is to death: around 5 in a cemetery, 7 or higher in a cozy living room.
Spectral Veil.danger: The character becomes more noticeable and accessible to ghosts.

Black Pestilence.description: The necromancer exhales a cloud of black mist, filled with black bile, despair, and visions of death.
Black Pestilence.avoidance: Victims in the area may roll Dexterity + Athletics to leap clear in time, if they have an action available.
Black Pestilence.effect: Everyone caught in the cloud makes a Willpower check: difficulty 8 for mortals, 7 for everyone else. They need more successes than the necromancer.
Black Pestilence.mortal_failure: On a failure, a mortal tries to kill themselves on the next turn, by the most effective means available. If stopped, they'll try again at the first opportunity. The effect lasts until the end of the scene, and may recur.
Black Pestilence.vampire_failure: On a failure, a vampire immediately falls into torpor. The duration is determined by Humanity / Path, as with torpor from aggravated wounds.
Black Pestilence.success: On a successful Willpower check, the victim avoids the main effect, but their check pool is reduced by 2d10 until the end of the scene.

Path of Entropy.description: The Path of Entropy is a rare and dangerous path governing the energies of death and decay. It was discovered by the Nagaraja, who called it Nihilistics. This path lets a necromancer see the marks of death, destroy objects, feed on entropy, summon hungry ghosts, and unleash a scream heard on both sides of the Shroud.

Eyes of Death.description: The necromancer sees the world through the mirror of Death, and discerns entropic marks on the living and non-living.
Eyes of Death.general_effect: 1 success lets the character see others' condition: healthy, wounded, sick, dying, cursed, or under destructive magic.

fate_reading.1 success: Roughly how long the victim has left to live, accurate to within a few weeks.
fate_reading.5 successes: Where and exactly how the victim will meet death.

Eyes of Death.limits: A given fate can only be read once per activation. The Storyteller may roll secretly so the player doesn't know how accurate the vision is.

Aura of Decay.description: The necromancer intensifies the aura of entropy around themselves, causing objects to break, rust, rot, crumble, and fail.
Aura of Decay.effect: The chosen object becomes useless. The exact nature of the failure is determined by the Storyteller; using the object usually results in a failed check.

speed_table.3 Blood points: breaks by the end of the scene

Aura of Decay.limits: This power can't be used to destroy a stake already driven into a heart, because activation requires spending blood.

Devour the Spirit.description: The necromancer absorbs entropic energy from places of death, or directly from ghosts.
Devour the Spirit.ambient_entropy: Ambient entropy can be absorbed where someone died no more than an hour ago, or in places saturated with death: a morgue, a cemetery, a murder scene. Usually 1–4 points of entropy can be gained.
Devour the Spirit.area_drain: Each point of entropy absorbed raises the difficulty of all Necromancy checks and death-related powers in that place and its surroundings by 1. Each point extends the effect by a day.
Devour the Spirit.ghost_feeding: To drink entropy from a ghost, the necromancer attacks it as with the Kiss. A ghost can hold up to 10 points of entropy. With each point, its body grows more transparent and weaker.
Devour the Spirit.ghost_resistance: The ghost is treated as paralyzed, but can move and resist if it wins an opposed Willpower check against the necromancer's Willpower at difficulty 6.
Devour the Spirit.danger: While feeding from a ghost, the necromancer is vulnerable to attacks from other ghosts, even those that normally can't affect the material world.
Devour the Spirit.entropy_as_blood: Each point of entropy counts as a Blood point for activating Disciplines, healing, and boosting Physical attributes, but cannot be used to wake from day-sleep.
Devour the Spirit.botch: On a botch, the character loses the ability to absorb entropy until the end of the night, but remains vulnerable to ghosts for 1–4 turns.

Breath of Thanatos.description: The necromancer draws in entropy and exhales it as an invisible necromantic miasma.
Breath of Thanatos.hungry_ghost_cloud: If used as a cloud, the power covers a radius of about 400 meters. Each additional Blood point extends the radius by another 400 meters.
Breath of Thanatos.ghost_effect: The cloud attracts hungry ghosts. They ignore the necromancer unless provoked, but attack other beings in the area. The cloud dissipates at the end of the scene.
Breath of Thanatos.curse_target: To infect a specific target or object, the necromancer must touch it, or roll Dexterity + Occult at difficulty 7.
Breath of Thanatos.curse_effect: The victim takes 1 aggravated wound and carries a mark of death until sunrise. The difficulty of social checks with those unused to such a sensation increases by 2.
Breath of Thanatos.detection: Supernatural perception can reveal the curse on the victim or object.
Breath of Thanatos.repeat_limit: Using the power again on the same target before sunrise has no effect.
Breath of Thanatos.botch: On a botch, the entropic energy turns against the necromancer: they suffer the miasma's effect, including an aggravated wound, and may attract dangerous spirits.

Cry of the Night.cost: 1 Willpower point + 1 Blood point per target beyond the first
Cry of the Night.range: the character's Necromancy rating, in meters
Cry of the Night.description: The necromancer unleashes a scream of pure entropy, audible in both the world of the living and the Shadowlands.
Cry of the Night.activation: The character chooses one or more victims within range. The amount of blood is limited by Generation, and can't be spent in advance or spread across several turns.
Cry of the Night.choice: Before rolling, the character chooses a beneficial or harmful effect.
Cry of the Night.beneficial_effect: Each target gets –2 to the difficulty of any action. The duration is 1 turn per success the necromancer scores.
Cry of the Night.harmful_effect: Each target takes 1 aggravated wound per success the necromancer scores.
Cry of the Night.targets: Any living being can be a target, including supernatural ones.
Cry of the Night.danger: The Cry of the Night is heard on both sides of the Shroud, and can draw the attention of beings from both worlds.
Cry of the Night.botch: On a botch, the character may summon vengeful spirits, including hungry ghosts, and won't be protected from their attention.

rituals.description: Necromancy includes many rituals. Some are tied to specific paths, others are obtained from the inhabitants of the world of the dead.
rituals.learning: A beginning necromancer receives one first-level ritual. Other rituals are usually obtained through the story, rather than purchased directly with experience.
rituals.compatibility: Necromantic rituals resemble Thaumaturgical ones in how they are learned, but are not compatible with them and cannot be substituted for one another.

Assamite Sorcery.description: Assamite Sorcery is the blood sorcery of the Magi, functionally close to Thaumaturgy but philosophically incompatible with the Tremere's hermetic system. It's flexible and syncretic, drawing on a wide range of mystical traditions: from Islamic alchemy and astronomy to Indian ecstatic cults and feng shui.

system.theme: Assamite magic, blades, blood, rituals, a flexible tradition
system.thaumaturgy_similarity: Mechanically identical to Thaumaturgy in every respect, but considered a separate and incompatible Discipline.
system.foreign_paths: Non-traditional paths cost twice as much; activation costs 1 additional Blood point, and rituals take twice as long and require 1 additional success.

Steel Awakening.description: The Assamite path of the blade, smithing symbolism, and the mystical bond between a weapon and its owner. The path's level cannot exceed the character's Craft or Melee. Its powers are calibrated for swords and knives; for other blade weapons, difficulty is +1.

Confession of the Blade.description: The sorcerer reads the history and condition of a blade.
Confession of the Blade.effect: Successes determine the depth of the information. 3+ successes may lower the difficulty of the next power or ritual directed at that weapon.

Iron Grip.description: The weapon will not leave its owner's hand against their will.
Iron Grip.effect: Strengthens the bond between blade and owner, protecting against disarmament and accidental loss.

Cleave Steel.description: The blade gains the power to cut through metal, armor, and an opponent's weapon, damaging or breaking them.

Steel Shield.description: The blade becomes a mystical defense against attacks, letting the wielder parry, block, or deflect things an ordinary weapon would struggle to stop.

Strike of Truth.description: The next blow with the blade is stripped of supernatural trappings and strikes the target as a clash of pure ideals.
Strike of Truth.duration: 1 turn per success, or until a successful attack
Strike of Truth.damage: The weapon deals base damage without the character's Strength or external supernatural weapon enhancements, but with attack successes and the character's own enhancements, such as Potence and Celerity.
Strike of Truth.defense_bypass: The victim's supernatural defenses, including Fortitude, do not work against Strike of Truth; soak comes from Stamina alone. Physical armor works normally.

Blood Sorcery.description: Blood Sorcery is occult work with vitae. A sorcerer tastes blood and learns its secrets, turns their own Blood into acid or poison, thickens vitae, draws blood from a distance, and makes an enemy's blood boil in their veins. The Discipline's main strength is its rituals: slow, demanding in components, but flexible effects for protection, surveillance, travel, curses, and secret magic.

system.limitations: requires training, formulas, and mentors; rituals take time and often need rare components; the most spectacular effects easily breach the Masquerade

Taste of Blood.description: A drop of blood reveals to the sorcerer the nature of whoever it belonged to.
Taste of Blood.effect: A success determines the resonance, intensity, and type of being: human, ghoul, vampire, or other. Vitae reveals Blood Potency; a triumph pinpoints Generation, diablerie, and the kind of supernatural being, if the sorcerer is familiar with such things.

Corrosive Vitae.description: The vampire's own Blood becomes acid against non-living matter.
Corrosive Vitae.effect: One dose, over about 5 minutes, eats through roughly 35 cm of soft material around the point of contact. Soft metals take longer; hard steel and alloys are up to the Storyteller. The non-living flesh of Kindred doesn't dissolve.

Extinguish Vitae.description: The sorcerer weakens another vampire's vitae, making their Hunger flare up.
Extinguish Vitae.pool: Intelligence + Blood Sorcery vs. Stamina + Composure
Extinguish Vitae.effect: A visible vampire target gains +1 Hunger on a win, +2 on a triumph. The victim may try to identify the source of the effect if they can see the sorcerer.

Scorpion's Touch.description: The vampire's blood turns into a paralytic poison delivered by a blade, spit, or kiss.
Scorpion's Touch.effect: The victim resists with Stamina + Occult or Fortitude. If the poisoner wins, mortals take aggravated damage and lose consciousness; vampires take indivisible superficial damage. The poison doesn't work in drinks, syringes, or on ordinary bullets.

Thicken the Blood.description: The sorcerer temporarily strengthens their own Blood beyond its normal limits.
Thicken the Blood.effect: A check at difficulty 2 + current Blood Potency. A success raises Blood Potency by 1 for the scene or until dawn; a triumph raises it by 2. The effect can exceed the Generational maximum.

Theft of Vitae.description: Blood tears free from a mortal's artery and flows through the air into the vampire's mouth.
Theft of Vitae.effect: Requires a visible mortal target and a contest. While feeding this way the vampire can do nothing else, but drinks twice as fast, or three times as fast on a triumph. The wound closes on its own afterward, but the sight almost certainly breaches the Masquerade.

Cauldron of Blood.description: The target's blood boils in their veins, causing monstrous pain and tearing the body apart.
Cauldron of Blood.effect: Requires touching the target and winning a contest. Each margin of success deals an aggravated wound; a mortal with any such damage dies. A vampire victim's Hunger rises with each wound, up to a maximum of 5.

Baal's Caress.description: The vampire's own Blood becomes a lethal poison, dangerous to mortals and Kindred alike.
Baal's Caress.effect: Works like Scorpion's Touch, but deals aggravated damage to every target. A mortal with any such damage dies instantly; a vampire who additionally loses an extra contest will fall into torpor at their next sleep.

Wake with Evening's Freshness.effect: If the vampire is disturbed during the day, they may wake ready to act with no daytime penalties for the scene; a triumph extends the effect until the next dawn.

Spider's Legs.effect: Lets the vampire crawl across walls and ceilings at roughly half normal speed, as long as hands and feet remain in contact with the surface.

Guiding Stone.effect: Creates a beacon whose direction and approximate distance the sorcerer can sense for a week, as long as the stone remains intact.

Truth of Blood.effect: Lets the sorcerer use a target's blood to tell a conscious lie from the truth; a triumph draws out additional forgotten or suppressed details.

Bond with the Sire.effect: Opens a brief telepathic conversation between a childe and their sire across an enormous distance.

Dagon's Call.effect: After prior contact with the sorcerer's Blood, the victim takes damage at a distance: their vessels burst and their lungs fill with blood.

Flame Eater.effect: Until the end of the night, fire deals half damage to the protected character; the component used is a finger bone from the caster.

Incorporeal Passage.effect: The sorcerer becomes visible and audible but immaterial, and can pass straight through walls as long as they hold a shard of mirror.

Shelter of the Haven.effect: Wraps the sleeping place in shadow, blocking sunlight within the protected perimeter until sunset.

The Gate.effect: Creates a pair of circles: an entrance and an exit. By entering the first circle and passing a check, the sorcerer instantly travels to the exit circle along with a small load or a single creature.

Heart of Stone.effect: The heart turns to stone and becomes immune to staking, but the vampire gains a serious emotional coldness and penalties to empathetic social actions.

rituals.description: Rituals are individual formulas of Blood Sorcery. They usually require a Blood trial, components, a casting time of 5 minutes × level, and an Intelligence + Blood Sorcery check at a difficulty equal to the ritual's level + 1. Rituals are slower than ordinary powers, but solve problems the powers don't address directly: protective wards, long-distance communication, fire resistance, intangibility, sunlight protection for a haven, and magical gates.

Thanatosis.description: Thanatosis is the Samedi's Discipline, power over decay, corpse-flesh, disease, and bodily breakdown. It doesn't just kill: it makes the victim's body dead before the victim even manages to die.

system.theme: decay, corpse-flesh, rot, death, infection

Witch's Wrinkles.description: The Samedi changes the degree of their own corpse-likeness, desiccation, and visible decay, heightening horror or masking certain features.

Rot.description: The Samedi's touch causes rapid rot in living or non-living flesh and organic matter.

Ashes to Ashes.description: The Samedi crumbles, or turns into corpse dust / a decaying substance, to escape threats or pass through narrow spaces.

Mortification.description: The Samedi deadens parts of the victim's body, reducing mobility, physical traits, and the ability to act.

Necrosis.description: This power causes the victim's body to die off en masse. When physical traits drop to zero, the victim nearly loses the ability to move, and can only crawl, roll, use Disciplines, and spend blood.

Insidious Plague.description: Lets the character delay the onset of Rot, Mortification, or Necrosis by hours, days, or weeks, turning the power into a hidden infection or an insurance policy.

Dust to Dust.description: A senior form of corpse decay, letting the Samedi or the victim turn to dust, fall apart, and return with consequences determined by the Storyteller.

Rotten Cabal.description: A senior power of the Samedi, spreading necrotic decay across a group of targets, or binding several bodies into a single rotting ritual effect.

DESCENDANT_OF_KARL_SCHREKT.name: Descendant of Karl Schrekt
DESCENDANT_OF_KARL_SCHREKT.description:
Karl Schrekt is a legendary Tremere, a former Camarilla Justicar, and one of the most dangerous hunters of supernatural threats. Before his Embrace, he destroyed vampires; afterward, he became their protector, destroying the clan's enemies and crushing any threat to the Masquerade.

After the fall of the Tremere Pyramid, his figure became even more important: he's a symbol of discipline, strength, and the old order. His descendants generally hold to strict principles: follow the Traditions, protect the clan, and guard the secrets of Blood Sorcery.

⚠️ Restriction: available only to members of clan Tremere.

options.full_description: You possess knowledge of the clan's past and its structure before the Pyramid's fall.
options.mechanic: Once per game session: ask the Storyteller a question about clan Tremere or House Tremere.

options.full_description: You fanatically follow Schrekt's teaching and don't bend to outside influence.
options.bonus: +2d10 to checks resisting manipulation or attempts to change your beliefs
options.mechanic: +2d10 to checks resisting manipulation or attempts to change your beliefs.

options.full_description: You're always prepared in advance to perform rituals.
options.mechanic: Once per story: perform a ritual you know in ~5 minutes with no preparation.

options.full_description: You have an ally among other supernatural beings.
options.mechanic: Ally, level ●●●. Once per story, they can be called upon (they'll arrive in 1–10 hours).

options.full_description: You study not only vampires, but other supernatural entities as well.
options.mechanic: Library ●●● + 3 Occult specialties. Once per story: ask a question about the supernatural.

FOLKLORIC.name: Folkloric Merits
FOLKLORIC.description: Some vampires retain traits that bring them closer to the living, or that loosen the constraints of unlife.

options.full_description: You can eat ordinary human food, and even enjoy it.
options.mechanic: Food doesn't satisfy Hunger. You must get rid of it before your day's sleep.

DESCENDANT_OF_HARDESTADT.name: Descendant of Hardestadt
DESCENDANT_OF_HARDESTADT.description: You're descended from Hardestadt, one of the founders of the Camarilla and a key figure in the history of clan Ventrue. His influence is still felt in Kindred politics today. Your lineage grants you authority, but also draws the attention of both allies and enemies.

options.full_description: Your voice carries the authority inherited from Hardestadt.
options.mechanic:
You can draw the attention of any audience regardless of conditions.
You cannot be ignored while you're speaking.
However, this doesn't guarantee your listeners' agreement.

options.full_description: You've inherited your ancestor's confidence and charisma.
options.mechanic: Once per story, you take no penalties when trying to convince others to carry out a dangerous or risky action.

options.full_description: Your lineage gives you weight among the Ventrue.
options.mechanic:
Your Status among the Ventrue can never be lower than 3.
This applies regardless of your status in other factions.

options.full_description: You have a way to contact Hardestadt's ancient allies.
options.mechanic:
Once per chronicle, you may try to contact one of the Camarilla's founders.
The response and any help depend on the importance of the situation (Storyteller's discretion).

options.full_description: You possess a document confirming that you've been named Hardestadt's heir.
options.mechanic:
You can lay claim to Hardestadt's legacy.
This can grant you enormous influence within the Camarilla.

Consequences:
— The Camarilla will be forced to react
— The Anarchs will view you as a threat
— You become a target of political struggle

LOWER_CLANS.name: Lower Clans
LOWER_CLANS.description: You belong to one of the clans historically considered Lower. In your domain, this division still matters, and you face prejudice from the elite. But it's exactly this environment that breeds counterculture, solidarity, and rebellion. You're used to living at the bottom of the social ladder — and have learned to turn it to your advantage.

options.full_description: You're used to humiliation and no longer react to it.
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
— you may completely ignore an insult or social provocation
— with no checks and no consequences (Stress, loss of Composure, etc.)

options.full_description: You've learned to use your clan bane as an advantage.
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
— gain 1 automatic success
— if your clan bane directly helps with the action

(for example: your ugliness causes fear, your madness produces an unconventional solution, etc.)

options.full_description: You maintain ties between the Lower Clans and know how to bring them together.
options.mechanic:
Split 3 dots between:
— Mawla
— Status (among the Lower Clans)

You can act as a go-between and unite members of the Lower Clans

options.full_description: The Lower Clans share knowledge and Disciplines among themselves.
options.mechanic:
Choose one clan Discipline belonging to another Lower Clan.

It counts as a clan Discipline for you:
— you purchase it at the normal (reduced) experience cost

options.full_description: You're capable of sparking a large-scale uprising against the Higher Clans.
options.mechanic:
+1d10 to all checks aimed at:
— undermining the Higher Clans' power
— organizing a coup

Once per chronicle:
— you may sacrifice your own background dots (up to 10 dots)
— to strip an equal amount of influence/resources from the Higher Clans

(the specific consequences are determined by the Storyteller)

FACTION_WAR_VETERAN.name: Faction War Veteran
FACTION_WAR_VETERAN.description: You lived through the war between the Camarilla and the Sabbat — a conflict that left a deep mark on the world of Kindred. You know how both sides operated, you've seen domains laid to ruin, and you remember which mistakes cost vampires their final death. This experience gives you knowledge, connections, and a reputation, but it can also draw enemies.

options.full_description: You lost a domain or loved ones during the war, and know its consequences firsthand.
options.mechanic: Once per story, you can learn important information from the Storyteller about the faction war in your domain.

options.full_description: You took part in the conflict and left your mark on the war.
options.mechanic:
Split 3 dots between:
— Status
— Mawla

This reflects your reputation and connections after the war.

options.full_description: You destroyed a significant enemy during the war.
options.mechanic:
Once per story, you can use your reputation to make an opponent back down from a confrontation.

The Storyteller may use this against you by drawing the attention of enemies.

options.full_description: You've studied the war's battleground and know how to use it to your advantage.
options.mechanic:
+2 to your domain's Chasse rating.

+2d10 to checks:
— Streetwise
— Larceny
— Stealth

(within your domain and the two neighboring domains)

options.full_description: You know how to stoke conflict and direct hatred between factions.
options.mechanic: +2d10 to social checks aimed at provoking conflict between factions.

FEEDING.name: Feeding
FEEDING.description: Some vampires have special abilities related to sensing or processing blood.

options.full_description: You can sense a resonance by smell, without tasting the blood.
options.mechanic: Check: Resolve + Awareness (difficulty 3). Difficulty may increase or decrease depending on conditions.

options.full_description: You can feed on cold, spoiled blood, and even plasma.
options.mechanic: Such blood satisfies Hunger but doesn't grant a resonance. Ventrue cannot take this advantage.

MAWLA.name: Mawla
MAWLA.description: A Mawla is an influential Kindred (or group) who helps you with advice, information, and occasionally intervenes in your affairs. This is usually a mentor, a patron, or a senior ally. In return, they expect favors and loyalty from you.

options.full_description: You have a patron among the young vampires.
options.mechanic: Can give advice and limited help.

options.full_description: You have an experienced and influential mentor.
options.mechanic: Can provide information, connections, and medium-level help.

options.full_description: Your patron is a very powerful vampire.
options.mechanic: Can influence politics, protect you, and provide serious resources.

options.full_description: You're connected to the city's senior leadership.
options.mechanic: Can speak on your behalf and protect you at a high level.

options.full_description: Your patron is one of the city's rulers.
options.mechanic: You're under the protection of the highest authority, but heavily dependent on their will.

CONVENTION_OF_THORNS.name: Convention of Thorns
CONVENTION_OF_THORNS.description: You've deeply studied the events of the Convention of Thorns — the historic gathering at which the Camarilla was created and the Traditions were formulated. You know the names of those who attended, the hidden agreements, and the secret proposals that were rejected or forgotten. This knowledge lets you pressure Kindred and manipulate politics by drawing on ancient obligations and secrets.

options.full_description: You've thoroughly studied the events and agreements of the Convention of Thorns.
options.mechanic: Once per story, you can get useful information from the Storyteller about the public aspects of the Convention, its attendees, or its agreements.

options.full_description: You know not only the official Traditions, but also the alternative versions debated at the Convention.
options.mechanic: Once per chronicle, you can cite a Tradition that wasn't adopted in order to influence a situation, if the ruling clans are willing to consider it.

options.full_description: You know hidden facts and secret agreements between the clans.
options.mechanic:
+1d10 to social checks when using this knowledge.

Once per story, you can learn the name of a powerful Kindred willing to do you a significant favor in exchange for your information.

options.full_description: Your standing and knowledge make you a candidate for high office in the Camarilla.
options.mechanic:
Kindred tend to support your candidacy (or your sire's).
You gain significant political influence.

options.full_description: You have enough knowledge and support to propose changes to the Camarilla's fundamental laws.
options.mechanic:
You can propose a new Tradition or amend an existing one.
If the proposal is sound and supported, it may be adopted.
Otherwise, you risk being declared a dangerous freethinker.

Either way, you'll be heard.

CAINITE_HERESY.name: Cainite Heresy
CAINITE_HERESY.description: The Cainite Heresy is a gnostic doctrine that arose at the dawn of Christianity through interaction between Kindred and mortal mystics. Heretics believe Caine was the true messiah, Christ was a Second Caine, and Cainites themselves are angels opposing a false demiurge God. Though the Inquisition nearly wiped out this movement, it has been reborn among the Anarchs and the prophets of Gehenna. To some it's a tool of manipulation; to others, genuine faith.

options.full_description: You're connected to the heretics and receive hidden knowledge from them.
options.mechanic: Once per story, the Storyteller gives you a direct hint about the plans or actions of Cainite heretics (past or present).

options.full_description: You hold a particular place in the city's heretical community.
options.mechanic:
Split 3 dots between:
— Allies
— Herd
— Mawla
— Retainers

You also gain the flaw "Dark Secret (Cainite Heresy)," which may manifest depending on the domain.

options.full_description: You can sense True Faith and are able to recognize a religious threat.
options.mechanic:
You can detect True Faith in mortals.
When needed, you use a pool equal to the highest level of this merit.
Alternatively, you can use this effect to find reliable allies.

options.full_description: You know how to conduct rituals that induce the ecstatic state of "Triumph of the Blood" in mortals.
options.mechanic:
Once per story, you may perform a ritual using special substances.
Participants fall into religious fury, revealing their worst sides.
The ritual requires a check and may trigger frenzy (Rage) in Kindred.

options.full_description: You play a key role in the heretics' prophecies. You may be considered an incarnation of Caine, or the bearer of an important artifact.
options.mechanic:
Once per story, you may automatically win a social conflict, if you can justify it through your standing.
You're known to many heretics and their affiliated groups.

FIORENZA_SAVONA.name: Fiorenza Savona
FIORENZA_SAVONA.description:
Fiorenza Savona is an ambitious and extremely influential member of clan Ventrue, who has concentrated her power not so much within Kindred society as among mortal elites. In response to the Lasombra's growing influence within religious structures, the Ventrue bet on a "new nobility" — corporations, international organizations, and political elites.

Fiorenza is one of the key players in this effort. She works with the UN, NGOs, economic institutions, and global elites, shaping the decisions that form the world. Her approach isn't to dominate vampires directly, but to control the people through whom everything else is controlled.

Ruthless, pragmatic, and extremely effective, she's rising quickly through the hierarchy. Many underestimate her as "just another rich Ventrue," but those who understand the real state of affairs know she already controls more than it seems.

options.full_description: Fiorenza is watching you, and considers you either an asset or a potential problem.
options.mechanic:
You gain a retainer:
→ a gifted mortal

Details:
— they work for Fiorenza, not for you
— they constantly observe and report

⚠️ If they die or are compromised:
→ Fiorenza loses trust in you

👍 If the reports are good:
→ you gain her favor (story bonuses)

options.full_description: You have the opportunity for a personal meeting with one of the busiest and most influential vampires around.
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
→ a personal meeting with Fiorenza

Effect:
— you may ask questions
— receive information, advice, or help

⚙️ Essentially:
→ a strong story resource at the level of "insider information"

options.full_description: You've known each other a long time, and your relationship brings real advantages.
options.mechanic:
Fiorenza = Mawla ●●●

She can:
— help in negotiations with the Ventrue
— give financial advice
— provide resources (such as private transportation)

⚠️ If abused:
→ she cuts ties with you

options.full_description: You've been drawn into a conspiracy within clan Ventrue against Fiorenza.
options.mechanic:
If you agree:
— you're bound by a blood bond
— your memory of the conspirators' identities is erased

In return:
→ you gain 6 dots to split between:
   • Contacts
   • Mawla
   • Resources

⚠️ Alternative:
→ become a double agent (very dangerous, but very powerful)

options.full_description: Fiorenza owes you, and can call on her global connections.
options.mechanic:
Once per chronicle:
→ Fiorenza influences a political leader

You gain a pool of:
→ 5d10

You may split these dice across any checks related to:
— politics
— government
— state decisions

💡 The Storyteller may increase the pool if:
— the action is large in scale
— the consequences are significant
— the plan is well thought out

HAVEN.name: Haven
HAVEN.description: A haven is your main place of safety. Each level increases its protection, secrecy, and control. Each dot increases the difficulty to detect or break in by 1, or grants +1d10 to defense.

options.full_description: A small but protected shelter.
options.mechanic: Harder to find and break into. +1d10 to checks to detect danger inside.

options.full_description: A well-protected, spacious haven.
options.mechanic: Increased protection and security. +2 to break-in difficulty, or +2d10 to defense.

options.full_description: A large and very well-protected property.
options.mechanic: A high level of security and control. +3 to break-in difficulty, or +3d10.

CONTACTS.name: Contacts
CONTACTS.description: You have connections among mortals who supply you with information. They operate in their own sphere and are best suited to gathering information rather than direct intervention.

options.full_description: You have one contact who can dig up simple information and do small favors.
options.mechanic: They can provide basic information or get something cheap and widely available (Resources level 1).

options.full_description: You have one contact with good capabilities and useful connections.
options.mechanic: They can provide reliable information or do an ordinary favor (Resources level 2).

options.full_description: You have a valuable contact in an important position.
options.mechanic: They can provide important information or do a significant favor (Resources level 4).

FAME.name: Fame
FAME.description: You're known among mortals or Kindred. This gives you social advantages, makes hunting easier, and opens doors, but makes you conspicuous and vulnerable.

options.full_description: You're known within a narrow circle.
options.mechanic: −1 to the difficulty of social checks with fans. You can sometimes use Fame in place of other traits.

options.full_description: Most people in the city recognize you.
options.mechanic: −2 to the difficulty of social checks with fans. Easier to draw victims' attention.

options.full_description: A large part of the country knows about you.
options.mechanic: −3 to the difficulty of social checks with fans. A strong bonus in social situations.

options.full_description: You're widely known in your field or in society.
options.mechanic: −4 to the difficulty of social checks with fans.

options.full_description: You're known around the world.
options.mechanic: −5 to the difficulty of social checks with fans. You're recognized almost everywhere.

VOERMAN_TWINS.name: Jeanette / Therese Voerman
VOERMAN_TWINS.description: You're connected to Jeanette and Therese Voerman — an influential figure among the Anarchs and owner of "The Asylum" club chain. Behind their success lies a dual nature: two personalities in one body. Working with them grants access to resources, influence, and opportunities, but it always comes with unpredictability and risk.

options.full_description: You have privileged access to The Asylum club.
options.mechanic:
You can hunt at the club 1–2 times per session at difficulty 2 with no penalties.
You get access to the best spots and never wait in line.

options.full_description: Jeanette and Therese trust you and give you assignments.
options.mechanic:
You receive assignments from the Voermans.
They're dangerous, but successful completion brings a reward.
The Voermans honor their obligations to you.

options.full_description: Jeanette has taken a liking to you and acts as your patron.
options.mechanic:
You can use the club as a haven.
You can host events there.
You can ask the Anarchs for favors.

Jeanette counts as your Mawla (3 dots), but only helps with Anarch and Malkavian matters.

options.full_description: Therese respects you and backs you in business matters.
options.mechanic:
Therese acts as your Mawla (4 dots).
She helps with business, finances, and gives advice.
She can vouch for you in domains that have an Asylum club.

options.full_description: You've earned the right to open your own Asylum club.
options.mechanic:
While the club is operating, split 4 dots between:
— Haven
— Herd
— Resources
— the domain's Chasse

Dots can be reallocated.

You get to decide whether the club counts as Elysium.

CARNA.name: Carna (House Carna)
CARNA.description: Carna is the leader of the breakaway House Carna, a group of Tremere who rejected the Pyramid, blood bonds, and the clan's traditional hierarchy. She advocates for freedom, a reimagining of magic, and the destruction of old power structures. Her followers seek new ways to practice ritual, often at the risk of their sanity and in defiance of taboo. House Carna grows through Anarchs and Camarilla exiles, while Carna herself prepares for an inevitable conflict with Tremere's legacy.

options.full_description: You share Carna's views and draw strength from the presence of her followers.
options.mechanic: When other members of House Carna are nearby, you gain +1d10 to all Willpower checks.

options.full_description: You followed Carna at the moment of her break from the Pyramid, and swore never to submit to a blood bond.
options.mechanic:
Each time you risk forming a blood bond, you may make a Willpower check (difficulty equal to the Blood Potency of the blood drunk).
On a success, you prevent the bond from forming.

options.full_description: You've mastered alternative methods of Carna's magic that let you bypass classical restrictions.
options.mechanic:
Once per story, you may perform a ritual you know without spending vitae.
If this triggers a Blood Triumph, you fall into psychosis until the end of the story (such as paranoia or a fear of blood).

options.full_description: You've learned Carna's methods for creating alternative bonds that replace the traditional blood bond.
options.mechanic:
After sexual contact, you create a link between yourself, your partner, and Carna.
The link can be strengthened by repetition.
This temporarily bypasses the Tremere clan bane.
The bond lasts until the end of the story.

options.full_description: You possess the legendary text that let Carna free herself from blood bonds.
options.mechanic:
You gain 1 automatic success on all Occult checks related to:
— Gehenna
— preventing it
— breaking blood bonds

You cannot be bound by a blood bond as long as you have the book and follow its teaching.

Drawbacks:
— Clan Tremere actively hunts you
— −1d10 to social or mental checks (Storyteller's discretion) due to paranoia

THEO_BELL.name: Theo Bell
THEO_BELL.description: Theo Bell is a former Camarilla agent who became a symbol of the Anarchs after the Conclave of Prague. For years he served Ventrue elders Hardestadt and Jan Pieterzoon, carrying out their will, but ultimately rebelled against them. It was he who fired the first shot that led to their deaths. Despite his reputation as a traitor, Theo remains an important figure, capable of acting as a go-between for the Camarilla and the Anarchs. He doesn't seek power, but he won't tolerate injustice, and knows how to keep the balance between sides.

options.full_description: You lead a group of mortal rebels, inspired by you or dependent on your blood. They follow you and are willing to take risks.
options.mechanic:
Equivalent to Allies (3 dots).
The group can independently carry out one dangerous task, once per story.

options.full_description: You were part of the Anarch movement long before the mass influx of new followers. You know their weaknesses and history.
options.mechanic: You gain 2 automatic successes on Investigation checks, if they involve recently joined Anarchs.

options.full_description: You have a way to reach Theo Bell — through an intermediary, a dead drop, or a mystical link.
options.mechanic: You may try to reach out to Theo for help. The response and extent of help are determined by the Storyteller based on your relationship.

options.full_description: You've earned Theo Bell's trust and are part of his inner circle of allies.
options.mechanic:
Theo Bell counts as your Mawla (5 dots).
However, this comes with obligations and additional risks.

options.full_description: You've built your own group of Brujah, who may lean toward the Camarilla, the Anarchs, or remain neutral. They're independent individuals, not mindless followers.
options.mechanic:
You gain 5 dots to split between:
— Contacts
— Haven (safe houses)
— Mawla
— Retainers

The group remembers your actions and may rise up against you if mistreated.

CARMELITA_NEILLSON.name: Carmelita Neillson
CARMELITA_NEILLSON.description:
Carmelita Neillson is a Toreador archaeologist of Brazilian-Irish descent, increasingly sought out by elders trying to make sense of Gehenna's omens. She studies ancient ruins, holds conversations with thousand-year-old Kindred, and gathers the living history of the vampire world.

Because the Camarilla forbids storing this kind of knowledge digitally, she builds hidden libraries around the world. Carmelita believes the greatest art isn't a painting or music, but the ability to tell a story — especially the story of one's own lineage.

She's friendly, curious, and possesses an outstanding talent for languages and storytelling. Among the Toreador she's respected for her depth, not her looks.

options.full_description: You share Carmelita's philosophy: story is the highest form of art.
options.mechanic:
When dealing with Toreador:
— they tend to listen to you
— they respond positively to stories, myths, and historical tales

⚙️ Mechanically:
→ the Storyteller may lower the difficulty of social checks or grant a situational bonus
(usually ~ -1 difficulty or +1d10)

options.full_description: You possess a relic or work of art given to you by Carmelita, which inspires you.
options.mechanic:
Before sleep:
— spend 1 hour studying the object
— make a check: Resolve + Academics (difficulty 5)

On a success:
→ +1 Willpower the following night

⏱ Limit: once per game session

options.full_description: You curate or guard one of Carmelita's secret libraries.
options.mechanic:
You gain:
— Haven ●●
— Library ●●

Effect:
→ access to historical knowledge
→ the ability to do research

⚠️ Drawback:
— other vampires regularly come to you (trouble, politics, attention)

options.full_description: You possess a recorded interview with an ancient vampire, containing dangerous knowledge.
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
→ ask the Storyteller a question about a clan or domain secret
→ receive truthful information

In addition:
— this is blackmail material
— may create plot complications
— the recording could potentially continue

options.full_description: You guard the resting place of an ancient vampire (possibly your own ancestor).
options.mechanic:
As long as the tomb is safe:
→ Carmelita owes you one major debt

If the tomb is violated:
→ serious consequences (Storyteller's discretion)
→ loss of trust / danger

AMBRUS_MAROPIS.name: Ambrus Maropis
AMBRUS_MAROPIS.description:
Ambrus Maropis is a mysterious and influential Nosferatu who almost never leaves his luxurious apartments in Cyprus. Despite his seclusion, he's widely known as a tastemaker among Kindred and a zealous defender of the Masquerade. Many Toreador hate him for his popularity and influence, especially knowing his true nature.

He operates through students and admirers, controlling the flow of information and trends. Ambrus is an expert in technology, digital security, and modern culture. His online persona is a carefully built instrument of influence: he sells information, consults on security, and helps vampires appear "alive" in mortal eyes.

options.full_description: You've deeply absorbed the principles of the Masquerade and follow them almost fanatically.
options.mechanic:
+1d10 to checks related to:
— finding cover
— avoiding detection

⚠️ The bonus doesn't apply if your views conflict with the domain's policy

options.full_description: You have access to illegal digital channels and Ambrus's network of fans.
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
— you can request a hack
— and obtain hidden information about any mortal (if it exists online)

⏱ Turnaround time: 2–20 hours

options.full_description: You're part of Ambrus's inner circle of students, with access to his knowledge and contacts.
options.mechanic:
You gain:
— access to information (including on the Second Inquisition, trends, security)
— help publishing on the dark web
— contacts (hackers, intermediaries, etc.)

Effect:
→ Mawla (●●●)

options.full_description: You know how to tap into global surveillance systems (analogous to PRISM/NUCLEON).
options.mechanic:
Once per story:
— when working with the internet or mobile networks
— gain 2 automatic successes on an Investigation check

⚠️ Use is limited for security reasons

options.full_description: Your digital and real-world identity is carefully hidden and protected.
options.mechanic:
You gain:
— 2 Masks, level ●● each
— the "Erased Identity" advantage

Bonus:
+3d10 to checks related to:
— covering your tracks online
— countering investigations
— digital anonymity

GOLCONDA.name: Golconda
GOLCONDA.description: Golconda is a legendary state of enlightenment in which a vampire achieves balance between Humanity and the Beast. Most consider it a myth, but some claim it's real. The path to Golconda is personal, and requires overcoming one's own weaknesses, sins, and the very nature of vampirism. Those who follow this path may gain control over the Beast, reduce their Hunger, and draw closer to liberation from the curse.
